````python
    async def _run_refactor(self):
        """Run refactoring analysis."""
        self.console.print("[blue]Analyzing refactoring opportunities...[/blue]")
        try:
            data = {
                "project_root": str(self.project_root),
                "model": self.model,
                "provider": self.provider
            }
            result = make_request("POST", "/refactor", data)
            
            if result.get("success"):
                self.console.print("[green]✓ Refactor analysis completed[/green]")
                
                suggestions = result.get("refactoring_suggestions", [])
                if suggestions:
                    self.console.print(Panel(
                        _format_refactor_suggestions(suggestions),
                        title="🔧 Refactoring Suggestions",
                        border_style="green"
                    ))
            else:
                self.console.print(f"[red]Refactor analysis failed:[/red] {result}")
        except Exception as e:
            self.console.print(f"[red]Refactor error:[/red] {e}")
    
    async def _run_show_hierarchy(self, spec_id: str):
        """Show specification hierarchy."""
        try:
            result = make_request("GET", f"/specification/{spec_id}/hierarchy")
            
            spec_name = result.get("name", "Unknown")
            self.console.print(f"[blue]Specification Hierarchy:[/blue] {spec_name}")
            
            spec_type = result.get("type", "monolithic")
            if spec_type == "composite":
                self.console.print(f"[cyan]Type:[/cyan] Composite (has components)")
                
                components = result.get("components", [])
                if components:
                    hierarchy_table = Table(title="🧩 Components")
                    hierarchy_table.add_column("Component ID", style="cyan")
                    hierarchy_table.add_column("Name", style="green")
                    hierarchy_table.add_column("Ready for Contracts", style="yellow")
                    hierarchy_table.add_column("Last Sync", style="dim")
                    
                    for comp in components:
                        ready = "✓" if comp.get("ready_for_contracts", False) else "✗"
                        last_sync = comp.get("last_sync", "Never")
                        if last_sync != "Never":
                            last_sync = last_sync.split("T")[0]  # Just the date
                        
                        hierarchy_table.add_row(
                            comp.get("id", "Unknown"),
                            comp.get("name", "Unknown"),
                            ready,
                            last_sync
                        )
                    
                    self.console.print(hierarchy_table)
                
                dependencies = result.get("dependencies", {})
                if dependencies:
                    self.console.print(Panel(
                        "\n".join(f"• {comp} depends on: {', '.join(deps)}" for comp, deps in dependencies.items()),
                        title="🔗 Dependencies",
                        border_style="blue"
                    ))
            else:
                self.console.print(f"[cyan]Type:[/cyan] Monolithic (no components)")
                self.console.print("[dim]Use 'analyze-decomposition' to explore decomposition opportunities[/dim]")
            
        except Exception as e:
            self.console.print(f"[red]Error showing hierarchy:[/red] {e}")
    
    async def _run_analyze_decomposition(self, spec_id: str):
        """Analyze decomposition opportunities."""
        self.console.print(f"[blue]Analyzing decomposition opportunities for:[/blue] {spec_id}")
        
        params = {
            "specification_id": spec_id,
            "model": self.model,
            "provider": self.provider
        }
        
        result = await stream_sse_request("/stream/analyze-decomposition", params)
        
        if result.get("success"):
            self.console.print(f"[green]✓ Analysis completed[/green]")
            
            opportunities = result.get("decomposition_opportunities", [])
            if opportunities:
                self.console.print(Panel(
                    "\n".join(f"• {opp}" for opp in opportunities),
                    title="🔍 Decomposition Opportunities",
                    border_style="green"
                ))
            
            suggested_components = result.get("suggested_components", [])
            if suggested_components:
                self.console.print(Panel(
                    "\n".join(f"• {comp}" for comp in suggested_components),
                    title="🧩 Suggested Components",
                    border_style="blue"
                ))
                
                self.console.print(f"[cyan]Use 'decompose {', '.join(suggested_components[:3])}' to proceed[/cyan]")
        else:
            self.console.print("[red]Analysis failed[/red]")
    
    async def _run_decompose_spec(self, spec_id: str, component_names: str):
        """Decompose specification into components."""
        self.console.print(f"[blue]Decomposing specification:[/blue] {spec_id}")
        self.console.print(f"[cyan]Components:[/cyan] {component_names}")
        
        params = {
            "specification_id": spec_id,
            "component_names": component_names,
            "model": self.model,
            "provider": self.provider
        }
        
        result = await stream_sse_request("/stream/decompose-specification", params)
        
        if result.get("success"):
            self.console.print(f"[green]✓ Specification decomposed successfully[/green]")
            
            components = result.get("created_components", [])
            if components:
                self.console.print(Panel(
                    "\n".join(f"• {comp['name']} ({comp['id']})" for comp in components),
                    title="🧩 Created Components",
                    border_style="green"
                ))
            
            self.console.print("[cyan]Use 'show-hierarchy' to see the new structure[/cyan]")
        else:
            self.console.print("[red]Decomposition failed[/red]")
    
    async def _run_create_component(self, parent_spec_id: str, component_name: str, description: str):
        """Create a new component."""
        self.console.print(f"[blue]Creating component:[/blue] {component_name}")
        self.console.print(f"[cyan]Description:[/cyan] {description}")
        
        params = {
            "parent_spec_id": parent_spec_id,
            "component_name": component_name,
            "description": description,
            "model": self.model,
            "provider": self.provider
        }
        
        result = await stream_sse_request("/stream/create-component", params)
        
        if result.get("success"):
            component_id = result.get("component_id")
            self.console.print(f"[green]✓ Component created:[/green] {component_id}")
            self.console.print(f"[cyan]Use 'refine-component {component_id} \"your input\"' to refine it[/cyan]")
        else:
            self.console.print("[red]Component creation failed[/red]")
    
    async def _run_refine_component(self, parent_spec_id: str, component_id: str, response: str):
        """Refine a component with parent synchronization."""
        self.console.print(f"[blue]Refining component:[/blue] {component_id}")
        self.console.print(f"[cyan]Input:[/cyan] {response}")
        
        params = {
            "parent_spec_id": parent_spec_id,
            "component_id": component_id,
            "user_response": response,
            "model": self.model,
            "provider": self.provider
        }
        
        result = await stream_sse_request("/stream/refine-component", params)
        
        if result.get("success"):
            self.console.print(f"[green]✓ Component refined with parent sync[/green]")
            
            sync_summary = result.get("sync_summary", {})
            if sync_summary:
                self.console.print(Panel(
                    f"Component: {sync_summary.get('component_changes', 'No changes')}\n"
                    f"Parent: {sync_summary.get('parent_changes', 'No changes')}\n"
                    f"Conflicts: {sync_summary.get('conflicts', 'None')}",
                    title="🔄 Sync Summary",
                    border_style="cyan"
                ))
            
            ready_for_contracts = result.get("ready_for_contracts", False)
            if ready_for_contracts:
                self.console.print("[bright_green]🎉 Component is ready for contract generation![/bright_green]")
        else:
            self.console.print("[red]Component refinement failed[/red]")
    
    async def _run_sync_status(self, spec_id: str):
        """Check synchronization status."""
        try:
            result = make_request("GET", f"/specification/{spec_id}/sync-status")
            
            self.console.print(f"[blue]Sync Status for:[/blue] {result.get('name', 'Unknown')}")
            
            overall_status = result.get("overall_status", "unknown")
            status_color = {
                "synced": "green",
                "pending": "yellow", 
                "conflicts": "red",
                "unknown": "dim"
            }.get(overall_status, "white")
            
            self.console.print(f"[{status_color}]Overall Status:[/{status_color}] {overall_status}")
            
            components = result.get("components", [])
            if components:
                sync_table = Table(title="🔄 Component Sync Status")
                sync_table.add_column("Component", style="cyan")
                sync_table.add_column("Status", style="white")
                sync_table.add_column("Last Sync", style="dim")
                sync_table.add_column("Pending", style="yellow")
                
                for comp in components:
                    status = comp.get("sync_status", "unknown")
                    last_sync = comp.get("last_sync", "Never")
                    if last_sync != "Never":
                        last_sync = last_sync.split("T")[0]
                    
                    pending = str(comp.get("pending_changes", 0))
                    
                    sync_table.add_row(
                        comp.get("name", "Unknown"),
                        status,
                        last_sync,
                        pending
                    )
                
                self.console.print(sync_table)
            
            conflicts = result.get("conflicts", [])
            if conflicts:
                self.console.print(Panel(
                    "\n".join(f"• {conflict}" for conflict in conflicts),
                    title="⚠️ Conflicts",
                    border_style="red"
                ))
            
        except Exception as e:
            self.console.print(f"[red]Error checking sync status:[/red] {e}")
````

````python
    def _get_available_spec_ids(self) -> List[str]:
        """Get list of available specification IDs for completion."""
        try:
            # Directly scan the filesystem for spec IDs
            specs_dir = Path(".axiomander/specs")
            if not specs_dir.exists():
                return []
            
            spec_ids = []
            for spec_path in specs_dir.iterdir():
                if spec_path.is_dir() and spec_path.name.startswith("spec_"):
                    # Verify it's a valid spec by checking for metadata
                    metadata_file = spec_path / "metadata.json"
                    if metadata_file.exists():
                        spec_ids.append(spec_path.name)
            
            return sorted(spec_ids)
        except Exception as e:
            # Debug: print error to help troubleshoot
            # print(f"Debug: Error getting spec IDs: {e}")
            return []
=======
    def _get_available_spec_ids(self) -> List[str]:
        """Get list of available specification IDs for completion."""
        try:
            # Directly scan the filesystem for spec IDs
            specs_dir = Path(".axiomander/specs")
            if not specs_dir.exists():
                return []
            
            spec_ids = []
            for spec_path in specs_dir.iterdir():
                if spec_path.is_dir() and spec_path.name.startswith("spec_"):
                    # Verify it's a valid spec by checking for metadata
                    metadata_file = spec_path / "metadata.json"
                    if metadata_file.exists():
                        spec_ids.append(spec_path.name)
            
            return sorted(spec_ids)
        except Exception as e:
            # Debug: print error to help troubleshoot
            # print(f"Debug: Error getting spec IDs: {e}")
            return []
    
    def _get_available_component_ids(self, parent_spec_id: str) -> List[str]:
        """Get list of available component IDs for a parent specification."""
        try:
            components_dir = Path(f".axiomander/specs/{parent_spec_id}/components")
            if not components_dir.exists():
                return []
            
            component_ids = []
            for comp_path in components_dir.iterdir():
                if comp_path.is_dir():
                    # Verify it's a valid component by checking for metadata
                    metadata_file = comp_path / "metadata.json"
                    if metadata_file.exists():
                        component_ids.append(comp_path.name)
            
            return sorted(component_ids)
        except Exception as e:
            return []
````

README.md
````markdown
<<<<<<< SEARCH
# Axiomander - The Helpful Contractual Salamander

A design-by-contract system for Python that provides decorators for expressing preconditions, postconditions, and invariants, along with introspection and analysis capabilities.

## Features

- **Contract Decorators**: `@precondition`, `@postcondition`, `@invariant`
- **Runtime Validation**: Contracts are evaluated at runtime with clear error messages
- **Introspection API**: Analyze and export contract information from your codebase
- **Project Scanning**: Discover and map all contracts across your project
- **CLI Tools**: Command-line interface for contract analysis and visualization
- **Type Safety**: Use exceptions instead of `pass` for unimplemented code

## Installation

```bash
pip install axiomander
```

## Quick Start

### Basic Contract Usage

```python
from axiomander import precondition, postcondition, invariant

# Define contract conditions as pure Python functions
def amount_is_positive(amount):
    return amount > 0

def balance_non_negative(self):
    return self.balance >= 0

@invariant(balance_non_negative, "Account balance must be non-negative")
class BankAccount:
    def __init__(self, initial_balance: float = 0.0):
        self.balance = initial_balance
    
    @precondition(amount_is_positive, "Deposit amount must be positive")
    def deposit(self, amount: float) -> float:
        old_balance = self.balance
        self.balance += amount
        return old_balance
```

### CLI Usage

```bash
# Analyze current project
axiomander map

# Show contract statistics
axiomander stats

# Validate contract references
axiomander validate

# Export to JSON
axiomander map --format json --output contracts.json
```

### Introspection API

```python
import axiomander

# Get all contracts in current session
contracts = axiomander.get_all_contracts()

# Scan a project for contracts
graph = axiomander.scan_project_contracts(".")

# Get statistics
stats = axiomander.get_contract_statistics(graph)
print(f"Found {stats['total_contracts']} contracts")

# Export to JSON
json_output = axiomander.export_contracts_to_json(graph)
```

## Contract Types

### Preconditions
Validate input parameters before function execution:

```python
def positive_number(x):
    return x > 0

@precondition(positive_number, "Input must be positive")
def sqrt(x: float) -> float:
    return x ** 0.5
```

### Postconditions
Validate results after function execution:

```python
def result_is_positive(x, result):
    return result >= 0

@postcondition(result_is_positive, "Result must be non-negative")
def abs_value(x: float) -> float:
    return abs(x)
```

### Invariants
Maintain class-level constraints:

```python
def balance_non_negative(account):
    return account.balance >= 0

@invariant(balance_non_negative, "Balance must never be negative")
class BankAccount:
    # ... implementation
```

## Exception-Based Development

Use axiomander exceptions for type-correct incremental development:

```python
from axiomander import ImplementThis, DontImplementThis

def calculate_complex_result(data):
    # Use ImplementThis instead of pass
    raise ImplementThis("Complex calculation algorithm needs implementation")

def deprecated_method(self):
    raise DontImplementThis("Use new_method() instead")
```

## Requirements

- **Git Repository**: All projects using axiomander must be in a git repository for change tracking
- **Python 3.12+**: Modern Python features and type hints

## Examples

See the `examples/` directory for complete examples:

- `bank_account.py`: Comprehensive banking system with all contract types

## Development

This project is part of a larger design-by-contract agent system that will include:

- LSP server for IDE integration
- VSCode extension with contract visualization
- AI-powered contract generation and analysis
- SaaS backend for collaborative contract development

## License

[License information to be added]
=======
# Axiomander - The Helpful Contractual Salamander 🦎

An AI-powered design-by-contract system for Python that combines traditional contract decorators with intelligent specification creation, refinement, and code generation capabilities.

## Features

### Core Contract System
- **Contract Decorators**: `@precondition`, `@postcondition`, `@invariant`
- **Runtime Validation**: Contracts are evaluated at runtime with clear error messages
- **Introspection API**: Analyze and export contract information from your codebase
- **Project Scanning**: Discover and map all contracts across your project
- **Type Safety**: Use exceptions instead of `pass` for unimplemented code

### AI-Powered Agent System
- **Interactive Specification Creation**: AI-guided behavioral specification development
- **Real-time Streaming**: See AI responses as they're generated
- **Hierarchical Specifications**: Break complex specs into manageable components
- **Contract Generation**: Automatic contract descriptions from refined specifications
- **Git Integration**: All changes are properly committed with meaningful messages
- **Multiple LLM Support**: Works with Anthropic Claude and OpenAI models

### Advanced Workflow Features
- **Specification Decomposition**: Break large specifications into focused components
- **Component Synchronization**: Bidirectional sync between parent and child specs
- **Interactive CLI**: Natural language conversation with mode-specific AI assistants
- **Project Analysis**: AI-powered identification of contract opportunities
- **Refactoring Support**: Contract-aware code improvement suggestions

## Installation

```bash
pip install axiomander
```

## Quick Start

### 1. Basic Contract Usage

```python
from axiomander import precondition, postcondition, invariant

# Define contract conditions as pure Python functions
def amount_is_positive(amount):
    return amount > 0

def balance_non_negative(self):
    return self.balance >= 0

@invariant(balance_non_negative, "Account balance must be non-negative")
class BankAccount:
    def __init__(self, initial_balance: float = 0.0):
        self.balance = initial_balance
    
    @precondition(amount_is_positive, "Deposit amount must be positive")
    def deposit(self, amount: float) -> float:
        old_balance = self.balance
        self.balance += amount
        return old_balance
```

### 2. AI-Powered Specification Creation

```bash
# Set your API key
export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=your_api_key_here

# Start the agent backend
axiomander-server backend &

# Create a new behavioral specification with AI assistance
axiomander create-spec "user_authentication" "Handle user login with email and password"

# Refine the specification through conversation
axiomander refine-spec spec_20241203_120000_1234 "What about password reset functionality?"

# Generate contract descriptions when ready
axiomander generate-contracts spec_20241203_120000_1234
```

### 3. Interactive Mode

```bash
# Start interactive mode for natural conversation
axiomander interactive

# Example conversation:
# > What functions in my project need contracts?
# > Help me specify user authentication behavior
# > /mode specification
# > /create-spec "payment_processing" "Handle credit card payments securely"
```

### 4. Project Analysis

```bash
# Analyze your project for contract opportunities
axiomander analyze

# Generate contracts for a specific aim
axiomander generate "user input validation"

# Decompose complex requirements
axiomander decompose "e-commerce checkout system"

# Analyze refactoring opportunities
axiomander refactor
```

## AI Agent Modes

### Exploration Mode
- Analyze existing codebases for contract opportunities
- Identify functions that would benefit from contracts
- Understand current project structure and contract coverage

### Specification Mode
- Create and refine behavioral specifications through AI conversation
- Break specifications into manageable components
- Prepare specifications for contract generation

### Refinement Mode
- Decompose high-level aims into implementation components
- Design system architecture with contracts in mind
- Plan implementation phases

### Implementation Mode
- Generate actual contract code from specifications
- Refactor existing code to improve contract clarity
- Implement contract-driven development workflows

## Hierarchical Specifications

Axiomander supports breaking complex specifications into focused components:

```bash
# Analyze a specification for decomposition opportunities
axiomander analyze-decomposition spec_20241203_120000_1234

# Decompose into components
axiomander decompose-spec spec_20241203_120000_1234 "authentication,authorization,user_profile"

# Work on individual components
axiomander refine-component spec_20241203_120000_1234 comp_auth_5678 "Add OAuth support"

# Check synchronization status
axiomander sync-status spec_20241203_120000_1234
```

## File Structure

Axiomander creates a structured specification system:

```
.axiomander/
├── specs/
│   ├── user_management_system/
│   │   ├── metadata.json          # Specification metadata
│   │   ├── spec.md                # Main specification document
│   │   ├── conversation.json      # AI conversation history
│   │   ├── contracts.json         # Generated contract descriptions
│   │   └── components/            # Sub-specifications
│   │       ├── authentication/
│   │       │   ├── metadata.json
│   │       │   ├── spec.md
│   │       │   ├── conversation.json
│   │       │   └── sync_metadata.json
│   │       └── authorization/
│   │           └── ...
│   └── index.json                 # Specification registry
└── contracts/
    └── graph.json                 # Contract analysis cache
```

## Contract Types

### Preconditions
Validate input parameters before function execution:

```python
def positive_number(x):
    return x > 0

@precondition(positive_number, "Input must be positive")
def sqrt(x: float) -> float:
    return x ** 0.5
```

### Postconditions
Validate results after function execution:

```python
def result_is_positive(x, result):
    return result >= 0

@postcondition(result_is_positive, "Result must be non-negative")
def abs_value(x: float) -> float:
    return abs(x)
```

### Invariants
Maintain class-level constraints:

```python
def balance_non_negative(account):
    return account.balance >= 0

@invariant(balance_non_negative, "Balance must never be negative")
class BankAccount:
    # ... implementation
```

## Exception-Based Development

Use axiomander exceptions for type-correct incremental development:

```python
from axiomander import ImplementThis, DontImplementThis

def calculate_complex_result(data):
    # Use ImplementThis instead of pass
    raise ImplementThis("Complex calculation algorithm needs implementation")

def deprecated_method(self):
    raise DontImplementThis("Use new_method() instead")
```

## Configuration

### API Keys
Set your LLM provider API key:

```bash
# For Anthropic Claude (recommended)
export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=your_anthropic_key

# For OpenAI
export OPENAI_API_KEY=your_openai_key
```

### Server Configuration
```bash
# Custom server URL (default: http://localhost:8000)
export AXIOMANDER_SERVER_URL=http://your-server:8000

# Start server with custom settings
axiomander-server backend --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8080
```

### Model Selection
```bash
# List available models
axiomander models

# Use specific models
axiomander generate "user auth" --model opus-4 --provider anthropic
axiomander create-spec "payment" "Handle payments" --model gpt-4 --provider openai
```

## Requirements

- **Git Repository**: All projects using axiomander must be in a git repository
- **Python 3.12+**: Modern Python features and type hints
- **API Key**: Anthropic or OpenAI API key for AI features

## Examples

See the `examples/` directory for complete examples:

- `bank_account.py`: Comprehensive banking system with all contract types

## Architecture

Axiomander consists of several integrated components:

- **Core Contract Library**: Python decorators and runtime validation
- **Static Analysis Engine**: Project scanning and contract discovery
- **AI Agent Backend**: LangGraph-powered specification and contract generation
- **Interactive CLI**: Natural language interface with streaming responses
- **LSP Server**: IDE integration (planned)
- **VSCode Extension**: Visual contract management (planned)

## Development Workflow

1. **Explore**: Analyze your project to understand contract opportunities
2. **Specify**: Create behavioral specifications through AI conversation
3. **Decompose**: Break complex specifications into manageable components
4. **Generate**: Create contract descriptions from refined specifications
5. **Implement**: Write actual contract code and integrate with your project
6. **Refactor**: Improve code structure while preserving contracts

## Contributing

This project is under active development. Key areas for contribution:

- Contract decorator enhancements
- AI prompt engineering and workflow improvements
- LSP server implementation
- VSCode extension development
- Documentation and examples

## License

[License information to be added]
````

````python
"""
Example: AI-Powered Specification Workflow

This example demonstrates how to use Axiomander's AI-powered specification
system to create, refine, and generate contracts through conversation.

This is a documentation example showing the workflow - the actual commands
would be run in the terminal with the axiomander CLI.
"""

# This example shows the complete workflow from specification to implementation

def specification_workflow_example():
    """
    Example workflow for creating specifications with AI assistance.
    
    This demonstrates the commands you would run, but doesn't execute them.
    Run these commands in your terminal after setting up your API key.
    """
    
    workflow_steps = [
        # Step 1: Set up your environment
        "export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=your_api_key_here",
        "axiomander-server backend &  # Start the agent backend",
        
        # Step 2: Create a new specification
        'axiomander create-spec "user_authentication" "Handle user login with email and password validation"',
        
        # Step 3: Refine through conversation
        'axiomander refine-spec spec_20241203_120000_1234 "What about password reset functionality?"',
        'axiomander refine-spec spec_20241203_120000_1234 "How should we handle failed login attempts?"',
        'axiomander refine-spec spec_20241203_120000_1234 "Add support for two-factor authentication"',
        
        # Step 4: Generate contracts when ready
        'axiomander generate-contracts spec_20241203_120000_1234',
        
        # Step 5: View the results
        'axiomander show-spec spec_20241203_120000_1234 --verbose',
        'axiomander show-contracts spec_20241203_120000_1234',
    ]
    
    print("AI-Powered Specification Workflow:")
    print("=" * 50)
    for i, step in enumerate(workflow_steps, 1):
        print(f"{i:2d}. {step}")
    
    print("\nInteractive Mode Alternative:")
    print("=" * 30)
    interactive_example = [
        "axiomander interactive",
        "# Then in interactive mode:",
        "> I want to specify user authentication behavior",
        "> What happens when someone enters the wrong password?",
        "> How should we handle password complexity requirements?",
        "> /generate-contracts  # When specification is complete",
    ]
    
    for step in interactive_example:
        print(step)


def hierarchical_specification_example():
    """
    Example of breaking a complex specification into components.
    """
    
    workflow_steps = [
        # Step 1: Create a complex specification
        'axiomander create-spec "e_commerce_system" "Complete online shopping system with cart, checkout, and payment"',
        
        # Step 2: Analyze for decomposition opportunities
        'axiomander analyze-decomposition spec_20241203_130000_5678',
        
        # Step 3: Decompose into components
        'axiomander decompose-spec spec_20241203_130000_5678 "shopping_cart,payment_processing,order_management,inventory_tracking"',
        
        # Step 4: Work on individual components
        'axiomander refine-component spec_20241203_130000_5678 comp_cart_1234 "Add support for discount codes and promotions"',
        'axiomander refine-component spec_20241203_130000_5678 comp_payment_5678 "Integrate with Stripe and PayPal"',
        
        # Step 5: Check synchronization
        'axiomander sync-status spec_20241203_130000_5678',
        'axiomander show-hierarchy spec_20241203_130000_5678',
    ]
    
    print("\nHierarchical Specification Example:")
    print("=" * 40)
    for i, step in enumerate(workflow_steps, 1):
        print(f"{i:2d}. {step}")


def project_analysis_example():
    """
    Example of analyzing an existing project for contract opportunities.
    """
    
    analysis_steps = [
        # Step 1: Analyze existing project
        'axiomander analyze --mode exploration',
        
        # Step 2: Get specific recommendations
        'axiomander interactive',
        '> What functions in my project need contracts?',
        '> Show me the most critical areas for contract coverage',
        '> /mode specification',
        '> Help me create contracts for my validation functions',
        
        # Step 3: Generate contracts for specific aims
        'axiomander generate "input validation for user data"',
        'axiomander generate "error handling for database operations"',
        
        # Step 4: Refactoring analysis
        'axiomander refactor --goals "improve contract clarity" "reduce complexity"',
    ]
    
    print("\nProject Analysis Example:")
    print("=" * 30)
    for i, step in enumerate(analysis_steps, 1):
        print(f"{i:2d}. {step}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    print("Axiomander AI-Powered Specification Examples")
    print("=" * 50)
    print()
    print("This file demonstrates the AI-powered workflow capabilities.")
    print("These are the commands you would run in your terminal.")
    print()
    
    specification_workflow_example()
    hierarchical_specification_example()
    project_analysis_example()
    
    print("\nNext Steps:")
    print("=" * 15)
    print("1. Set your ANTHROPIC_API_KEY environment variable")
    print("2. Start the agent backend: axiomander-server backend")
    print("3. Try the interactive mode: axiomander interactive")
    print("4. Or create your first specification with: axiomander create-spec")
````

````python
"""
Example: Interactive Session Usage

This example demonstrates how to use Axiomander's interactive mode
for natural language conversation with AI assistants.

Run with: axiomander interactive
"""

def interactive_mode_examples():
    """
    Examples of natural language interactions in different modes.
    
    These show the types of conversations you can have with the AI
    in each mode of the interactive session.
    """
    
    examples = {
        "exploration_mode": [
            "What functions in my project need contracts?",
            "Analyze my codebase for contract opportunities",
            "Show me the current contract coverage",
            "Which classes would benefit from invariants?",
            "Help me understand where preconditions would be most valuable",
            "/analyze  # Run project analysis",
            "/scan     # Scan for existing contracts",
            "/stats    # Show contract statistics",
        ],
        
        "specification_mode": [
            "I want to specify user authentication behavior",
            "What happens when the password is incorrect?",
            "How should we handle account lockouts?",
            "What about password reset functionality?",
            "This specification is getting complex - should I break it into components?",
            "/show-spec                    # Show current specification",
            "/analyze-decomposition        # Analyze for component opportunities", 
            "/decompose auth,validation    # Break into components",
            "/generate-contracts           # Generate contract descriptions",
        ],
        
        "refinement_mode": [
            "Break down 'user management system' into components",
            "How should I structure a payment processing system?",
            "What contracts do I need for a REST API?",
            "Help me design the architecture for an e-commerce system",
            "/decompose 'online shopping platform'",
            "/generate 'secure payment processing'",
        ],
        
        "implementation_mode": [
            "Help me implement contracts for user validation",
            "How do I refactor this function to use contracts?",
            "Generate tests for my contract specifications",
            "What's the best way to handle contract violations?",
            "/refactor                     # Analyze refactoring opportunities",
            "/implement spec_12345         # Generate implementation",
        ]
    }
    
    print("Interactive Mode Examples")
    print("=" * 30)
    print()
    
    for mode, conversations in examples.items():
        print(f"{mode.replace('_', ' ').title()}:")
        print("-" * (len(mode) + 1))
        
        for conversation in conversations:
            if conversation.startswith("/"):
                print(f"  {conversation:<30} # Command")
            else:
                print(f"  > {conversation}")
        print()


def interactive_commands_reference():
    """
    Reference for interactive mode commands.
    """
    
    commands = {
        "Session Management": [
            "/status                       # Show current session status",
            "/help                         # Show available commands", 
            "/mode <mode>                  # Change mode (exploration/specification/refinement/implementation)",
            "/project <path>               # Change project directory",
            "/model <model>                # Change LLM model (sonnet/opus/haiku)",
            "/provider <provider>          # Change LLM provider (anthropic/openai)",
            "/quit                         # Exit interactive session",
        ],
        
        "Specification Management": [
            "/create-spec <name> <desc>    # Create new specification",
            "/list-specs                   # List all specifications",
            "/select-spec <id>             # Select specification to work with",
            "/show-spec                    # Show current specification details",
            "/refine <response>            # Refine current specification",
            "/generate-contracts           # Generate contracts from specification",
        ],
        
        "Hierarchical Specifications": [
            "/show-hierarchy               # Show specification component structure",
            "/analyze-decomposition        # Analyze decomposition opportunities",
            "/decompose <components>       # Decompose into named components",
            "/create-component <name> <desc> # Create new component",
            "/refine-component <id> <resp> # Refine component with parent sync",
            "/sync-status                  # Check synchronization status",
        ],
        
        "Project Analysis": [
            "/analyze                      # Analyze project for contract opportunities",
            "/scan                         # Scan project and build contract graph",
            "/stats                        # Show contract statistics",
            "/generate <aim>               # Generate contracts for specific aim",
            "/refactor                     # Analyze refactoring opportunities",
        ]
    }
    
    print("Interactive Commands Reference")
    print("=" * 35)
    print()
    
    for category, command_list in commands.items():
        print(f"{category}:")
        print("-" * (len(category) + 1))
        
        for command in command_list:
            print(f"  {command}")
        print()


def tab_completion_examples():
    """
    Examples of tab completion in interactive mode.
    """
    
    print("Tab Completion Examples")
    print("=" * 25)
    print()
    
    examples = [
        "Command completion:",
        "  /hel<TAB>           → /help",
        "  /show-<TAB>         → /show-spec, /show-hierarchy",
        "",
        "Specification ID completion:",
        "  /select-spec spec_<TAB>  → spec_20241203_120000_1234",
        "  /refine-spec <TAB>       → Shows available spec IDs",
        "",
        "Model completion:",
        "  /model son<TAB>     → sonnet",
        "  /model <TAB>        → sonnet, opus, haiku, ...",
        "",
        "Provider completion:",
        "  /provider ant<TAB>  → anthropic",
        "  /provider <TAB>     → anthropic, openai",
        "",
        "Directory completion:",
        "  /project ./src/<TAB>     → Shows subdirectories",
        "  /project /home/<TAB>     → Shows home subdirectories",
    ]
    
    for example in examples:
        print(example)


def workflow_integration_example():
    """
    Example showing how interactive mode integrates with the full workflow.
    """
    
    print("\nWorkflow Integration Example")
    print("=" * 35)
    print()
    
    workflow = [
        "# Start interactive session",
        "axiomander interactive",
        "",
        "# Exploration phase",
        "> What functions in my project need contracts?",
        "> /analyze",
        "> Show me the most critical areas",
        "",
        "# Switch to specification mode", 
        "> /mode specification",
        "> I want to specify user authentication",
        "> /create-spec 'user_auth' 'Handle login and registration'",
        "",
        "# Refine through conversation",
        "> What about password validation rules?",
        "> How should we handle failed login attempts?",
        "> /show-spec",
        "",
        "# Decompose if needed",
        "> This is getting complex - should I break it down?",
        "> /analyze-decomposition",
        "> /decompose authentication,validation,session_management",
        "",
        "# Work on components",
        "> /refine-component comp_auth_1234 'Add OAuth support'",
        "> /sync-status",
        "",
        "# Generate contracts",
        "> /generate-contracts",
        "> /show-contracts",
        "",
        "# Switch to implementation mode",
        "> /mode implementation", 
        "> Help me implement these contracts in my code",
    ]
    
    for step in workflow:
        print(step)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    print("Axiomander Interactive Mode Examples")
    print("=" * 40)
    print()
    print("This file shows how to use the interactive mode effectively.")
    print("Start interactive mode with: axiomander interactive")
    print()
    
    interactive_mode_examples()
    interactive_commands_reference()
    tab_completion_examples()
    workflow_integration_example()
    
    print("\nTips for Interactive Mode:")
    print("=" * 30)
    print("• Use natural language for conversation")
    print("• Use /commands for explicit actions")
    print("• Press TAB for command and ID completion")
    print("• Each mode has a specialized AI assistant")
    print("• Switch modes as your workflow progresses")
    print("• Use /help to see available commands anytime")
````

````python
"""
Example: Advanced Contract Patterns

This example demonstrates more sophisticated contract patterns and
integration with the AI-powered specification system.
"""

from axiomander import precondition, postcondition, invariant, ImplementThis
from typing import List, Dict, Optional
from dataclasses import dataclass
from enum import Enum


# Advanced contract condition functions
def valid_email_format(email: str) -> bool:
    """Check if email has valid format."""
    return "@" in email and "." in email.split("@")[1]


def password_meets_requirements(password: str) -> bool:
    """Check password complexity requirements."""
    return (len(password) >= 8 and 
            any(c.isupper() for c in password) and
            any(c.islower() for c in password) and
            any(c.isdigit() for c in password))


def user_exists_in_system(self, user_id: int) -> bool:
    """Check if user exists in the system."""
    return user_id in self.users


def session_is_valid(self, session_id: str) -> bool:
    """Check if session is valid and not expired."""
    return (session_id in self.active_sessions and 
            not self.active_sessions[session_id].is_expired())


def user_has_permission(self, user_id: int, permission: str) -> bool:
    """Check if user has required permission."""
    user = self.users.get(user_id)
    return user is not None and permission in user.permissions


def result_contains_user_data(user_id: int, result: Dict) -> bool:
    """Verify result contains expected user data."""
    return "user_id" in result and result["user_id"] == user_id


def session_created_successfully(session_id: str, result: Dict) -> bool:
    """Verify session was created with proper structure."""
    return (result.get("session_id") == session_id and 
            "expires_at" in result and
            "created_at" in result)


def users_dict_is_valid(self) -> bool:
    """Invariant: users dictionary maintains valid structure."""
    return (isinstance(self.users, dict) and
            all(isinstance(uid, int) and uid > 0 for uid in self.users.keys()))


def no_expired_sessions(self) -> bool:
    """Invariant: no expired sessions in active_sessions."""
    return all(not session.is_expired() 
               for session in self.active_sessions.values())


# Enums for better type safety
class UserRole(Enum):
    ADMIN = "admin"
    USER = "user"
    MODERATOR = "moderator"


class SessionStatus(Enum):
    ACTIVE = "active"
    EXPIRED = "expired"
    REVOKED = "revoked"


@dataclass
class User:
    """User data structure with contracts."""
    user_id: int
    email: str
    password_hash: str
    role: UserRole
    permissions: List[str]
    created_at: str
    is_active: bool = True


@dataclass 
class Session:
    """Session data structure."""
    session_id: str
    user_id: int
    created_at: str
    expires_at: str
    status: SessionStatus = SessionStatus.ACTIVE
    
    def is_expired(self) -> bool:
        """Check if session is expired."""
        # Simplified - in real implementation would check actual time
        return self.status == SessionStatus.EXPIRED


@invariant(users_dict_is_valid, "Users dictionary must maintain valid structure")
@invariant(no_expired_sessions, "No expired sessions should remain in active_sessions")
class UserManagementSystem:
    """
    Advanced user management system demonstrating sophisticated contracts.
    
    This class shows how contracts can be used for:
    - Complex validation logic
    - Security requirements
    - Data integrity constraints
    - Business rule enforcement
    """
    
    def __init__(self):
        self.users: Dict[int, User] = {}
        self.active_sessions: Dict[str, Session] = {}
        self.next_user_id = 1
    
    @precondition(lambda self, email: valid_email_format(email), 
                  "Email must have valid format")
    @precondition(lambda self, password: password_meets_requirements(password),
                  "Password must meet complexity requirements")
    @postcondition(lambda self, email, password, result: result["user_id"] > 0,
                   "Must return valid user ID")
    @postcondition(lambda self, email, password, result: result["email"] == email,
                   "Result must contain the provided email")
    def register_user(self, email: str, password: str, role: UserRole = UserRole.USER) -> Dict:
        """
        Register a new user with email and password.
        
        This method demonstrates:
        - Input validation contracts
        - Business rule enforcement
        - Output guarantee contracts
        """
        # Check if email already exists
        for user in self.users.values():
            if user.email == email:
                raise ValueError("Email already registered")
        
        user_id = self.next_user_id
        self.next_user_id += 1
        
        # In real implementation, would hash the password
        password_hash = f"hashed_{password}"
        
        # Set default permissions based on role
        permissions = {
            UserRole.ADMIN: ["read", "write", "delete", "admin"],
            UserRole.MODERATOR: ["read", "write", "moderate"],
            UserRole.USER: ["read"]
        }[role]
        
        user = User(
            user_id=user_id,
            email=email,
            password_hash=password_hash,
            role=role,
            permissions=permissions,
            created_at="2024-12-03T12:00:00Z"  # Simplified
        )
        
        self.users[user_id] = user
        
        return {
            "user_id": user_id,
            "email": email,
            "role": role.value,
            "created_at": user.created_at
        }
    
    @precondition(lambda self, email: valid_email_format(email),
                  "Email must have valid format")
    @precondition(lambda self, password: len(password) > 0,
                  "Password cannot be empty")
    @postcondition(lambda self, email, password, result: "session_id" in result,
                   "Must return session information")
    def authenticate_user(self, email: str, password: str) -> Dict:
        """
        Authenticate user and create session.
        
        Demonstrates:
        - Authentication logic contracts
        - Session creation guarantees
        - Security-focused postconditions
        """
        # Find user by email
        user = None
        for u in self.users.values():
            if u.email == email and u.is_active:
                user = u
                break
        
        if not user:
            raise ValueError("Invalid credentials")
        
        # In real implementation, would verify password hash
        expected_hash = f"hashed_{password}"
        if user.password_hash != expected_hash:
            raise ValueError("Invalid credentials")
        
        # Create session
        session_id = f"session_{user.user_id}_{len(self.active_sessions)}"
        session = Session(
            session_id=session_id,
            user_id=user.user_id,
            created_at="2024-12-03T12:00:00Z",
            expires_at="2024-12-03T16:00:00Z"  # 4 hours
        )
        
        self.active_sessions[session_id] = session
        
        return {
            "session_id": session_id,
            "user_id": user.user_id,
            "expires_at": session.expires_at,
            "created_at": session.created_at
        }
    
    @precondition(lambda self, session_id: isinstance(session_id, str) and len(session_id) > 0,
                  "Session ID must be non-empty string")
    @precondition(lambda self, session_id: session_is_valid(self, session_id),
                  "Session must be valid and not expired")
    @postcondition(lambda self, session_id, result: result_contains_user_data(
                   self.active_sessions[session_id].user_id, result),
                   "Result must contain user data for the session")
    def get_user_profile(self, session_id: str) -> Dict:
        """
        Get user profile for authenticated session.
        
        Demonstrates:
        - Session validation contracts
        - Authorization checks
        - Data access guarantees
        """
        session = self.active_sessions[session_id]
        user = self.users[session.user_id]
        
        return {
            "user_id": user.user_id,
            "email": user.email,
            "role": user.role.value,
            "permissions": user.permissions,
            "created_at": user.created_at,
            "is_active": user.is_active
        }
    
    @precondition(lambda self, session_id: session_is_valid(self, session_id),
                  "Session must be valid")
    @precondition(lambda self, session_id, permission: isinstance(permission, str),
                  "Permission must be a string")
    @postcondition(lambda self, session_id, permission, result: isinstance(result, bool),
                   "Must return boolean result")
    def check_permission(self, session_id: str, permission: str) -> bool:
        """
        Check if user has specific permission.
        
        Demonstrates:
        - Authorization contract patterns
        - Permission-based access control
        - Boolean result guarantees
        """
        session = self.active_sessions[session_id]
        return user_has_permission(self, session.user_id, permission)
    
    @precondition(lambda self, user_id: user_exists_in_system(self, user_id),
                  "User must exist in system")
    @precondition(lambda self, user_id, admin_session_id: session_is_valid(self, admin_session_id),
                  "Admin session must be valid")
    @precondition(lambda self, user_id, admin_session_id: user_has_permission(
                  self, self.active_sessions[admin_session_id].user_id, "admin"),
                  "Must have admin permission to deactivate users")
    def deactivate_user(self, user_id: int, admin_session_id: str) -> Dict:
        """
        Deactivate a user account (admin only).
        
        Demonstrates:
        - Administrative action contracts
        - Permission-based preconditions
        - Audit trail requirements
        """
        user = self.users[user_id]
        user.is_active = False
        
        # Revoke all active sessions for this user
        sessions_to_revoke = [
            sid for sid, session in self.active_sessions.items()
            if session.user_id == user_id
        ]
        
        for session_id in sessions_to_revoke:
            self.active_sessions[session_id].status = SessionStatus.REVOKED
            del self.active_sessions[session_id]
        
        return {
            "user_id": user_id,
            "deactivated_at": "2024-12-03T12:00:00Z",
            "sessions_revoked": len(sessions_to_revoke)
        }
    
    def cleanup_expired_sessions(self) -> int:
        """
        Clean up expired sessions to maintain invariants.
        
        This method helps maintain the no_expired_sessions invariant
        by periodically removing expired sessions.
        """
        expired_sessions = [
            sid for sid, session in self.active_sessions.items()
            if session.is_expired()
        ]
        
        for session_id in expired_sessions:
            del self.active_sessions[session_id]
        
        return len(expired_sessions)
    
    def advanced_user_search(self, criteria: Dict) -> List[Dict]:
        """
        Advanced user search with complex business logic.
        
        This method would benefit from AI-generated contracts
        based on a behavioral specification.
        """
        # This is a placeholder for complex search logic
        raise ImplementThis(
            "Advanced search requires specification of search criteria, "
            "result filtering rules, and performance constraints. "
            "Use axiomander to create a behavioral specification first."
        )


def demonstrate_contract_violations():
    """
    Demonstrate how contracts catch violations at runtime.
    """
    system = UserManagementSystem()
    
    print("Demonstrating Contract Violations:")
    print("=" * 40)
    
    # Test cases that should trigger contract violations
    test_cases = [
        ("Invalid email format", lambda: system.register_user("invalid-email", "Password123!")),
        ("Weak password", lambda: system.register_user("test@example.com", "weak")),
        ("Empty session ID", lambda: system.get_user_profile("")),
        ("Invalid session", lambda: system.get_user_profile("invalid_session")),
    ]
    
    for description, test_func in test_cases:
        try:
            test_func()
            print(f"❌ {description}: Should have failed but didn't")
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"✅ {description}: Caught - {e}")


def demonstrate_successful_workflow():
    """
    Demonstrate successful workflow with contracts.
    """
    system = UserManagementSystem()
    
    print("\nDemonstrating Successful Workflow:")
    print("=" * 40)
    
    try:
        # Register users
        admin_result = system.register_user("admin@example.com", "AdminPass123!", UserRole.ADMIN)
        user_result = system.register_user("user@example.com", "UserPass123!", UserRole.USER)
        
        print(f"✅ Registered admin: {admin_result['user_id']}")
        print(f"✅ Registered user: {user_result['user_id']}")
        
        # Authenticate
        admin_auth = system.authenticate_user("admin@example.com", "AdminPass123!")
        user_auth = system.authenticate_user("user@example.com", "UserPass123!")
        
        print(f"✅ Admin authenticated: {admin_auth['session_id']}")
        print(f"✅ User authenticated: {user_auth['session_id']}")
        
        # Get profiles
        admin_profile = system.get_user_profile(admin_auth['session_id'])
        user_profile = system.get_user_profile(user_auth['session_id'])
        
        print(f"✅ Got admin profile: {admin_profile['email']}")
        print(f"✅ Got user profile: {user_profile['email']}")
        
        # Check permissions
        admin_can_admin = system.check_permission(admin_auth['session_id'], "admin")
        user_can_admin = system.check_permission(user_auth['session_id'], "admin")
        
        print(f"✅ Admin has admin permission: {admin_can_admin}")
        print(f"✅ User has admin permission: {user_can_admin}")
        
        # Admin deactivates user
        deactivation_result = system.deactivate_user(user_result['user_id'], admin_auth['session_id'])
        print(f"✅ User deactivated: {deactivation_result}")
        
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"❌ Workflow failed: {e}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    print("Advanced Contract Patterns Example")
    print("=" * 40)
    print()
    print("This example shows sophisticated contract patterns for:")
    print("• Complex validation logic")
    print("• Security and authorization")
    print("• Business rule enforcement")
    print("• Data integrity constraints")
    print()
    
    demonstrate_successful_workflow()
    demonstrate_contract_violations()
    
    print("\nNext Steps:")
    print("=" * 15)
    print("1. Use 'axiomander analyze' to find contract opportunities in your code")
    print("2. Create specifications for complex business logic like advanced_user_search()")
    print("3. Use 'axiomander interactive' to refine contract requirements")
    print("4. Generate contract descriptions with 'axiomander generate-contracts'")
````

These additional examples provide:

1. **specification_workflow.py** - Shows the complete AI-powered workflow from specification creation to contract generation
2. **interactive_session.py** - Demonstrates how to use interactive mode effectively with examples of natural language conversations and command usage
3. **advanced_contracts.py** - Shows sophisticated contract patterns for real-world scenarios like user management, authentication, and authorization

These examples complement the existing `bank_account.py` by showing:
- How to use the AI-powered features
- Interactive mode capabilities
- Complex contract patterns
- Integration between specification and implementation
- Tab completion and command reference

The examples are designed to be both educational and practical, showing users how to leverage Axiomander's full capabilities.

#### /ask How does the decompose operation work - I would like to decompose interactively where each segment is given a suggested name and scope and I approve and then it is constructed.

#### How does the decompose operation work - I would like to decompose interactively where each segment is given a suggested name and scope and I approve and then it is constructed.  

Looking at the current implementation, the decompose operation works in a batch mode where you provide all component names upfront and they're all created at once. Here's how it currently works and how we could enhance it for interactive decomposition:

## Current Decompose Operation

**Current Flow:**
1. `axiomander analyze-decomposition spec_id` - AI analyzes and suggests component opportunities
2. `axiomander decompose-spec spec_id "comp1,comp2,comp3"` - Creates all components at once
3. Each component gets an AI-generated specification based on the component name and parent spec

**Current Limitations:**
- No preview of what each component will contain
- No approval step before creation
- Component scope is inferred from name only
- All-or-nothing creation

## Proposed Interactive Decomposition

Here's how we could enhance it for interactive approval:

### Enhanced Workflow

```bash
# 1. Analyze and get interactive decomposition suggestions
axiomander decompose-interactive spec_id

# This would:
# - AI analyzes the specification
# - Suggests 3-5 component breakdowns with names and scopes
# - Shows preview of what each component would contain
# - Asks for approval/modification of each component
# - Creates only approved components
```

### Interactive Session Example

```
> /decompose-interactive

🤖 AI: I've analyzed your "user_management_system" specification. Here are suggested components:

┌─ Component 1: "authentication" ─────────────────────────────────┐
│ Scope: Login, logout, password validation, session management   │
│ Sections from parent: "Login Process", "Session Handling"       │
│ Dependencies: None                                               │
│ Estimated complexity: Medium                                     │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Approve this component? [Y/n/edit]: y

┌─ Component 2: "user_profile" ───────────────────────────────────┐
│ Scope: Profile creation, updates, data validation               │
│ Sections from parent: "User Data", "Profile Management"         │
│ Dependencies: authentication                                     │
│ Estimated complexity: Low                                        │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Approve this component? [Y/n/edit]: edit

What would you like to change about "user_profile"?
> Rename to "profile_management" and add avatar upload functionality

Updated component:
┌─ Component 2: "profile_management" ─────────────────────────────┐
│ Scope: Profile creation, updates, data validation, avatars      │
│ Sections from parent: "User Data", "Profile Management"         │
│ Dependencies: authentication                                     │
│ Estimated complexity: Medium                                     │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Approve this component? [Y/n/edit]: y

Creating approved components...
✓ Created: authentication (comp_20241203_120000_1234)
✓ Created: profile_management (comp_20241203_120000_5678)
```

## Implementation Plan

### 1. New Backend Endpoint

```python
@app.get("/stream/decompose-interactive")
async def stream_decompose_interactive(
    specification_id: str,
    model: str = "sonnet",
    provider: str = "anthropic",
    api_key: str = Depends(verify_api_key)
):
    """Interactive decomposition with component preview and approval."""
```

### 2. Enhanced AI Analysis

The AI would provide structured component suggestions:

```python
# AI generates detailed component analysis
component_suggestions = [
    {
        "name": "authentication",
        "suggested_scope": "Login, logout, password validation, session management",
        "parent_sections": ["Login Process", "Session Handling", "Security Requirements"],
        "dependencies": [],
        "complexity": "medium",
        "rationale": "Authentication is a distinct concern that can be developed independently",
        "preview_content": "# Authentication Component\n\n## Login Process\n..."
    },
    {
        "name": "user_profile", 
        "suggested_scope": "Profile creation, updates, data validation",
        "parent_sections": ["User Data", "Profile Management"],
        "dependencies": ["authentication"],
        "complexity": "low",
        "rationale": "Profile management has clear boundaries and depends only on auth",
        "preview_content": "# User Profile Component\n\n## Profile Data\n..."
    }
]
```

### 3. Interactive CLI Command

```python
@app.command()
def decompose_interactive(
    specification_id: str = typer.Argument(..., help="Specification ID to decompose"),
    model: str = typer.Option("sonnet", "--model", help="LLM model to use"),
    provider: str = typer.Option("anthropic", "--provider", help="LLM provider"),
):
    """Interactively decompose specification with component preview and approval."""
```

### 4. Component Preview and Approval

```python
def preview_component(component_suggestion):
    """Show detailed component preview."""
    console.print(Panel(
        f"Scope: {component_suggestion['suggested_scope']}\n"
        f"Sections: {', '.join(component_suggestion['parent_sections'])}\n"
        f"Dependencies: {', '.join(component_suggestion['dependencies']) or 'None'}\n"
        f"Complexity: {component_suggestion['complexity']}\n\n"
        f"Rationale: {component_suggestion['rationale']}",
        title=f"Component: {component_suggestion['name']}",
        border_style="blue"
    ))
    
    # Show preview of generated content
    if component_suggestion.get('preview_content'):
        console.print(Panel(
            component_suggestion['preview_content'][:300] + "...",
            title="Content Preview",
            border_style="dim"
        ))

def get_user_approval(component_suggestion):
    """Get user approval for component."""
    choice = Prompt.ask(
        "Approve this component?",
        choices=["y", "n", "edit", "preview"],
        default="y"
    )
    
    if choice == "edit":
        return edit_component(component_suggestion)
    elif choice == "preview":
        show_full_preview(component_suggestion)
        return get_user_approval(component_suggestion)
    else:
        return choice == "y"
```

### 5. Component Editing

```python
def edit_component(component_suggestion):
    """Allow user to edit component details."""
    console.print("[cyan]What would you like to change?[/cyan]")
    console.print("1. Name")
    console.print("2. Scope") 
    console.print("3. Add/remove sections")
    console.print("4. Custom instructions")
    
    choice = Prompt.ask("Select option", choices=["1", "2", "3", "4"])
    
    if choice == "1":
        new_name = Prompt.ask("New component name")
        component_suggestion["name"] = new_name
    elif choice == "2":
        new_scope = Prompt.ask("New scope description")
        component_suggestion["suggested_scope"] = new_scope
    # ... handle other options
    
    # Regenerate preview with changes
    updated_component = regenerate_component_with_changes(component_suggestion)
    preview_component(updated_component)
    
    return get_user_approval(updated_component)
```

## Benefits of Interactive Decomposition

1. **User Control**: See exactly what each component will contain before creation
2. **Iterative Refinement**: Edit component scope and boundaries before committing
3. **Dependency Awareness**: Understand component relationships upfront
4. **Scope Validation**: Ensure components have appropriate boundaries
5. **Selective Creation**: Only create components you actually want

## Integration with Current System

This would work alongside the existing batch decomposition:

- `decompose-spec` - Current batch mode (keep for automation)
- `decompose-interactive` - New interactive mode (better for exploration)
- Both create the same hierarchical structure
- Both integrate with the sync system

Would you like me to implement this interactive decomposition system? It would provide much better control over the component creation process while maintaining the existing functionality.

> Tokens: 90k sent, 1.7k received. Cost: $0.30 message, $21.66 session.  
> Warning: it's best to only add files that need changes to the chat.  
> https://aider.chat/docs/troubleshooting/edit-errors.html  

#### Yes please implement this.  

I'll implement the interactive decomposition system. This requires changes to both the backend and client to support the interactive workflow with component preview and approval.

````python
@app.get("/stream/decompose-interactive")
async def stream_decompose_interactive(
    specification_id: str,
    model: str = "sonnet",
    provider: str = "anthropic",
    api_key: str = Depends(verify_api_key)
):
    """Interactive decomposition with component preview and approval."""
    session_id = generate_session_id()
    
    async def decompose_interactive_stream():
        try:
            await emit_sse_event(session_id, "progress", {
                "step": "loading_specification",
                "progress": 0,
                "message": "Loading specification for interactive decomposition..."
            })
            
            # Load specification from files
            spec_dir = f".axiomander/specs/{specification_id}"
            spec_path = Path(spec_dir)
            
            if not spec_path.exists():
                raise Exception(f"Specification {specification_id} not found")
            
            # Load metadata and content
            metadata_path = spec_path / "metadata.json"
            with open(metadata_path, 'r') as f:
                metadata = json.load(f)
            
            # Check if already composite
            if metadata.get("type") == "composite":
                raise Exception("Specification is already decomposed into components")
            
            spec_content_path = spec_path / "spec.md"
            spec_content = ""
            if spec_content_path.exists():
                spec_content = spec_content_path.read_text(encoding='utf-8')
            
            conversation_path = spec_path / "conversation.json"
            conversation = {"messages": []}
            if conversation_path.exists():
                with open(conversation_path, 'r') as f:
                    conversation = json.load(f)
            
            await emit_sse_event(session_id, "progress", {
                "step": "analyzing_components",
                "progress": 20,
                "message": "Analyzing specification for component suggestions..."
            })
            
            # Create context for AI
            context = AgentContext(
                project_root=metadata["project_root"],
                mode=AgentMode.SPECIFICATION,
                model=model,
                provider=LLMProvider(provider)
            )
            
            llm_service = await create_llm_service_from_context(context, api_key)
            
            # Generate detailed component suggestions
            system_prompt = """You are an expert in software architecture and specification decomposition. Analyze the given specification and suggest 3-5 natural component breakdowns.

For each suggested component, provide:
1. **Component Name**: Clear, descriptive name (snake_case)
2. **Scope**: What this component will handle (1-2 sentences)
3. **Parent Sections**: Which sections from the parent spec this covers
4. **Dependencies**: Which other components this depends on
5. **Complexity**: Estimated complexity (low/medium/high)
6. **Rationale**: Why this is a good component boundary
7. **Preview Content**: A brief preview of what the component spec would contain

Focus on creating cohesive, loosely-coupled components with clear boundaries. Each component should represent a distinct concern or functional area.

Return your analysis as a structured response that clearly separates each component suggestion."""
            
            # Build analysis context
            analysis_context = f"""
SPECIFICATION TO DECOMPOSE:
Name: {metadata.get('name', 'Unknown')}
Created: {metadata.get('created_at', 'Unknown')}

SPECIFICATION CONTENT:
{spec_content}

CONVERSATION CONTEXT:
"""
            
            # Add conversation context
            for msg in conversation.get("messages", []):
                role = msg.get("role", "unknown").title()
                content = msg.get("content", "")
                analysis_context += f"\n{role}: {content}\n"
            
            await emit_sse_event(session_id, "progress", {
                "step": "generating_suggestions",
                "progress": 40,
                "message": "Generating component suggestions..."
            })
            
            # Get AI component suggestions
            messages = [HumanMessage(content=f"Analyze this specification for interactive decomposition:\n\n{analysis_context}")]
            
            ai_response = ""
            token_buffer = ""
            async for token in llm_service.chat_model.astream([SystemMessage(content=system_prompt)] + messages):
                if hasattr(token, 'content') and token.content:
                    ai_response += token.content
                    token_buffer += token.content
                    
                    # Send tokens in larger chunks to reduce fragmentation
                    if len(token_buffer) >= 15 or token.content.endswith((' ', '\n', '.', '!', '?', ':', ';', ',')):
                        await emit_sse_event(session_id, "ai_response", {
                            "content": token_buffer,
                            "complete": False
                        })
                        token_buffer = ""
            
            # Send any remaining buffered content
            if token_buffer:
                await emit_sse_event(session_id, "ai_response", {
                    "content": token_buffer,
                    "complete": False
                })
            
            await emit_sse_event(session_id, "ai_response", {
                "content": "",
                "complete": True
            })
            
            await emit_sse_event(session_id, "progress", {
                "step": "parsing_suggestions",
                "progress": 70,
                "message": "Parsing component suggestions..."
            })
            
            # Parse AI response into structured component suggestions
            component_suggestions = _parse_component_suggestions(ai_response)
            
            await emit_sse_event(session_id, "progress", {
                "step": "ready_for_interaction",
                "progress": 90,
                "message": "Component suggestions ready for review..."
            })
            
            await emit_sse_event(session_id, "progress", {
                "step": "complete",
                "progress": 100,
                "message": "Interactive decomposition ready!"
            })
            
            # Send completion event with component suggestions
            await emit_sse_event(session_id, "complete", {
                "success": True,
                "specification_id": specification_id,
                "component_suggestions": component_suggestions,
                "ai_analysis": ai_response,
                "interaction_mode": "component_approval",
                "next_steps": [
                    "Review each suggested component",
                    "Approve, edit, or reject components",
                    "Create approved components"
                ]
            })
            
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(f"Error in interactive decomposition stream: {e}")
            await emit_sse_event(session_id, "error", {
                "message": str(e),
                "step": "error"
            })
            await emit_sse_event(session_id, "complete", {
                "success": False,
                "error": str(e)
            })
    
    # Start the background task
    asyncio.create_task(decompose_interactive_stream())
    
    # Return SSE stream
    return StreamingResponse(
        sse_generator(session_id),
        media_type="text/plain",
        headers={
            "Cache-Control": "no-cache",
            "Connection": "keep-alive",
            "Content-Type": "text/event-stream",
        }
    )

async def stream_decompose_specification(
    specification_id: str,
    component_names: str,  # comma-separated
    model: str = "sonnet",
    provider: str = "anthropic",
    api_key: str = Depends(verify_api_key)
):
    """Decompose specification into named components (batch mode)."""
    session_id = generate_session_id()
````
