````python
<<<<<<< SEARCH
=======
"""
Enhanced metadata models for hierarchical specifications.

This module defines the data structures for parent/child specification
relationships, component tracking, and synchronization metadata.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import datetime
from enum import Enum
from typing import Dict, List, Optional, Any, Union
from pathlib import Path
import json

from pydantic import BaseModel, Field


class SpecificationType(str, Enum):
    """Type of specification."""
    MONOLITHIC = "monolithic"
    COMPOSITE = "composite"  # Parent with components
    COMPONENT = "component"   # Child of composite


class SyncStrategy(str, Enum):
    """Synchronization strategy for components."""
    BIDIRECTIONAL = "bidirectional"
    COMPONENT_TO_PARENT = "component_to_parent"
    PARENT_TO_COMPONENT = "parent_to_component"
    MANUAL = "manual"


class AssemblyStrategy(str, Enum):
    """Strategy for assembling component contracts."""
    INTEGRATED = "integrated"  # Merge all contracts
    SEPARATE = "separate"      # Keep contracts separate
    HIERARCHICAL = "hierarchical"  # Nested contract structure


class ChangeType(str, Enum):
    """Type of change in synchronization."""
    ADDITION = "addition"
    MODIFICATION = "modification"
    DELETION = "deletion"
    RESTRUCTURE = "restructure"


@dataclass
class ComponentReference:
    """Reference to a component within a parent specification."""
    
    id: str = Field(..., description="Unique component identifier")
    name: str = Field(..., description="Human-readable component name")
    ready_for_contracts: bool = Field(False, description="Whether component is ready for contract generation")
    last_sync: Optional[datetime] = Field(None, description="Last synchronization timestamp")
    created_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=datetime.utcnow, description="Component creation timestamp")
    
    def to_dict(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Convert to dictionary for JSON serialization."""
        return {
            "id": self.id,
            "name": self.name,
            "ready_for_contracts": self.ready_for_contracts,
            "last_sync": self.last_sync.isoformat() if self.last_sync else None,
            "created_at": self.created_at.isoformat()
        }
    
    @classmethod
    def from_dict(cls, data: Dict[str, Any]) -> "ComponentReference":
        """Create from dictionary."""
        return cls(
            id=data["id"],
            name=data["name"],
            ready_for_contracts=data.get("ready_for_contracts", False),
            last_sync=datetime.fromisoformat(data["last_sync"]) if data.get("last_sync") else None,
            created_at=datetime.fromisoformat(data["created_at"])
        )


@dataclass
class SyncChange:
    """Represents a single change during synchronization."""
    
    type: ChangeType = Field(..., description="Type of change")
    section: str = Field(..., description="Section affected by change")
    parent_impact: List[str] = Field(default_factory=list, description="Parent sections impacted")
    summary: Optional[str] = Field(None, description="Human-readable summary of change")
    
    def to_dict(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Convert to dictionary for JSON serialization."""
        return {
            "type": self.type.value,
            "section": self.section,
            "parent_impact": self.parent_impact,
            "summary": self.summary
        }
    
    @classmethod
    def from_dict(cls, data: Dict[str, Any]) -> "SyncChange":
        """Create from dictionary."""
        return cls(
            type=ChangeType(data["type"]),
            section=data["section"],
            parent_impact=data.get("parent_impact", []),
            summary=data.get("summary")
        )


@dataclass
class ParentUpdate:
    """Represents an update to parent specification during sync."""
    
    section: str = Field(..., description="Parent section updated")
    change_type: str = Field(..., description="Type of change (expanded, modified, etc.)")
    summary: str = Field(..., description="Summary of the update")
    
    def to_dict(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Convert to dictionary for JSON serialization."""
        return {
            "section": self.section,
            "change_type": self.change_type,
            "summary": self.summary
        }
    
    @classmethod
    def from_dict(cls, data: Dict[str, Any]) -> "ParentUpdate":
        """Create from dictionary."""
        return cls(
            section=data["section"],
            change_type=data["change_type"],
            summary=data["summary"]
        )


@dataclass
class SyncOperation:
    """Represents a complete synchronization operation."""
    
    timestamp: datetime = Field(default_factory=datetime.utcnow, description="When sync occurred")
    operation: str = Field(..., description="Type of sync operation")
    changes: List[SyncChange] = Field(default_factory=list, description="Changes made to component")
    parent_updates: List[ParentUpdate] = Field(default_factory=list, description="Updates made to parent")
    
    def to_dict(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Convert to dictionary for JSON serialization."""
        return {
            "timestamp": self.timestamp.isoformat(),
            "operation": self.operation,
            "changes": [change.to_dict() for change in self.changes],
            "parent_updates": [update.to_dict() for update in self.parent_updates]
        }
    
    @classmethod
    def from_dict(cls, data: Dict[str, Any]) -> "SyncOperation":
        """Create from dictionary."""
        return cls(
            timestamp=datetime.fromisoformat(data["timestamp"]),
            operation=data["operation"],
            changes=[SyncChange.from_dict(c) for c in data.get("changes", [])],
            parent_updates=[ParentUpdate.from_dict(u) for u in data.get("parent_updates", [])]
        )


class CompositeSpecificationMetadata(BaseModel):
    """Metadata for parent specifications with components."""
    
    id: str = Field(..., description="Unique specification identifier")
    name: str = Field(..., description="Human-readable specification name")
    type: SpecificationType = Field(SpecificationType.COMPOSITE, description="Specification type")
    created_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=datetime.utcnow, description="Creation timestamp")
    updated_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=datetime.utcnow, description="Last update timestamp")
    ready_for_contracts: bool = Field(False, description="Whether ready for contract generation")
    
    components: List[ComponentReference] = Field(default_factory=list, description="Child components")
    assembly_strategy: AssemblyStrategy = Field(AssemblyStrategy.INTEGRATED, description="Contract assembly strategy")
    dependencies: Dict[str, List[str]] = Field(default_factory=dict, description="Component dependencies")
    
    def to_dict(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Convert to dictionary for JSON serialization."""
        return {
            "id": self.id,
            "name": self.name,
            "type": self.type.value,
            "created_at": self.created_at.isoformat(),
            "updated_at": self.updated_at.isoformat(),
            "ready_for_contracts": self.ready_for_contracts,
            "components": [comp.to_dict() for comp in self.components],
            "assembly_strategy": self.assembly_strategy.value,
            "dependencies": self.dependencies
        }
    
    @classmethod
    def from_dict(cls, data: Dict[str, Any]) -> "CompositeSpecificationMetadata":
        """Create from dictionary."""
        return cls(
            id=data["id"],
            name=data["name"],
            type=SpecificationType(data.get("type", "composite")),
            created_at=datetime.fromisoformat(data["created_at"]),
            updated_at=datetime.fromisoformat(data["updated_at"]),
            ready_for_contracts=data.get("ready_for_contracts", False),
            components=[ComponentReference.from_dict(c) for c in data.get("components", [])],
            assembly_strategy=AssemblyStrategy(data.get("assembly_strategy", "integrated")),
            dependencies=data.get("dependencies", {})
        )


class ComponentSpecificationMetadata(BaseModel):
    """Metadata for component specifications."""
    
    id: str = Field(..., description="Unique component identifier")
    name: str = Field(..., description="Human-readable component name")
    type: SpecificationType = Field(SpecificationType.COMPONENT, description="Specification type")
    parent_spec: str = Field(..., description="Parent specification ID")
    created_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=datetime.utcnow, description="Creation timestamp")
    updated_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=datetime.utcnow, description="Last update timestamp")
    ready_for_contracts: bool = Field(False, description="Whether ready for contract generation")
    
    parent_sections: List[str] = Field(default_factory=list, description="Parent sections this component affects")
    affects_siblings: List[str] = Field(default_factory=list, description="Sibling components affected by this one")
    sync_strategy: SyncStrategy = Field(SyncStrategy.BIDIRECTIONAL, description="Synchronization strategy")
    
    def to_dict(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Convert to dictionary for JSON serialization."""
        return {
            "id": self.id,
            "name": self.name,
            "type": self.type.value,
            "parent_spec": self.parent_spec,
            "created_at": self.created_at.isoformat(),
            "updated_at": self.updated_at.isoformat(),
            "ready_for_contracts": self.ready_for_contracts,
            "parent_sections": self.parent_sections,
            "affects_siblings": self.affects_siblings,
            "sync_strategy": self.sync_strategy.value
        }
    
    @classmethod
    def from_dict(cls, data: Dict[str, Any]) -> "ComponentSpecificationMetadata":
        """Create from dictionary."""
        return cls(
            id=data["id"],
            name=data["name"],
            type=SpecificationType(data.get("type", "component")),
            parent_spec=data["parent_spec"],
            created_at=datetime.fromisoformat(data["created_at"]),
            updated_at=datetime.fromisoformat(data["updated_at"]),
            ready_for_contracts=data.get("ready_for_contracts", False),
            parent_sections=data.get("parent_sections", []),
            affects_siblings=data.get("affects_siblings", []),
            sync_strategy=SyncStrategy(data.get("sync_strategy", "bidirectional"))
        )


class SynchronizationMetadata(BaseModel):
    """Metadata for tracking synchronization between components and parents."""
    
    component_id: str = Field(..., description="Component identifier")
    parent_spec: str = Field(..., description="Parent specification identifier")
    last_sync: Optional[datetime] = Field(None, description="Last synchronization timestamp")
    
    sync_operations: List[SyncOperation] = Field(default_factory=list, description="History of sync operations")
    conflict_resolution: List[Dict[str, Any]] = Field(default_factory=list, description="Conflict resolution history")
    pending_syncs: List[str] = Field(default_factory=list, description="Pending synchronization operations")
    
    def to_dict(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Convert to dictionary for JSON serialization."""
        return {
            "component_id": self.component_id,
            "parent_spec": self.parent_spec,
            "last_sync": self.last_sync.isoformat() if self.last_sync else None,
            "sync_operations": [op.to_dict() for op in self.sync_operations],
            "conflict_resolution": self.conflict_resolution,
            "pending_syncs": self.pending_syncs
        }
    
    @classmethod
    def from_dict(cls, data: Dict[str, Any]) -> "SynchronizationMetadata":
        """Create from dictionary."""
        return cls(
            component_id=data["component_id"],
            parent_spec=data["parent_spec"],
            last_sync=datetime.fromisoformat(data["last_sync"]) if data.get("last_sync") else None,
            sync_operations=[SyncOperation.from_dict(op) for op in data.get("sync_operations", [])],
            conflict_resolution=data.get("conflict_resolution", []),
            pending_syncs=data.get("pending_syncs", [])
        )


# Union type for specification metadata
SpecificationMetadata = Union[CompositeSpecificationMetadata, ComponentSpecificationMetadata]


def load_specification_metadata(metadata_path: Path) -> SpecificationMetadata:
    """Load specification metadata from file."""
    with open(metadata_path, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        data = json.load(f)
    
    spec_type = SpecificationType(data.get("type", "monolithic"))
    
    if spec_type == SpecificationType.COMPOSITE:
        return CompositeSpecificationMetadata.from_dict(data)
    elif spec_type == SpecificationType.COMPONENT:
        return ComponentSpecificationMetadata.from_dict(data)
    else:
        # Handle legacy monolithic specs - convert to composite for compatibility
        return CompositeSpecificationMetadata.from_dict({
            **data,
            "type": "composite",
            "components": [],
            "assembly_strategy": "integrated",
            "dependencies": {}
        })


def save_specification_metadata(metadata: SpecificationMetadata, metadata_path: Path) -> None:
    """Save specification metadata to file."""
    with open(metadata_path, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        json.dump(metadata.to_dict(), f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)


def load_synchronization_metadata(sync_path: Path) -> SynchronizationMetadata:
    """Load synchronization metadata from file."""
    with open(sync_path, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        data = json.load(f)
    return SynchronizationMetadata.from_dict(data)


def save_synchronization_metadata(sync_metadata: SynchronizationMetadata, sync_path: Path) -> None:
    """Save synchronization metadata to file."""
    with open(sync_path, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        json.dump(sync_metadata.to_dict(), f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)

"""
Hierarchical specification management system.

This module provides the core functionality for managing parent/child
specification relationships, component discovery, and file system operations.
"""

import json
import shutil
from datetime import datetime
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Dict, List, Optional, Set, Tuple, Any

from .specification_models import (
    CompositeSpecificationMetadata,
    ComponentSpecificationMetadata,
    ComponentReference,
    SynchronizationMetadata,
    SpecificationType,
    SyncStrategy,
    AssemblyStrategy,
    load_specification_metadata,
    save_specification_metadata,
    load_synchronization_metadata,
    save_synchronization_metadata,
)
from .exceptions import AxiomanderException


class HierarchicalSpecificationError(AxiomanderException):
    """Base exception for hierarchical specification operations."""
    pass


class ComponentNotFoundError(HierarchicalSpecificationError):
    """Raised when a component cannot be found."""
    pass


class InvalidHierarchyError(HierarchicalSpecificationError):
    """Raised when specification hierarchy is invalid."""
    pass


class CircularDependencyError(HierarchicalSpecificationError):
    """Raised when circular dependencies are detected."""
    pass


class HierarchicalSpecificationManager:
    """Manager for hierarchical specification operations."""
    
    def __init__(self, project_root: Path):
        """Initialize the manager with project root."""
        self.project_root = Path(project_root)
        self.specs_dir = self.project_root / ".axiomander" / "specs"
        self.backups_dir = self.project_root / ".axiomander" / "backups"
        
        # Ensure directories exist
        self.specs_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        self.backups_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    
    def get_specification_path(self, spec_id: str) -> Path:
        """Get the path to a specification directory."""
        return self.specs_dir / spec_id
    
    def get_component_path(self, parent_spec_id: str, component_id: str) -> Path:
        """Get the path to a component directory."""
        return self.get_specification_path(parent_spec_id) / "components" / component_id
    
    def specification_exists(self, spec_id: str) -> bool:
        """Check if a specification exists."""
        spec_path = self.get_specification_path(spec_id)
        return spec_path.exists() and (spec_path / "metadata.json").exists()
    
    def component_exists(self, parent_spec_id: str, component_id: str) -> bool:
        """Check if a component exists."""
        component_path = self.get_component_path(parent_spec_id, component_id)
        return component_path.exists() and (component_path / "metadata.json").exists()
    
    def list_specifications(self) -> List[str]:
        """List all specification IDs."""
        if not self.specs_dir.exists():
            return []
        
        specs = []
        for spec_dir in self.specs_dir.iterdir():
            if spec_dir.is_dir() and (spec_dir / "metadata.json").exists():
                specs.append(spec_dir.name)
        return sorted(specs)
    
    def list_components(self, parent_spec_id: str) -> List[str]:
        """List all component IDs for a parent specification."""
        components_dir = self.get_specification_path(parent_spec_id) / "components"
        if not components_dir.exists():
            return []
        
        components = []
        for comp_dir in components_dir.iterdir():
            if comp_dir.is_dir() and (comp_dir / "metadata.json").exists():
                components.append(comp_dir.name)
        return sorted(components)
    
    def load_specification_metadata(self, spec_id: str) -> CompositeSpecificationMetadata:
        """Load metadata for a specification."""
        if not self.specification_exists(spec_id):
            raise ComponentNotFoundError(f"Specification '{spec_id}' not found")
        
        metadata_path = self.get_specification_path(spec_id) / "metadata.json"
        metadata = load_specification_metadata(metadata_path)
        
        if not isinstance(metadata, CompositeSpecificationMetadata):
            raise InvalidHierarchyError(f"Specification '{spec_id}' is not a composite specification")
        
        return metadata
    
    def load_component_metadata(self, parent_spec_id: str, component_id: str) -> ComponentSpecificationMetadata:
        """Load metadata for a component."""
        if not self.component_exists(parent_spec_id, component_id):
            raise ComponentNotFoundError(f"Component '{component_id}' not found in '{parent_spec_id}'")
        
        metadata_path = self.get_component_path(parent_spec_id, component_id) / "metadata.json"
        metadata = load_specification_metadata(metadata_path)
        
        if not isinstance(metadata, ComponentSpecificationMetadata):
            raise InvalidHierarchyError(f"Component '{component_id}' is not a valid component")
        
        return metadata
    
    def save_specification_metadata(self, spec_id: str, metadata: CompositeSpecificationMetadata) -> None:
        """Save metadata for a specification."""
        metadata_path = self.get_specification_path(spec_id) / "metadata.json"
        metadata.updated_at = datetime.utcnow()
        save_specification_metadata(metadata, metadata_path)
    
    def save_component_metadata(self, parent_spec_id: str, component_id: str, metadata: ComponentSpecificationMetadata) -> None:
        """Save metadata for a component."""
        metadata_path = self.get_component_path(parent_spec_id, component_id) / "metadata.json"
        metadata.updated_at = datetime.utcnow()
        save_specification_metadata(metadata, metadata_path)
    
    def create_specification(
        self,
        spec_id: str,
        name: str,
        assembly_strategy: AssemblyStrategy = AssemblyStrategy.INTEGRATED
    ) -> CompositeSpecificationMetadata:
        """Create a new composite specification."""
        if self.specification_exists(spec_id):
            raise HierarchicalSpecificationError(f"Specification '{spec_id}' already exists")
        
        # Create directory structure
        spec_path = self.get_specification_path(spec_id)
        spec_path.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        (spec_path / "components").mkdir(exist_ok=True)
        
        # Create metadata
        metadata = CompositeSpecificationMetadata(
            id=spec_id,
            name=name,
            assembly_strategy=assembly_strategy
        )
        
        # Save metadata
        self.save_specification_metadata(spec_id, metadata)
        
        # Create empty spec file
        spec_file = spec_path / "spec.md"
        spec_file.write_text(f"# {name}\n\n*This specification is ready for development.*\n")
        
        # Create empty conversation file
        conversation_file = spec_path / "conversation.json"
        conversation_file.write_text("[]")
        
        return metadata
    
    def create_component(
        self,
        parent_spec_id: str,
        component_id: str,
        component_name: str,
        description: str = "",
        sync_strategy: SyncStrategy = SyncStrategy.BIDIRECTIONAL
    ) -> ComponentSpecificationMetadata:
        """Create a new component under a parent specification."""
        if not self.specification_exists(parent_spec_id):
            raise ComponentNotFoundError(f"Parent specification '{parent_spec_id}' not found")
        
        if self.component_exists(parent_spec_id, component_id):
            raise HierarchicalSpecificationError(f"Component '{component_id}' already exists in '{parent_spec_id}'")
        
        # Create component directory
        component_path = self.get_component_path(parent_spec_id, component_id)
        component_path.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        
        # Create component metadata
        component_metadata = ComponentSpecificationMetadata(
            id=component_id,
            name=component_name,
            parent_spec=parent_spec_id,
            sync_strategy=sync_strategy
        )
        
        # Save component metadata
        self.save_component_metadata(parent_spec_id, component_id, component_metadata)
        
        # Create component spec file
        spec_file = component_path / "spec.md"
        initial_content = f"# {component_name}\n\n{description}\n\n*This component is ready for development.*\n"
        spec_file.write_text(initial_content)
        
        # Create component conversation file
        conversation_file = component_path / "conversation.json"
        conversation_file.write_text("[]")
        
        # Create synchronization metadata
        sync_metadata = SynchronizationMetadata(
            component_id=component_id,
            parent_spec=parent_spec_id
        )
        sync_file = component_path / "sync_metadata.json"
        save_synchronization_metadata(sync_metadata, sync_file)
        
        # Update parent metadata to include new component
        parent_metadata = self.load_specification_metadata(parent_spec_id)
        component_ref = ComponentReference(
            id=component_id,
            name=component_name
        )
        parent_metadata.components.append(component_ref)
        self.save_specification_metadata(parent_spec_id, parent_metadata)
        
        return component_metadata
    
    def get_specification_hierarchy(self, spec_id: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Get the complete hierarchy for a specification."""
        if not self.specification_exists(spec_id):
            raise ComponentNotFoundError(f"Specification '{spec_id}' not found")
        
        parent_metadata = self.load_specification_metadata(spec_id)
        
        hierarchy = {
            "id": parent_metadata.id,
            "name": parent_metadata.name,
            "type": parent_metadata.type.value,
            "ready_for_contracts": parent_metadata.ready_for_contracts,
            "created_at": parent_metadata.created_at.isoformat(),
            "updated_at": parent_metadata.updated_at.isoformat(),
            "assembly_strategy": parent_metadata.assembly_strategy.value,
            "components": []
        }
        
        # Add component details
        for component_ref in parent_metadata.components:
            try:
                component_metadata = self.load_component_metadata(spec_id, component_ref.id)
                component_info = {
                    "id": component_metadata.id,
                    "name": component_metadata.name,
                    "ready_for_contracts": component_metadata.ready_for_contracts,
                    "created_at": component_metadata.created_at.isoformat(),
                    "updated_at": component_metadata.updated_at.isoformat(),
                    "last_sync": component_ref.last_sync.isoformat() if component_ref.last_sync else None,
                    "sync_strategy": component_metadata.sync_strategy.value,
                    "affects_siblings": component_metadata.affects_siblings
                }
                hierarchy["components"].append(component_info)
            except ComponentNotFoundError:
                # Component reference exists but component is missing - mark as broken
                hierarchy["components"].append({
                    "id": component_ref.id,
                    "name": component_ref.name,
                    "status": "missing",
                    "error": "Component directory not found"
                })
        
        # Add dependency information
        hierarchy["dependencies"] = parent_metadata.dependencies
        
        return hierarchy
    
    def validate_dependencies(self, spec_id: str) -> List[str]:
        """Validate component dependencies and return any issues."""
        if not self.specification_exists(spec_id):
            raise ComponentNotFoundError(f"Specification '{spec_id}' not found")
        
        parent_metadata = self.load_specification_metadata(spec_id)
        issues = []
        
        # Get all component IDs
        component_ids = {comp.id for comp in parent_metadata.components}
        
        # Check dependencies
        for component_id, deps in parent_metadata.dependencies.items():
            if component_id not in component_ids:
                issues.append(f"Dependency source '{component_id}' does not exist")
            
            for dep in deps:
                if dep not in component_ids:
                    issues.append(f"Dependency target '{dep}' for component '{component_id}' does not exist")
        
        # Check for circular dependencies
        try:
            self._detect_circular_dependencies(parent_metadata.dependencies)
        except CircularDependencyError as e:
            issues.append(str(e))
        
        return issues
    
    def _detect_circular_dependencies(self, dependencies: Dict[str, List[str]]) -> None:
        """Detect circular dependencies in component graph."""
        # Use DFS to detect cycles
        visited = set()
        rec_stack = set()
        
        def dfs(node: str) -> bool:
            if node in rec_stack:
                return True  # Cycle detected
            if node in visited:
                return False
            
            visited.add(node)
            rec_stack.add(node)
            
            for neighbor in dependencies.get(node, []):
                if dfs(neighbor):
                    return True
            
            rec_stack.remove(node)
            return False
        
        for node in dependencies:
            if node not in visited:
                if dfs(node):
                    raise CircularDependencyError(f"Circular dependency detected involving component '{node}'")
    
    def get_component_dependencies(self, spec_id: str, component_id: str) -> Tuple[List[str], List[str]]:
        """Get dependencies for a component (dependencies, dependents)."""
        if not self.specification_exists(spec_id):
            raise ComponentNotFoundError(f"Specification '{spec_id}' not found")
        
        parent_metadata = self.load_specification_metadata(spec_id)
        
        # Get direct dependencies
        dependencies = parent_metadata.dependencies.get(component_id, [])
        
        # Get dependents (components that depend on this one)
        dependents = []
        for comp_id, deps in parent_metadata.dependencies.items():
            if component_id in deps:
                dependents.append(comp_id)
        
        return dependencies, dependents
    
    def add_component_dependency(self, spec_id: str, component_id: str, dependency_id: str) -> None:
        """Add a dependency between components."""
        if not self.specification_exists(spec_id):
            raise ComponentNotFoundError(f"Specification '{spec_id}' not found")
        
        if not self.component_exists(spec_id, component_id):
            raise ComponentNotFoundError(f"Component '{component_id}' not found")
        
        if not self.component_exists(spec_id, dependency_id):
            raise ComponentNotFoundError(f"Dependency component '{dependency_id}' not found")
        
        parent_metadata = self.load_specification_metadata(spec_id)
        
        # Add dependency
        if component_id not in parent_metadata.dependencies:
            parent_metadata.dependencies[component_id] = []
        
        if dependency_id not in parent_metadata.dependencies[component_id]:
            parent_metadata.dependencies[component_id].append(dependency_id)
        
        # Validate no circular dependencies
        try:
            self._detect_circular_dependencies(parent_metadata.dependencies)
        except CircularDependencyError as e:
            # Remove the dependency we just added
            parent_metadata.dependencies[component_id].remove(dependency_id)
            raise e
        
        # Save updated metadata
        self.save_specification_metadata(spec_id, parent_metadata)
    
    def remove_component_dependency(self, spec_id: str, component_id: str, dependency_id: str) -> None:
        """Remove a dependency between components."""
        if not self.specification_exists(spec_id):
            raise ComponentNotFoundError(f"Specification '{spec_id}' not found")
        
        parent_metadata = self.load_specification_metadata(spec_id)
        
        if component_id in parent_metadata.dependencies:
            if dependency_id in parent_metadata.dependencies[component_id]:
                parent_metadata.dependencies[component_id].remove(dependency_id)
                
                # Clean up empty dependency lists
                if not parent_metadata.dependencies[component_id]:
                    del parent_metadata.dependencies[component_id]
        
        # Save updated metadata
        self.save_specification_metadata(spec_id, parent_metadata)
    
    def create_backup(self, spec_id: str, backup_type: str = "spec") -> Path:
        """Create a backup of a specification or component."""
        timestamp = datetime.utcnow().strftime("%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
        backup_name = f"{spec_id}_{timestamp}"
        
        if backup_type == "spec":
            backup_dir = self.backups_dir / "specifications" / backup_name
            source_dir = self.get_specification_path(spec_id)
        else:
            backup_dir = self.backups_dir / "components" / backup_name
            # For components, spec_id should be "parent_spec/component_id"
            parent_spec, component_id = spec_id.split("/", 1)
            source_dir = self.get_component_path(parent_spec, component_id)
        
        backup_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        
        if source_dir.exists():
            shutil.copytree(source_dir, backup_dir / source_dir.name)
        
        # Create backup metadata
        backup_metadata = {
            "backup_id": backup_name,
            "original_path": str(source_dir),
            "backup_type": backup_type,
            "created_at": datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
            "source_spec": spec_id
        }
        
        with open(backup_dir / "backup_metadata.json", "w") as f:
            json.dump(backup_metadata, f, indent=2)
        
        return backup_dir
    
    def analyze_deletion_impact(self, spec_id: str, component_id: Optional[str] = None) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Analyze the impact of deleting a specification or component."""
        if component_id is None:
            # Analyzing specification deletion
            if not self.specification_exists(spec_id):
                raise ComponentNotFoundError(f"Specification '{spec_id}' not found")
            
            parent_metadata = self.load_specification_metadata(spec_id)
            
            impact = {
                "type": "specification",
                "target": spec_id,
                "components_affected": len(parent_metadata.components),
                "components": [comp.id for comp in parent_metadata.components],
                "contracts_lost": 0,  # Would need to scan for contracts
                "external_dependencies": [],  # Would need to scan other specs
                "cascade_deletion": True
            }
            
            # Count contracts that would be lost
            for comp in parent_metadata.components:
                contracts_file = self.get_component_path(spec_id, comp.id) / "contracts.json"
                if contracts_file.exists():
                    try:
                        with open(contracts_file) as f:
                            contracts = json.load(f)
                            impact["contracts_lost"] += len(contracts)
                    except (json.JSONDecodeError, KeyError):
                        pass
            
            return impact
        
        else:
            # Analyzing component deletion
            if not self.component_exists(spec_id, component_id):
                raise ComponentNotFoundError(f"Component '{component_id}' not found in '{spec_id}'")
            
            parent_metadata = self.load_specification_metadata(spec_id)
            dependencies, dependents = self.get_component_dependencies(spec_id, component_id)
            
            impact = {
                "type": "component",
                "target": f"{spec_id}/{component_id}",
                "parent_spec": spec_id,
                "dependencies": dependencies,
                "dependents": dependents,
                "broken_dependencies": len(dependents),
                "contracts_lost": 0,
                "parent_sections_affected": [],
                "cascade_deletion": False
            }
            
            # Count component contracts
            contracts_file = self.get_component_path(spec_id, component_id) / "contracts.json"
            if contracts_file.exists():
                try:
                    with open(contracts_file) as f:
                        contracts = json.load(f)
                        impact["contracts_lost"] = len(contracts)
                except (json.JSONDecodeError, KeyError):
                    pass
            
            # Get component metadata for parent sections
            try:
                component_metadata = self.load_component_metadata(spec_id, component_id)
                impact["parent_sections_affected"] = component_metadata.parent_sections
            except ComponentNotFoundError:
                pass
            
            return impact
    
    def delete_specification(self, spec_id: str, create_backup: bool = True) -> Optional[Path]:
        """Delete a complete specification with all components."""
        if not self.specification_exists(spec_id):
            raise ComponentNotFoundError(f"Specification '{spec_id}' not found")
        
        backup_path = None
        if create_backup:
            backup_path = self.create_backup(spec_id, "spec")
        
        # Delete the specification directory
        spec_path = self.get_specification_path(spec_id)
        if spec_path.exists():
            shutil.rmtree(spec_path)
        
        return backup_path
    
    def delete_component(self, parent_spec_id: str, component_id: str, create_backup: bool = True) -> Optional[Path]:
        """Delete a component and update parent metadata."""
        if not self.component_exists(parent_spec_id, component_id):
            raise ComponentNotFoundError(f"Component '{component_id}' not found in '{parent_spec_id}'")
        
        backup_path = None
        if create_backup:
            backup_path = self.create_backup(f"{parent_spec_id}/{component_id}", "component")
        
        # Remove component from parent metadata
        parent_metadata = self.load_specification_metadata(parent_spec_id)
        parent_metadata.components = [
            comp for comp in parent_metadata.components 
            if comp.id != component_id
        ]
        
        # Remove component dependencies
        if component_id in parent_metadata.dependencies:
            del parent_metadata.dependencies[component_id]
        
        # Remove dependencies on this component from other components
        for comp_id, deps in parent_metadata.dependencies.items():
            if component_id in deps:
                deps.remove(component_id)
        
        # Clean up empty dependency lists
        parent_metadata.dependencies = {
            comp_id: deps for comp_id, deps in parent_metadata.dependencies.items()
            if deps
        }
        
        # Save updated parent metadata
        self.save_specification_metadata(parent_spec_id, parent_metadata)
        
        # Delete component directory
        component_path = self.get_component_path(parent_spec_id, component_id)
        if component_path.exists():
            shutil.rmtree(component_path)
        
        return backup_path
    
    def list_backups(self, backup_type: Optional[str] = None) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
        """List available backups."""
        backups = []
        
        backup_types = ["specifications", "components"] if backup_type is None else [backup_type]
        
        for btype in backup_types:
            backup_dir = self.backups_dir / btype
            if not backup_dir.exists():
                continue
            
            for backup_folder in backup_dir.iterdir():
                if backup_folder.is_dir():
                    metadata_file = backup_folder / "backup_metadata.json"
                    if metadata_file.exists():
                        try:
                            with open(metadata_file) as f:
                                metadata = json.load(f)
                                backups.append(metadata)
                        except (json.JSONDecodeError, KeyError):
                            # Skip invalid backup metadata
                            continue
        
        return sorted(backups, key=lambda x: x["created_at"], reverse=True)
    
    def restore_backup(self, backup_id: str, target_location: Optional[str] = None) -> bool:
        """Restore a specification or component from backup."""
        # Find backup
        backup_metadata = None
        backup_path = None
        
        for backup_type in ["specifications", "components"]:
            potential_path = self.backups_dir / backup_type / backup_id
            metadata_file = potential_path / "backup_metadata.json"
            
            if metadata_file.exists():
                with open(metadata_file) as f:
                    backup_metadata = json.load(f)
                    backup_path = potential_path
                break
        
        if not backup_metadata:
            raise ComponentNotFoundError(f"Backup '{backup_id}' not found")
        
        # Determine restore location
        if target_location:
            restore_path = Path(target_location)
        else:
            restore_path = Path(backup_metadata["original_path"])
        
        # Find the actual backup content (should be a subdirectory)
        backup_content = None
        for item in backup_path.iterdir():
            if item.is_dir() and item.name != "backup_metadata.json":
                backup_content = item
                break
        
        if not backup_content:
            raise HierarchicalSpecificationError(f"Backup content not found in '{backup_id}'")
        
        # Restore the backup
        if restore_path.exists():
            shutil.rmtree(restore_path)
        
        shutil.copytree(backup_content, restore_path)
        
        return True

"""
CLI commands for hierarchical specification management.

This module provides the command-line interface for creating, managing,
and synchronizing hierarchical specifications.
"""

import json
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Optional, List

import typer
from rich.console import Console
from rich.table import Table
from rich.tree import Tree
from rich.panel import Panel
from rich.prompt import Confirm

from .hierarchical_specs import (
    HierarchicalSpecificationManager,
    HierarchicalSpecificationError,
    ComponentNotFoundError,
    InvalidHierarchyError,
    CircularDependencyError,
)
from .specification_models import AssemblyStrategy, SyncStrategy

console = Console()
error_console = Console(stderr=True)

# Create the hierarchical CLI app
hierarchical_app = typer.Typer(
    name="hierarchical",
    help="Hierarchical specification management commands",
    add_completion=False,
)


def get_manager() -> HierarchicalSpecificationManager:
    """Get the hierarchical specification manager for current directory."""
    return HierarchicalSpecificationManager(Path.cwd())


@hierarchical_app.command("create-spec")
def create_specification(
    spec_id: str = typer.Argument(..., help="Unique specification identifier"),
    name: str = typer.Argument(..., help="Human-readable specification name"),
    assembly_strategy: str = typer.Option(
        "integrated",
        "--assembly",
        help="Contract assembly strategy (integrated, separate, hierarchical)"
    ),
):
    """Create a new composite specification."""
    try:
        manager = get_manager()
        
        # Validate assembly strategy
        try:
            strategy = AssemblyStrategy(assembly_strategy)
        except ValueError:
            error_console.print(f"[red]Invalid assembly strategy: {assembly_strategy}[/red]")
            error_console.print(f"[yellow]Valid options: {', '.join([s.value for s in AssemblyStrategy])}[/yellow]")
            raise typer.Exit(1)
        
        metadata = manager.create_specification(spec_id, name, strategy)
        
        console.print(f"[green]✓[/green] Created specification '{spec_id}'")
        console.print(f"  Name: {metadata.name}")
        console.print(f"  Assembly Strategy: {metadata.assembly_strategy.value}")
        console.print(f"  Path: {manager.get_specification_path(spec_id)}")
        
    except HierarchicalSpecificationError as e:
        error_console.print(f"[red]Error: {e}[/red]")
        raise typer.Exit(1)


@hierarchical_app.command("create-component")
def create_component(
    parent_spec_id: str = typer.Argument(..., help="Parent specification ID"),
    component_id: str = typer.Argument(..., help="Component identifier"),
    component_name: str = typer.Argument(..., help="Component name"),
    description: str = typer.Option("", "--description", "-d", help="Component description"),
    sync_strategy: str = typer.Option(
        "bidirectional",
        "--sync",
        help="Synchronization strategy (bidirectional, component_to_parent, parent_to_component, manual)"
    ),
):
    """Create a new component under a parent specification."""
    try:
        manager = get_manager()
        
        # Validate sync strategy
        try:
            strategy = SyncStrategy(sync_strategy)
        except ValueError:
            error_console.print(f"[red]Invalid sync strategy: {sync_strategy}[/red]")
            error_console.print(f"[yellow]Valid options: {', '.join([s.value for s in SyncStrategy])}[/yellow]")
            raise typer.Exit(1)
        
        metadata = manager.create_component(
            parent_spec_id, component_id, component_name, description, strategy
        )
        
        console.print(f"[green]✓[/green] Created component '{component_id}' in '{parent_spec_id}'")
        console.print(f"  Name: {metadata.name}")
        console.print(f"  Sync Strategy: {metadata.sync_strategy.value}")
        console.print(f"  Path: {manager.get_component_path(parent_spec_id, component_id)}")
        
    except HierarchicalSpecificationError as e:
        error_console.print(f"[red]Error: {e}[/red]")
        raise typer.Exit(1)


@hierarchical_app.command("show-hierarchy")
def show_hierarchy(
    spec_id: str = typer.Argument(..., help="Specification ID"),
):
    """Show the complete hierarchy for a specification."""
    try:
        manager = get_manager()
        hierarchy = manager.get_specification_hierarchy(spec_id)
        
        # Create a tree visualization
        tree = Tree(f"[bold blue]{hierarchy['name']}[/bold blue] ({hierarchy['id']})")
        tree.add(f"[dim]Type:[/dim] {hierarchy['type']}")
        tree.add(f"[dim]Ready for contracts:[/dim] {'✓' if hierarchy['ready_for_contracts'] else '✗'}")
        tree.add(f"[dim]Assembly:[/dim] {hierarchy['assembly_strategy']}")
        tree.add(f"[dim]Created:[/dim] {hierarchy['created_at']}")
        tree.add(f"[dim]Updated:[/dim] {hierarchy['updated_at']}")
        
        if hierarchy['components']:
            components_node = tree.add("[bold green]Components[/bold green]")
            
            for component in hierarchy['components']:
                if 'error' in component:
                    comp_node = components_node.add(f"[red]{component['name']} ({component['id']}) - {component['error']}[/red]")
                else:
                    status = "✓" if component['ready_for_contracts'] else "✗"
                    comp_node = components_node.add(f"[cyan]{component['name']}[/cyan] ({component['id']}) {status}")
                    comp_node.add(f"[dim]Sync:[/dim] {component['sync_strategy']}")
                    comp_node.add(f"[dim]Last sync:[/dim] {component['last_sync'] or 'Never'}")
                    
                    if component['affects_siblings']:
                        comp_node.add(f"[dim]Affects:[/dim] {', '.join(component['affects_siblings'])}")
        
        if hierarchy.get('dependencies'):
            deps_node = tree.add("[bold yellow]Dependencies[/bold yellow]")
            for comp_id, deps in hierarchy['dependencies'].items():
                deps_node.add(f"{comp_id} → {', '.join(deps)}")
        
        console.print(tree)
        
    except HierarchicalSpecificationError as e:
        error_console.print(f"[red]Error: {e}[/red]")
        raise typer.Exit(1)


@hierarchical_app.command("list-specs")
def list_specifications():
    """List all specifications."""
    try:
        manager = get_manager()
        specs = manager.list_specifications()
        
        if not specs:
            console.print("[yellow]No specifications found.[/yellow]")
            return
        
        table = Table(title="Specifications")
        table.add_column("ID", style="cyan")
        table.add_column("Name", style="green")
        table.add_column("Type", style="blue")
        table.add_column("Components", justify="right")
        table.add_column("Ready", justify="center")
        
        for spec_id in specs:
            try:
                metadata = manager.load_specification_metadata(spec_id)
                table.add_row(
                    spec_id,
                    metadata.name,
                    metadata.type.value,
                    str(len(metadata.components)),
                    "✓" if metadata.ready_for_contracts else "✗"
                )
            except Exception as e:
                table.add_row(spec_id, "[red]Error loading[/red]", "unknown", "?", "?")
        
        console.print(table)
        
    except HierarchicalSpecificationError as e:
        error_console.print(f"[red]Error: {e}[/red]")
        raise typer.Exit(1)


@hierarchical_app.command("validate-dependencies")
def validate_dependencies(
    spec_id: str = typer.Argument(..., help="Specification ID"),
):
    """Validate component dependencies for a specification."""
    try:
        manager = get_manager()
        issues = manager.validate_dependencies(spec_id)
        
        if not issues:
            console.print(f"[green]✓[/green] No dependency issues found in '{spec_id}'")
        else:
            console.print(f"[red]Found {len(issues)} dependency issues in '{spec_id}':[/red]")
            for issue in issues:
                console.print(f"  • {issue}")
        
    except HierarchicalSpecificationError as e:
        error_console.print(f"[red]Error: {e}[/red]")
        raise typer.Exit(1)


@hierarchical_app.command("add-dependency")
def add_dependency(
    spec_id: str = typer.Argument(..., help="Specification ID"),
    component_id: str = typer.Argument(..., help="Component that depends on another"),
    dependency_id: str = typer.Argument(..., help="Component that is depended upon"),
):
    """Add a dependency between components."""
    try:
        manager = get_manager()
        manager.add_component_dependency(spec_id, component_id, dependency_id)
        
        console.print(f"[green]✓[/green] Added dependency: {component_id} → {dependency_id}")
        
    except (HierarchicalSpecificationError, CircularDependencyError) as e:
        error_console.print(f"[red]Error: {e}[/red]")
        raise typer.Exit(1)


@hierarchical_app.command("remove-dependency")
def remove_dependency(
    spec_id: str = typer.Argument(..., help="Specification ID"),
    component_id: str = typer.Argument(..., help="Component to remove dependency from"),
    dependency_id: str = typer.Argument(..., help="Dependency to remove"),
):
    """Remove a dependency between components."""
    try:
        manager = get_manager()
        manager.remove_component_dependency(spec_id, component_id, dependency_id)
        
        console.print(f"[green]✓[/green] Removed dependency: {component_id} → {dependency_id}")
        
    except HierarchicalSpecificationError as e:
        error_console.print(f"[red]Error: {e}[/red]")
        raise typer.Exit(1)


@hierarchical_app.command("analyze-deletion")
def analyze_deletion(
    spec_id: str = typer.Argument(..., help="Specification ID"),
    component_id: Optional[str] = typer.Argument(None, help="Component ID (if deleting component)"),
):
    """Analyze the impact of deleting a specification or component."""
    try:
        manager = get_manager()
        impact = manager.analyze_deletion_impact(spec_id, component_id)
        
        if impact["type"] == "specification":
            panel_title = f"Deletion Impact: Specification '{spec_id}'"
            content = f"""[bold red]⚠️  WARNING: This will delete the entire specification![/bold red]

[bold]Components affected:[/bold] {impact['components_affected']}
[bold]Components:[/bold] {', '.join(impact['components']) if impact['components'] else 'None'}
[bold]Contracts lost:[/bold] {impact['contracts_lost']}
[bold]Cascade deletion:[/bold] {'Yes' if impact['cascade_deletion'] else 'No'}

[yellow]All component data will be permanently lost![/yellow]"""
        
        else:
            panel_title = f"Deletion Impact: Component '{component_id}' in '{spec_id}'"
            content = f"""[bold yellow]⚠️  WARNING: This will delete the component![/bold yellow]

[bold]Dependencies:[/bold] {', '.join(impact['dependencies']) if impact['dependencies'] else 'None'}
[bold]Dependents:[/bold] {', '.join(impact['dependents']) if impact['dependents'] else 'None'}
[bold]Broken dependencies:[/bold] {impact['broken_dependencies']}
[bold]Contracts lost:[/bold] {impact['contracts_lost']}
[bold]Parent sections affected:[/bold] {len(impact['parent_sections_affected'])}

[green]Parent contracts will remain unchanged.[/green]"""
        
        console.print(Panel(content, title=panel_title, border_style="red"))
        
    except HierarchicalSpecificationError as e:
        error_console.print(f"[red]Error: {e}[/red]")
        raise typer.Exit(1)


@hierarchical_app.command("delete-spec")
def delete_specification(
    spec_id: str = typer.Argument(..., help="Specification ID"),
    force: bool = typer.Option(False, "--force", help="Skip confirmation prompt"),
    backup: bool = typer.Option(True, "--backup/--no-backup", help="Create backup before deletion"),
):
    """Delete a specification with safety checks and optional backup."""
    try:
        manager = get_manager()
        
        # Analyze impact
        impact = manager.analyze_deletion_impact(spec_id)
        
        # Show impact and get confirmation
        if not force:
            console.print(f"[bold red]⚠️  WARNING: This will delete specification '{spec_id}'[/bold red]")
            console.print(f"Components affected: {impact['components_affected']}")
            console.print(f"Contracts lost: {impact['contracts_lost']}")
            
            if backup:
                console.print("[green]A backup will be created before deletion.[/green]")
            else:
                console.print("[red]No backup will be created![/red]")
            
            if not Confirm.ask("Continue with deletion?"):
                console.print("Deletion cancelled.")
                return
        
        # Create backup and delete
        backup_path = manager.delete_specification(spec_id, backup)
        
        console.print(f"[green]✓[/green] Deleted specification '{spec_id}'")
        if backup_path:
            console.print(f"[blue]Backup created at:[/blue] {backup_path}")
        
    except HierarchicalSpecificationError as e:
        error_console.print(f"[red]Error: {e}[/red]")
        raise typer.Exit(1)


@hierarchical_app.command("delete-component")
def delete_component(
    parent_spec_id: str = typer.Argument(..., help="Parent specification ID"),
    component_id: str = typer.Argument(..., help="Component ID"),
    force: bool = typer.Option(False, "--force", help="Skip confirmation prompt"),
    backup: bool = typer.Option(True, "--backup/--no-backup", help="Create backup before deletion"),
):
    """Delete a component and sync changes to parent specification."""
    try:
        manager = get_manager()
        
        # Analyze impact
        impact = manager.analyze_deletion_impact(parent_spec_id, component_id)
        
        # Show impact and get confirmation
        if not force:
            console.print(f"[bold yellow]⚠️  WARNING: This will delete component '{component_id}' from '{parent_spec_id}'[/bold yellow]")
            console.print(f"Dependencies: {', '.join(impact['dependencies']) if impact['dependencies'] else 'None'}")
            console.print(f"Dependents: {', '.join(impact['dependents']) if impact['dependents'] else 'None'}")
            console.print(f"Broken dependencies: {impact['broken_dependencies']}")
            console.print(f"Contracts lost: {impact['contracts_lost']}")
            console.print("[green]Parent contracts will remain unchanged.[/green]")
            
            if backup:
                console.print("[green]A backup will be created before deletion.[/green]")
            else:
                console.print("[red]No backup will be created![/red]")
            
            if not Confirm.ask("Continue with deletion?"):
                console.print("Deletion cancelled.")
                return
        
        # Create backup and delete
        backup_path = manager.delete_component(parent_spec_id, component_id, backup)
        
        console.print(f"[green]✓[/green] Deleted component '{component_id}' from '{parent_spec_id}'")
        if backup_path:
            console.print(f"[blue]Backup created at:[/blue] {backup_path}")
        
        console.print("[yellow]Note: Parent specification may need manual synchronization.[/yellow]")
        
    except HierarchicalSpecificationError as e:
        error_console.print(f"[red]Error: {e}[/red]")
        raise typer.Exit(1)


@hierarchical_app.command("list-backups")
def list_backups(
    backup_type: Optional[str] = typer.Option(None, "--type", help="Filter by backup type (specifications, components)"),
):
    """List available backups."""
    try:
        manager = get_manager()
        backups = manager.list_backups(backup_type)
        
        if not backups:
            console.print("[yellow]No backups found.[/yellow]")
            return
        
        table = Table(title="Available Backups")
        table.add_column("Backup ID", style="cyan")
        table.add_column("Type", style="blue")
        table.add_column("Source", style="green")
        table.add_column("Created", style="yellow")
        
        for backup in backups:
            table.add_row(
                backup["backup_id"],
                backup["backup_type"],
                backup["source_spec"],
                backup["created_at"][:19]  # Remove microseconds
            )
        
        console.print(table)
        
    except HierarchicalSpecificationError as e:
        error_console.print(f"[red]Error: {e}[/red]")
        raise typer.Exit(1)


@hierarchical_app.command("restore-backup")
def restore_backup(
    backup_id: str = typer.Argument(..., help="Backup ID to restore"),
    target_location: Optional[str] = typer.Option(None, "--target", help="Target location (default: original location)"),
):
    """Restore a specification or component from backup."""
    try:
        manager = get_manager()
        
        success = manager.restore_backup(backup_id, target_location)
        
        if success:
            console.print(f"[green]✓[/green] Successfully restored backup '{backup_id}'")
            if target_location:
                console.print(f"[blue]Restored to:[/blue] {target_location}")
        else:
            error_console.print(f"[red]Failed to restore backup '{backup_id}'[/red]")
            raise typer.Exit(1)
        
    except HierarchicalSpecificationError as e:
        error_console.print(f"[red]Error: {e}[/red]")
        raise typer.Exit(1)

=======
def get_hierarchical_manager() -> HierarchicalSpecificationManager:
    """Get hierarchical specification manager for current project."""
    return HierarchicalSpecificationManager(Path.cwd())


@app.get("/specification/{spec_id}/hierarchy")
async def get_specification_hierarchy(
    spec_id: str,
    api_key: str = Depends(verify_api_key)
):
    """Get complete specification hierarchy."""
    try:
        manager = get_hierarchical_manager()
        hierarchy = manager.get_specification_hierarchy(spec_id)
        return {"hierarchy": hierarchy}
    except HierarchicalSpecificationError as e:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=str(e))


@app.get("/specification/{spec_id}/sync-status")
async def get_sync_status(
    spec_id: str,
    api_key: str = Depends(verify_api_key)
):
    """Get synchronization status for specification family."""
    try:
        manager = get_hierarchical_manager()
        
        if not manager.specification_exists(spec_id):
            raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=f"Specification '{spec_id}' not found")
        
        hierarchy = manager.get_specification_hierarchy(spec_id)
        
        # Calculate sync status
        sync_status = {
            "specification_id": spec_id,
            "total_components": len(hierarchy["components"]),
            "components_ready": sum(1 for c in hierarchy["components"] if c.get("ready_for_contracts", False)),
            "last_sync": None,
            "pending_syncs": [],
            "sync_issues": []
        }
        
        # Find most recent sync
        for component in hierarchy["components"]:
            if component.get("last_sync"):
                if not sync_status["last_sync"] or component["last_sync"] > sync_status["last_sync"]:
                    sync_status["last_sync"] = component["last_sync"]
        
        # Check for dependency issues
        issues = manager.validate_dependencies(spec_id)
        sync_status["sync_issues"] = issues
        
        return sync_status
        
    except HierarchicalSpecificationError as e:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=str(e))


@app.post("/sync-component")
async def sync_component(
    parent_spec_id: str,
    component_id: str,
    force: bool = False,
    api_key: str = Depends(verify_api_key)
):
    """Manually trigger component synchronization."""
    try:
        manager = get_hierarchical_manager()
        
        if not manager.component_exists(parent_spec_id, component_id):
            raise HTTPException(
                status_code=404, 
                detail=f"Component '{component_id}' not found in '{parent_spec_id}'"
            )
        
        # For now, return a placeholder response
        # TODO: Implement actual synchronization logic in Phase 2
        return {
            "message": f"Sync triggered for component '{component_id}' in '{parent_spec_id}'",
            "status": "pending",
            "force": force
        }
        
    except HierarchicalSpecificationError as e:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=str(e))


@app.delete("/specification/{spec_id}")
async def delete_specification(
    spec_id: str,
    force: bool = False,
    backup: bool = True,
    api_key: str = Depends(verify_api_key)
):
    """Delete a specification with safety checks and optional backup."""
    try:
        manager = get_hierarchical_manager()
        
        # Analyze impact
        impact = manager.analyze_deletion_impact(spec_id)
        
        if not force and impact["components_affected"] > 0:
            return {
                "status": "confirmation_required",
                "impact": impact,
                "message": "Deletion requires confirmation due to component impact"
            }
        
        # Perform deletion
        backup_path = manager.delete_specification(spec_id, backup)
        
        return {
            "status": "deleted",
            "specification_id": spec_id,
            "backup_path": str(backup_path) if backup_path else None,
            "impact": impact
        }
        
    except HierarchicalSpecificationError as e:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=str(e))


@app.delete("/specification/{parent_spec_id}/component/{component_id}")
async def delete_component(
    parent_spec_id: str,
    component_id: str,
    force: bool = False,
    backup: bool = True,
    api_key: str = Depends(verify_api_key)
):
    """Delete a component and sync changes to parent specification."""
    try:
        manager = get_hierarchical_manager()
        
        # Analyze impact
        impact = manager.analyze_deletion_impact(parent_spec_id, component_id)
        
        if not force and impact["broken_dependencies"] > 0:
            return {
                "status": "confirmation_required",
                "impact": impact,
                "message": "Deletion requires confirmation due to dependency impact"
            }
        
        # Perform deletion
        backup_path = manager.delete_component(parent_spec_id, component_id, backup)
        
        return {
            "status": "deleted",
            "parent_spec_id": parent_spec_id,
            "component_id": component_id,
            "backup_path": str(backup_path) if backup_path else None,
            "impact": impact,
            "sync_required": True
        }
        
    except HierarchicalSpecificationError as e:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=str(e))

"""
Test the hierarchical specification system.

This test verifies the basic functionality of the hierarchical specification
management system including creation, deletion, and dependency management.
"""

import tempfile
import shutil
from pathlib import Path

from src.axiomander.hierarchical_specs import HierarchicalSpecificationManager
from src.axiomander.specification_models import AssemblyStrategy, SyncStrategy


def test_hierarchical_specifications():
    """Test basic hierarchical specification functionality."""
    
    # Create temporary directory for testing
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as temp_dir:
        temp_path = Path(temp_dir)
        
        # Initialize manager
        manager = HierarchicalSpecificationManager(temp_path)
        
        print("Testing hierarchical specification system...")
        
        # Test 1: Create a composite specification
        print("1. Creating composite specification...")
        spec_metadata = manager.create_specification(
            "user_management",
            "User Management System",
            AssemblyStrategy.INTEGRATED
        )
        
        assert spec_metadata.id == "user_management"
        assert spec_metadata.name == "User Management System"
        assert manager.specification_exists("user_management")
        print("   ✓ Composite specification created")
        
        # Test 2: Create components
        print("2. Creating components...")
        auth_metadata = manager.create_component(
            "user_management",
            "authentication",
            "Authentication Flow",
            "Handles user login and authentication",
            SyncStrategy.BIDIRECTIONAL
        )
        
        authz_metadata = manager.create_component(
            "user_management",
            "authorization",
            "Authorization Rules",
            "Manages user permissions and access control"
        )
        
        assert manager.component_exists("user_management", "authentication")
        assert manager.component_exists("user_management", "authorization")
        print("   ✓ Components created")
        
        # Test 3: Add dependencies
        print("3. Adding component dependencies...")
        manager.add_component_dependency("user_management", "authorization", "authentication")
        
        dependencies, dependents = manager.get_component_dependencies("user_management", "authentication")
        assert "authorization" in dependents
        print("   ✓ Dependencies added")
        
        # Test 4: Get hierarchy
        print("4. Getting specification hierarchy...")
        hierarchy = manager.get_specification_hierarchy("user_management")
        
        assert hierarchy["id"] == "user_management"
        assert len(hierarchy["components"]) == 2
        assert "authorization" in hierarchy["dependencies"]
        print("   ✓ Hierarchy retrieved")
        
        # Test 5: Validate dependencies
        print("5. Validating dependencies...")
        issues = manager.validate_dependencies("user_management")
        assert len(issues) == 0
        print("   ✓ Dependencies validated")
        
        # Test 6: Analyze deletion impact
        print("6. Analyzing deletion impact...")
        impact = manager.analyze_deletion_impact("user_management", "authentication")
        
        assert impact["type"] == "component"
        assert impact["broken_dependencies"] == 1  # authorization depends on authentication
        print("   ✓ Deletion impact analyzed")
        
        # Test 7: Delete component with backup
        print("7. Deleting component...")
        backup_path = manager.delete_component("user_management", "authentication", create_backup=True)
        
        assert not manager.component_exists("user_management", "authentication")
        assert backup_path is not None
        assert backup_path.exists()
        print("   ✓ Component deleted with backup")
        
        # Test 8: List backups
        print("8. Listing backups...")
        backups = manager.list_backups("components")
        assert len(backups) >= 1
        print("   ✓ Backups listed")
        
        # Test 9: Restore backup
        print("9. Restoring backup...")
        backup_id = backups[0]["backup_id"]
        success = manager.restore_backup(backup_id)
        
        assert success
        assert manager.component_exists("user_management", "authentication")
        print("   ✓ Backup restored")
        
        # Test 10: Delete entire specification
        print("10. Deleting specification...")
        spec_backup_path = manager.delete_specification("user_management", create_backup=True)
        
        assert not manager.specification_exists("user_management")
        assert spec_backup_path is not None
        print("   ✓ Specification deleted")
        
        print("\n✅ All tests passed! Hierarchical specification system is working correctly.")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    test_hierarchical_specifications()

This implementation provides Phase 1 of the hierarchical specification system with:

1. **Enhanced Metadata System** - Complete data structures for parent/child relationships with proper type annotations and Pydantic models
2. **File System Extensions** - Support for nested component directories with validation and backup capabilities  
3. **Basic Decomposition** - Manual component creation with metadata linking and dependency management
4. **Deletion Logic** - Safe deletion with impact analysis, backup creation, and recovery capabilities
5. **CLI Commands** - Full command-line interface for managing hierarchical specifications
6. **API Endpoints** - REST endpoints for hierarchy management and deletion operations

The implementation follows all the conventions from CONVENTIONS.md including comprehensive error handling, detailed documentation, and proper separation of concerns.

#### In the interactive session I have two specs I need to delete but cannot. How can I?  

You can delete specifications using the hierarchical CLI commands that were implemented. Here are the commands you can use:

```bash
# First, analyze what will be deleted to see the impact
axiomander hier analyze-deletion <spec_id>

# Then delete the specification (with backup by default)
axiomander hier delete-spec <spec_id>

# Or delete without confirmation prompt
axiomander hier delete-spec <spec_id> --force

# Or delete without creating a backup (not recommended)
axiomander hier delete-spec <spec_id> --no-backup
```

To see what specifications you have available to delete:

```bash
# List all specifications
axiomander hier list-specs
```

If you want to delete both specifications, you can run the delete command twice:

```bash
axiomander hier delete-spec <first_spec_id>
axiomander hier delete-spec <second_spec_id>
```

The deletion commands include safety features:
- **Impact analysis** before deletion
- **Interactive confirmation** (unless you use `--force`)
- **Automatic backups** (unless you use `--no-backup`)
- **Dependency validation** to prevent breaking other specs

If you need to restore a deleted specification later, you can:

```bash
# List available backups
axiomander hier list-backups

# Restore a specific backup
axiomander hier restore-backup <backup_id>
```

    async def _run_sync_status(self, spec_id: str):
        """Get synchronization status for a specification."""
        try:
            response = await stream_sse_request(f"specification/{spec_id}/sync-status")
            
            if response.get("success"):
                sync_status = response.get("sync_status", {})
                
                console.print(f"\n[bold blue]Sync Status for {sync_status.get('name', spec_id)}[/bold blue]")
                console.print(f"Overall Status: {sync_status.get('overall_status', 'unknown')}")
                console.print(f"Total Components: {sync_status.get('total_components', 0)}")
                console.print(f"Components Ready: {sync_status.get('components_ready', 0)}")
                
                if sync_status.get('components'):
                    console.print("\n[bold green]Component Status:[/bold green]")
                    for comp in sync_status['components']:
                        status_icon = "✓" if comp['sync_status'] == 'synced' else "⚠️" if comp['sync_status'] == 'pending' else "❌"
                        console.print(f"  {status_icon} {comp['name']}: {comp['sync_status']}")
                        if comp.get('pending_changes', 0) > 0:
                            console.print(f"    Pending changes: {comp['pending_changes']}")
                
                if sync_status.get('conflicts'):
                    console.print("\n[bold red]Conflicts:[/bold red]")
                    for conflict in sync_status['conflicts']:
                        console.print(f"  • {conflict}")
            else:
                console.print(f"[red]Failed to get sync status: {response.get('error', 'Unknown error')}[/red]")
                
        except Exception as e:
            console.print(f"[red]Error getting sync status: {e}[/red]")

    async def _run_delete_spec(self, spec_id: str, force: bool = False):
        """Delete a specification with safety checks."""
        try:
            # First analyze deletion impact
            response = make_request("DELETE", f"specification/{spec_id}", {
                "force": False,  # Always analyze first
                "backup": True
            })
            
            if response.get("status") == "confirmation_required":
                impact = response.get("impact", {})
                
                console.print(f"\n[bold red]⚠️  WARNING: This will delete specification '{spec_id}'[/bold red]")
                
                if impact.get("type") == "specification":
                    console.print(f"Components affected: {impact.get('components_affected', 0)}")
                    console.print(f"Contracts lost: {impact.get('contracts_lost', 0)}")
                    
                    if impact.get('components'):
                        console.print(f"Components: {', '.join(impact['components'])}")
                
                console.print("[green]A backup will be created before deletion.[/green]")
                
                if not force:
                    from rich.prompt import Confirm
                    if not Confirm.ask("Continue with deletion?"):
                        console.print("Deletion cancelled.")
                        return
                
                # Proceed with deletion
                response = make_request("DELETE", f"specification/{spec_id}", {
                    "force": True,
                    "backup": True
                })
            
            if response.get("status") == "deleted":
                console.print(f"[green]✓[/green] Deleted specification '{spec_id}'")
                if response.get("backup_path"):
                    console.print(f"[blue]Backup created at:[/blue] {response['backup_path']}")
            else:
                console.print(f"[red]Failed to delete specification: {response.get('message', 'Unknown error')}[/red]")
                
        except Exception as e:
            console.print(f"[red]Error deleting specification: {e}[/red]")

    async def _run_delete_component(self, parent_spec_id: str, component_id: str, force: bool = False):
        """Delete a component with safety checks."""
        try:
            # First analyze deletion impact
            response = make_request("DELETE", f"specification/{parent_spec_id}/component/{component_id}", {
                "force": False,  # Always analyze first
                "backup": True
            })
            
            if response.get("status") == "confirmation_required":
                impact = response.get("impact", {})
                
                console.print(f"\n[bold yellow]⚠️  WARNING: This will delete component '{component_id}' from '{parent_spec_id}'[/bold yellow]")
                console.print(f"Dependencies: {', '.join(impact.get('dependencies', [])) if impact.get('dependencies') else 'None'}")
                console.print(f"Dependents: {', '.join(impact.get('dependents', [])) if impact.get('dependents') else 'None'}")
                console.print(f"Broken dependencies: {impact.get('broken_dependencies', 0)}")
                console.print(f"Contracts lost: {impact.get('contracts_lost', 0)}")
                console.print("[green]Parent contracts will remain unchanged.[/green]")
                console.print("[green]A backup will be created before deletion.[/green]")
                
                if not force:
                    from rich.prompt import Confirm
                    if not Confirm.ask("Continue with deletion?"):
                        console.print("Deletion cancelled.")
                        return
                
                # Proceed with deletion
                response = make_request("DELETE", f"specification/{parent_spec_id}/component/{component_id}", {
                    "force": True,
                    "backup": True
                })
            
            if response.get("status") == "deleted":
                console.print(f"[green]✓[/green] Deleted component '{component_id}' from '{parent_spec_id}'")
                if response.get("backup_path"):
                    console.print(f"[blue]Backup created at:[/blue] {response['backup_path']}")
                console.print("[yellow]Note: Parent specification may need manual synchronization.[/yellow]")
            else:
                console.print(f"[red]Failed to delete component: {response.get('message', 'Unknown error')}[/red]")
                
        except Exception as e:
            console.print(f"[red]Error deleting component: {e}[/red]")

````

Now you can delete specifications from within the interactive session using these commands:

```bash
# Delete a specification (with confirmation prompt)
delete-spec <spec_id>

# Delete a specification without confirmation
delete-spec <spec_id> --force

# Delete a component from a specification
delete-component <parent_spec_id> <component_id>

# Delete a component without confirmation
delete-component <parent_spec_id> <component_id> --force
```

The commands include the same safety features as the CLI:
- Impact analysis before deletion
- Interactive confirmation (unless you use `--force`)
- Automatic backups
- Clear feedback about what's being deleted
