"""
Interactive session for axiomander client.
"""

import typer
import asyncio
import os
import json
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Dict, Any, List, Optional
from rich.console import Console
from rich.panel import Panel
from rich.table import Table
from rich.prompt import Prompt, Confirm

from .completion import InteractiveCompletion
from .handlers import InteractiveHandlers
from ..config import _validate_api_key_for_provider
from ..http_client import make_request, stream_sse_request
from ..formatting import _display_statistics

console = Console()


class InteractiveSession:
    """Interactive session manager for axiomander."""
    
    def __init__(self):
        self.project_root = Path(".").resolve()
        self.current_spec_id = None
        self.current_mode = "exploration"  # exploration, specification, refinement, implementation
        self.provider = "anthropic"
        self.model = "sonnet"
        self.console = Console()
        self.session_active = True
        self.readline_available = False
        
        # Initialize completion and handlers
        self.completion = InteractiveCompletion(self)
        self.handlers = InteractiveHandlers(self)
        
        self._setup_completion()
    
    def _setup_completion(self):
        """Set up tab completion for the interactive session."""
        try:
            import readline
            # Set up readline for tab completion and history
            readline.set_completer(self.completion.completer)
            readline.parse_and_bind("tab: complete")
            
            # Set word delimiters (space and common punctuation, but NOT /)
            readline.set_completer_delims(' \t\n`!@#$%^&*()=+[{]}\\|;:\'",<>?')
            
            # Enable command history
            readline.set_history_length(1000)
            
            # Try to load existing history
            history_file = Path.home() / ".axiomander_history"
            try:
                readline.read_history_file(str(history_file))
            except FileNotFoundError:
                pass  # No history file yet
            
            # Save history on exit
            import atexit
            atexit.register(lambda: readline.write_history_file(str(history_file)))
            
            self.readline_available = True
        except ImportError:
            # readline not available on all platforms
            self.readline_available = False
    
    def get_context_info(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Get current context information."""
        context = {
            "project_root": str(self.project_root),
            "current_spec": self.current_spec_id,
            "mode": self.current_mode,
            "provider": self.provider,
            "model": self.model
        }
        
        # Check for existing specifications
        specs_dir = Path(".axiomander/specs")
        if specs_dir.exists():
            specs = []
            for spec_path in specs_dir.iterdir():
                if spec_path.is_dir() and spec_path.name.startswith("spec_"):
                    metadata_file = spec_path / "metadata.json"
                    if metadata_file.exists():
                        try:
                            with open(metadata_file, 'r') as f:
                                metadata = json.load(f)
                            specs.append({
                                "id": spec_path.name,
                                "name": metadata.get("name", "Unknown"),
                                "ready_for_contracts": metadata.get("ready_for_contracts", False)
                            })
                        except:
                            continue
            context["available_specs"] = specs
        else:
            context["available_specs"] = []
        
        return context
    
    def display_status(self):
        """Display current session status."""
        context = self.get_context_info()
        
        # Main status panel
        status_text = f"""
[bold blue]Project:[/bold blue] {context['project_root']}
[bold green]Mode:[/bold green] {context['mode'].title()}
[bold yellow]Model:[/bold yellow] {context['model']} ({context['provider']})
[bold cyan]Current Spec:[/bold cyan] {context['current_spec'] or 'None'}
"""
        
        self.console.print(Panel(
            status_text.strip(),
            title="🦎 Axiomander Interactive Session",
            border_style="blue"
        ))
        
        # Available specifications
        specs = context.get("available_specs", [])
        if specs:
            spec_table = Table(title="📝 Available Specifications")
            spec_table.add_column("ID", style="cyan")
            spec_table.add_column("Name", style="green")
            spec_table.add_column("Ready for Contracts", style="yellow")
            
            for spec in specs:
                ready = "✓" if spec.get("ready_for_contracts", False) else "✗"
                spec_table.add_row(
                    spec["id"],
                    spec["name"],
                    ready
                )
            
            self.console.print(spec_table)
    
    def get_available_commands(self) -> List[Dict[str, str]]:
        """Get available commands based on current context."""
        context = self.get_context_info()
        commands = []
        
        # Always available commands
        commands.extend([
            {"cmd": "status", "desc": "Show current session status"},
            {"cmd": "project <path>", "desc": "Change project directory (TAB completes paths)"},
            {"cmd": "model <model>", "desc": "Change LLM model (TAB completes model names)"},
            {"cmd": "provider <provider>", "desc": "Change LLM provider (TAB completes providers)"},
            {"cmd": "mode <mode>", "desc": "Change mode (TAB completes mode names)"},
            {"cmd": "help", "desc": "Show available commands"},
            {"cmd": "completion-help", "desc": "Show tab completion help"},
            {"cmd": "debug-completion", "desc": "Show completion debug info"},
            {"cmd": "quit", "desc": "Exit interactive session"},
        ])
        
        # Context-specific commands
        if context["mode"] == "exploration":
            commands.extend([
                {"cmd": "analyze", "desc": "Analyze current project for contract opportunities"},
                {"cmd": "scan", "desc": "Scan project and build contract graph"},
                {"cmd": "stats", "desc": "Show contract statistics"},
                {"cmd": "create-spec <name> <description>", "desc": "Create new specification"},
                {"cmd": "list-specs", "desc": "List all specifications"},
            ])
        
        elif context["mode"] == "specification":
            if context["current_spec"]:
                commands.extend([
                    {"cmd": "show-spec", "desc": "Show current specification details"},
                    {"cmd": "show-hierarchy", "desc": "Show specification hierarchy and components"},
                    {"cmd": "refine <response>", "desc": "Refine current specification with your input"},
                    {"cmd": "edit-spec <instructions>", "desc": "Direct specification editing with AI assistance"},
                    {"cmd": "generate-contracts", "desc": "Generate contracts from current specification"},
                    {"cmd": "analyze-decomposition", "desc": "Analyze decomposition opportunities"},
                    {"cmd": "decompose <component_names>", "desc": "Decompose into components (comma-separated names)"},
                    {"cmd": "decompose-interactive", "desc": "Interactive decomposition with component preview and approval"},
                    {"cmd": "create-component <name> <description>", "desc": "Create new component"},
                    {"cmd": "refine-component <component_id> <response>", "desc": "Refine component with parent sync"},
                    {"cmd": "sync-status", "desc": "Check synchronization status"},
                ])
            else:
                commands.extend([
                    {"cmd": "select-spec <spec_id>", "desc": "Select a specification to work with (TAB completes IDs)"},
                    {"cmd": "create-spec <name> <description>", "desc": "Create new specification"},
                ])
        
        elif context["mode"] == "refinement":
            commands.extend([
                {"cmd": "decompose <aim>", "desc": "Decompose high-level aim into components"},
                {"cmd": "generate <aim>", "desc": "Generate contracts for specific aim"},
            ])
        
        elif context["mode"] == "implementation":
            commands.extend([
                {"cmd": "refactor", "desc": "Analyze refactoring opportunities"},
                {"cmd": "implement <spec_id>", "desc": "Generate implementation for specification (TAB completes IDs)"},
            ])
        
        # Specification selection commands
        specs = context.get("available_specs", [])
        if specs and not context["current_spec"]:
            commands.append({"cmd": "select-spec <spec_id>", "desc": "Select specification to work with (TAB completes IDs)"})
        
        return commands
    
    def display_commands(self):
        """Display available commands."""
        commands = self.get_available_commands()
        
        cmd_table = Table(title="🎯 Available Commands")
        cmd_table.add_column("Command", style="cyan", width=30)
        cmd_table.add_column("Description", style="white")
        
        for cmd in commands:
            cmd_table.add_row(cmd["cmd"], cmd["desc"])
        
        self.console.print(cmd_table)
        
        # Show completion hints
        spec_ids = self.completion.get_available_spec_ids()
        if spec_ids:
            self.console.print(f"\n[dim]💡 Use TAB to complete specification IDs: {', '.join(spec_ids[:3])}{'...' if len(spec_ids) > 3 else ''}[/dim]")
        
        self.console.print("[dim]💡 Use TAB to complete commands, models, providers, and paths[/dim]")
    
    async def execute_command(self, command: str, args: List[str]):
        """Execute a command with arguments."""
        await self.handlers.execute_command(command, args)
    
    async def handle_conversation(self, user_input: str):
        """Handle natural conversation with mode-specific AI agent."""
        await self.handlers.handle_conversation(user_input)
    
    def _confirm_exit(self) -> bool:
        """Confirm exit with the user."""
        try:
            return Confirm.ask("\n[yellow]Exit interactive mode?[/yellow]", console=self.console)
        except (KeyboardInterrupt, EOFError):
            return True
    
    async def run(self):
        """Run the interactive session."""
        self.console.print("[bold green]🦎 Welcome to Axiomander Interactive Mode![/bold green]")
        self.console.print("[dim]Type '/help' for commands or just chat naturally with AI[/dim]")
        self.console.print("[dim]Commands start with '/' - everything else is conversation[/dim]")
        
        if self.readline_available:
            self.console.print("[dim]✓ TAB completion enabled[/dim]\n")
        else:
            self.console.print("[dim]⚠ TAB completion not available (readline module missing)[/dim]\n")
        
        self.display_status()
        self.console.print()
        
        # Show mode-specific welcome message
        mode_welcome = self.handlers.get_mode_welcome_message()
        if mode_welcome:
            self.console.print(Panel(
                mode_welcome,
                title=f"🎯 {self.current_mode.title()} Mode",
                border_style="green"
            ))
        
        self.console.print("\n[dim]💡 Try saying something like:[/dim]")
        example_inputs = self.handlers.get_mode_example_inputs()
        for example in example_inputs:
            self.console.print(f"[dim]  • {example}[/dim]")
        self.console.print(f"[dim]  • /help (for commands)[/dim]")
        
        while self.session_active:
            try:
                # Get user input with readline support for tab completion
                mode_indicator = f"[{self.current_mode}]"
                if self.readline_available:
                    try:
                        # Use readline-compatible prompt with proper escape sequences
                        # \001 and \002 tell readline that the enclosed characters are non-printing
                        colored_mode = f"\001\033[1;33m\002{mode_indicator}\001\033[0m\002"
                        colored_prompt = f"\001\033[1;36m\002axiomander>\001\033[0m\002"
                        prompt = f"\n{colored_mode} {colored_prompt} "
                        user_input = input(prompt)
                    except (EOFError, KeyboardInterrupt):
                        if self._confirm_exit():
                            break
                        else:
                            continue
                else:
                    # Fallback to Rich prompt if readline not available
                    user_input = Prompt.ask(f"\n[bold yellow]{mode_indicator}[/bold yellow] [bold cyan]axiomander>[/bold cyan]", console=self.console)
                
                if not user_input.strip():
                    continue
                
                # Check if input is a command (starts with /) or natural conversation
                if user_input.startswith('/'):
                    # Handle as command
                    command_input = user_input[1:]  # Remove the /
                    parts = command_input.strip().split()
                    if parts:
                        command = parts[0].lower()
                        args = parts[1:] if len(parts) > 1 else []
                        await self.execute_command(command, args)
                    else:
                        self.console.print("[yellow]Empty command. Type '/help' for available commands.[/yellow]")
                else:
                    # Handle as natural conversation with AI
                    await self.handle_conversation(user_input)
                
            except KeyboardInterrupt:
                if self._confirm_exit():
                    break
                else:
                    continue
            except EOFError:
                break
        
        self.console.print("[green]Session ended. Goodbye! 🦎[/green]")


def interactive():
    """Start interactive mode for contextual AI-powered development."""
    session = InteractiveSession()
    asyncio.run(session.run())

> 216█async def execute_file_operation(operation: Dict[str, Any]):  
> 217│    """Execute file operations locally."""  
> 218│    op_type = operation.get("operation")  
> 219│    path = operation.get("path")  
> 220│    content = operation.get("content", "")  
> 221│    encoding = operation.get("encoding", "utf-8")  
> 222│  
> 223│    try:  
> 224█        path_obj = Path(path)  
> 225│  
> 226│        if op_type == "create_directory":  
> 227│            path_obj.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)  
> 228█            console.print(f"[dim]Created directory: {path}[/dim]")  
> 229│  
> 230│        elif op_type == "create_file":  
> 231│            path_obj.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)  
> 232│            path_obj.write_text(content, encoding=encoding)  
> 233█            console.print(f"[dim]Created file: {path}[/dim]")  
> 234│  
> 235│        elif op_type == "update_file":  
> 236│            path_obj.write_text(content, encoding=encoding)  
> 237█            console.print(f"[dim]Updated file: {path}[/dim]")  
> 238│  
> 239│        elif op_type == "update_index":  
> 240│            # Special handling for index updates  
> 241│            if path_obj.exists():  
> 242█                existing_data = json.loads(path_obj.read_text())  
> 243█                new_data = json.loads(content)  
> 244│                existing_data.update(new_data)  
> 245█                path_obj.write_text(json.dumps(existing_data, indent=2), encoding=encoding)  
> 246│            else:  
> 247│                path_obj.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)  
> 248│                path_obj.write_text(content, encoding=encoding)  
> 249█            console.print(f"[dim]Updated index: {path}[/dim]")  
> 250│  
> 251│        elif op_type == "delete_file":  
> 252│            if path_obj.exists():  
> 253│                path_obj.unlink()  
> 254█                console.print(f"[dim]Deleted file: {path}[/dim]")  
> 255│  
> 256│    except Exception as e:  
> 257█        console.print(f"[red]File operation failed: {e}[/red]")  
> 258│        raise  
> 259│  
> 260│  
> 261█async def execute_git_operation(operation: Dict[str, Any]):  
> 262│    """Execute git operations locally."""  
> 263│    op_type = operation.get("operation")  
> 264│    message = operation.get("message", "")  
> ...⋮...  
> 267│    try:  
> 268│        if op_type == "commit":  
> 269│            # Add files to git  
> 270│            if files:  
> 271│                for file_pattern in files:  
> 272█                    result = subprocess.run(  
> 273│                        ["git", "add", file_pattern],  
> 274│                        capture_output=True,  
> 275│                        text=True,  
> 276│                        cwd="."  
> 277│                    )  
> 278│                    if result.returncode != 0:  
> 279█                        console.print(f"[yellow]Warning: git add failed for {file_pattern}: {result.stderr}[/yellow]")  
> 280│  
> 281│            # Commit changes  
> 282█            result = subprocess.run(  
> 283│                ["git", "commit", "-m", message],  
> 284│                capture_output=True,  
> 285│                text=True,  
> 286│                cwd="."  
> 287│            )  
> 288│  
> 289│            if result.returncode == 0:  
> 290█                console.print(f"[dim]Committed: {message}[/dim]")  
> 291│            else:  
> 292│                # Check if it's just "nothing to commit"  
> 293│                if "nothing to commit" in result.stdout:  
> 294█                    console.print(f"[dim]No changes to commit[/dim]")  
> 295│                else:  
> 296█                    console.print(f"[yellow]Git commit warning: {result.stderr}[/yellow]")  
> 297│  
> 298│        elif op_type == "branch":  
> 299│            branch_name = operation.get("branch_name", "")  
> 300█            result = subprocess.run(  
> 301│                ["git", "checkout", "-b", branch_name],  
> 302│                capture_output=True,  
> 303│                text=True,  
> 304│                cwd="."  
> 305│            )  
> 306│  
> 307│            if result.returncode == 0:  
> 308█                console.print(f"[dim]Created branch: {branch_name}[/dim]")  
> 309│            else:  
> 310█                console.print(f"[yellow]Branch creation warning: {result.stderr}[/yellow]")  
> 311│  
> 312│    except Exception as e:  
> 313█        console.print(f"[red]Git operation failed: {e}[/red]")  
> 314│        # Don't raise - git operations are not critical  
> 315│  
> 316│  
> 317█def wait_for_task(task_id: str, show_progress: bool = True) -> Dict[str, Any]:  
> 318│    """Wait for a background task to complete and return the result."""  
> 319│    if show_progress:  
> 320█        with Progress(  
> 321█            SpinnerColumn(),  
> 322█            TextColumn("[progress.description]{task.description}"),  
> 323█            console=console,  
> 324│        ) as progress:  
> 325│            task = progress.add_task("Processing...", total=None)  
> 326│  
> 327│            while True:  
> 328│                result = make_request("GET", f"/task/{task_id}")  
> 329│  
> 330│                if result["status"] == "completed":  
> 331│                    progress.update(task, description="✓ Completed")  
> 332│                    return result  
> 333│                elif result["status"] == "failed":  
> 334│                    progress.update(task, description="✗ Failed")  
> 335█                    console.print(f"[red]Task failed: {result.get('error', 'Unknown error')}[/red]")  
> 336█                    raise typer.Exit(1)  
> 337│                else:  
> 338│                    current_step = result.get("current_step", "Processing")  
> 339│                    progress_pct = result.get("progress", 0)  
> 340│                    progress.update(task, description=f"{current_step} ({progress_pct}%)")  
> 341│  
> 342█                time.sleep(1)  
> 343│    else:  
> 344│        while True:  
> 345│            result = make_request("GET", f"/task/{task_id}")  
> 346│            if result["status"] in ["completed", "failed"]:  
> 347│                return result  
> 348█            time.sleep(1)  
> 349│  
> 350│

> 357█    provider: str = typer.Option("anthropic", "--provider", help="LLM provider (anthropic, openai)"),  
> 358█    wait: bool = typer.Option(True, "--wait/--no-wait", help="Wait for analysis to complete"),  
> 359│):  
> 360│    """Analyze a project and get AI-powered recommendations."""  
> 361│    # Validate API key for the selected provider  
> 362│    _validate_api_key_for_provider(provider)  
> 363│  
> 364█    console.print(f"[blue]Analyzing project:[/blue] {Path(project).resolve()}")  
> 365│  
> 366│    data = {  
> 367█        "project_root": str(Path(project).resolve()),  
> 368│        "mode": mode,  
> 369│        "focus_areas": focus_areas or [],  
> 370│        "model": model,

> 1305│):

> 1310│    try:  
> 1311│        data = {  
> 1312│            "parent_spec_id": parent_spec_id,  
> 1313│            "component_id": component_id,

> 1319█            console.print(f"[green]✓ Component synchronized successfully[/green]")  
> 1320│

> 1329│                ))

> 1333█            raise typer.Exit(1)  
> 1334│  
> 1335│    except Exception as e:

> 1627█def _edit_component_suggestion(suggestion: ComponentSuggestion) -> bool:  
> 1628│    """Allow user to edit component suggestion. Returns True if changes were made."""  
> 1629█    console.print("\n[cyan]What would you like to edit?[/cyan]")  
> 1630█    console.print("1. Component name")  
> 1631█    console.print("2. Scope description")  
> 1632█    console.print("3. Add custom instructions")  
> 1633█    console.print("4. Cancel editing")  
> 1634│  
> 1635█    choice = Prompt.ask("Select option", choices=["1", "2", "3", "4"], default="4")  
> 1636│  
> 1637│    if choice == "1":  
> 1638│        current_name = suggestion.name  
> 1639█        new_name = Prompt.ask(f"New component name", default=current_name)  
> 1640│        if new_name and new_name != current_name:  
> 1641│            suggestion.name = new_name  
> 1642█            console.print(f"[green]✓ Updated name to: {new_name}[/green]")  
> 1643│            return True  
> 1644│  
> 1645│    elif choice == "2":  
> 1646│        current_scope = suggestion.suggested_scope  
> 1647█        console.print(f"[dim]Current scope: {current_scope}[/dim]")  
> 1648█        new_scope = Prompt.ask("New scope description")  
> 1649│        if new_scope and new_scope != current_scope:  
> 1650│            suggestion.suggested_scope = new_scope  
> 1651█            console.print(f"[green]✓ Updated scope[/green]")  
> 1652│            return True  
> 1653│  
> 1654│    elif choice == "3":  
> 1655█        instructions = Prompt.ask("Additional instructions for this component")  
> 1656│        if instructions:  
> 1657│            # Add instructions to rationale  
> 1658│            current_rationale = suggestion.rationale  
> 1659│            suggestion.rationale = f"{current_rationale}\n\nUser instructions: {instructions}"  
> 1660█            console.print(f"[green]✓ Added custom instructions[/green]")  
> 1661│            return True  
> 1662│  
> 1663│    elif choice == "4":  
> 1664█        console.print("[yellow]Editing cancelled[/yellow]")  
> 1665│        return False  
> 1666│  
> 1667│    return False  
> 1668│

> 1683│        "specification_id": specification_id,  
> ...⋮...

"""
Tab completion support for interactive axiomander session.
"""

from typing import List, Optional, Set
from pathlib import Path


class InteractiveCompletion:
    """Handles tab completion for the interactive session."""
    
    def __init__(self, session):
        self.session = session
        
        # Available commands by mode
        self.base_commands = [
            "status", "project", "model", "provider", "mode", "help", 
            "completion-help", "debug-completion", "quit"
        ]
        
        self.exploration_commands = [
            "analyze", "scan", "stats", "create-spec", "list-specs"
        ]
        
        self.specification_commands = [
            "show-spec", "show-hierarchy", "refine", "edit-spec", 
            "generate-contracts", "analyze-decomposition", "decompose",
            "decompose-interactive", "create-component", "refine-component",
            "sync-status", "select-spec"
        ]
        
        self.refinement_commands = [
            "decompose", "generate"
        ]
        
        self.implementation_commands = [
            "refactor", "implement"
        ]
        
        # Available providers and models
        self.providers = ["anthropic", "openai"]
        self.models = {
            "anthropic": ["sonnet", "haiku", "opus"],
            "openai": ["gpt-4", "gpt-3.5-turbo"]
        }
        
        self.modes = ["exploration", "specification", "refinement", "implementation"]
    
    def get_available_commands(self) -> List[str]:
        """Get available commands for current mode."""
        commands = self.base_commands.copy()
        
        if self.session.current_mode == "exploration":
            commands.extend(self.exploration_commands)
        elif self.session.current_mode == "specification":
            commands.extend(self.specification_commands)
        elif self.session.current_mode == "refinement":
            commands.extend(self.refinement_commands)
        elif self.session.current_mode == "implementation":
            commands.extend(self.implementation_commands)
        
        return commands
    
    def get_available_spec_ids(self) -> List[str]:
        """Get available specification IDs."""
        specs_dir = Path(".axiomander/specs")
        if not specs_dir.exists():
            return []
        
        spec_ids = []
        for spec_path in specs_dir.iterdir():
            if spec_path.is_dir() and spec_path.name.startswith("spec_"):
                spec_ids.append(spec_path.name)
        
        return spec_ids
    
    def get_path_completions(self, partial_path: str) -> List[str]:
        """Get path completions for the given partial path."""
        try:
            if not partial_path:
                # Complete from current directory
                base_path = Path(".")
                prefix = ""
            else:
                path = Path(partial_path)
                if partial_path.endswith("/"):
                    # Complete from the directory
                    base_path = path
                    prefix = partial_path
                else:
                    # Complete from parent directory
                    base_path = path.parent
                    prefix = str(path.parent) + "/" if path.parent != Path(".") else ""
            
            if not base_path.exists():
                return []
            
            completions = []
            for item in base_path.iterdir():
                if item.is_dir():
                    completions.append(prefix + item.name + "/")
                else:
                    completions.append(prefix + item.name)
            
            return sorted(completions)
        except:
            return []
    
    def completer(self, text: str, state: int) -> Optional[str]:
        """Readline completer function."""
        try:
            if state == 0:
                # First call - generate completions
                self.matches = self._get_completions(text)
            
            if state < len(self.matches):
                return self.matches[state]
            else:
                return None
        except:
            return None
    
    def _get_completions(self, text: str) -> List[str]:
        """Generate completions for the given text."""
        # Get the current line buffer
        try:
            import readline
            line_buffer = readline.get_line_buffer()
        except:
            line_buffer = text
        
        # Remove leading slash if present (command prefix)
        if line_buffer.startswith('/'):
            line_buffer = line_buffer[1:]
        
        parts = line_buffer.split()
        
        if not parts:
            # Complete commands
            commands = self.get_available_commands()
            return [cmd for cmd in commands if cmd.startswith(text)]
        
        command = parts[0].lower()
        
        # Command-specific completions
        if command == "model":
            if len(parts) == 1:
                # Complete model names for current provider
                models = self.models.get(self.session.provider, [])
                return [model for model in models if model.startswith(text)]
        
        elif command == "provider":
            if len(parts) == 1:
                return [provider for provider in self.providers if provider.startswith(text)]
        
        elif command == "mode":
            if len(parts) == 1:
                return [mode for mode in self.modes if mode.startswith(text)]
        
        elif command == "project":
            if len(parts) >= 1:
                # Complete paths
                return self.get_path_completions(text)
        
        elif command in ["select-spec", "implement"]:
            if len(parts) == 1:
                # Complete specification IDs
                spec_ids = self.get_available_spec_ids()
                return [spec_id for spec_id in spec_ids if spec_id.startswith(text)]
        
        elif command == "refine-component":
            if len(parts) == 1:
                # Complete component IDs (would need to implement this)
                return []
        
        # Default: complete commands if at the beginning
        if len(parts) == 1:
            commands = self.get_available_commands()
            return [cmd for cmd in commands if cmd.startswith(text)]
        
        return []

"""
Command and conversation handlers for interactive axiomander session.
"""

import json
import asyncio
from pathlib import Path
from typing import List, Dict, Any, Optional
from rich.console import Console
from rich.panel import Panel
from rich.table import Table
from rich.prompt import Confirm

from ..config import get_server_config
from ..http_client import make_request, stream_sse_request
from ..formatting import _display_statistics

console = Console()


class InteractiveHandlers:
    """Handles commands and conversations for the interactive session."""
    
    def __init__(self, session):
        self.session = session
        self.console = Console()
    
    async def execute_command(self, command: str, args: List[str]):
        """Execute a command with arguments."""
        try:
            if command == "help":
                self.session.display_commands()
            
            elif command == "status":
                self.session.display_status()
            
            elif command == "completion-help":
                self._show_completion_help()
            
            elif command == "debug-completion":
                self._show_completion_debug()
            
            elif command == "quit":
                self.session.session_active = False
            
            elif command == "project":
                await self._handle_project_command(args)
            
            elif command == "model":
                await self._handle_model_command(args)
            
            elif command == "provider":
                await self._handle_provider_command(args)
            
            elif command == "mode":
                await self._handle_mode_command(args)
            
            elif command == "analyze":
                await self._handle_analyze_command()
            
            elif command == "scan":
                await self._handle_scan_command()
            
            elif command == "stats":
                await self._handle_stats_command()
            
            elif command == "create-spec":
                await self._handle_create_spec_command(args)
            
            elif command == "list-specs":
                await self._handle_list_specs_command()
            
            elif command == "select-spec":
                await self._handle_select_spec_command(args)
            
            elif command == "show-spec":
                await self._handle_show_spec_command()
            
            elif command == "show-hierarchy":
                await self._handle_show_hierarchy_command()
            
            else:
                self.console.print(f"[red]Unknown command: {command}[/red]")
                self.console.print("[dim]Type '/help' for available commands[/dim]")
        
        except Exception as e:
            self.console.print(f"[red]Error executing command: {e}[/red]")
    
    async def handle_conversation(self, user_input: str):
        """Handle natural conversation with mode-specific AI agent."""
        self.console.print(f"[dim]🤖 AI conversation not yet implemented[/dim]")
        self.console.print(f"[dim]You said: {user_input}[/dim]")
        self.console.print(f"[dim]Current mode: {self.session.current_mode}[/dim]")
    
    def get_mode_welcome_message(self) -> str:
        """Get welcome message for current mode."""
        messages = {
            "exploration": "Explore your codebase and discover contract opportunities. I can help analyze your project structure and suggest where contracts would be most beneficial.",
            "specification": "Work with specifications and components. I can help refine specifications, decompose them into components, and generate contracts.",
            "refinement": "Refine and improve existing contracts. I can help decompose high-level aims and generate targeted contracts.",
            "implementation": "Generate implementations from specifications. I can help refactor code and implement contract-based designs."
        }
        return messages.get(self.session.current_mode, "")
    
    def get_mode_example_inputs(self) -> List[str]:
        """Get example inputs for current mode."""
        examples = {
            "exploration": [
                "Analyze my project for contract opportunities",
                "What functions would benefit from contracts?",
                "Show me the current contract statistics"
            ],
            "specification": [
                "Help me refine this specification",
                "Break this down into smaller components",
                "Generate contracts for the current spec"
            ],
            "refinement": [
                "Improve the contracts for error handling",
                "Make the preconditions more specific",
                "Add invariants for data consistency"
            ],
            "implementation": [
                "Generate implementation for this specification",
                "Refactor this code to use contracts",
                "Show me implementation opportunities"
            ]
        }
        return examples.get(self.session.current_mode, [])
    
    def _show_completion_help(self):
        """Show tab completion help."""
        help_text = """
[bold cyan]Tab Completion Help[/bold cyan]

• Commands: Type '/' and press TAB to see available commands
• Models: Type '/model ' and press TAB to see available models
• Providers: Type '/provider ' and press TAB to see available providers
• Modes: Type '/mode ' and press TAB to see available modes
• Paths: Type '/project ' and press TAB to browse directories
• Spec IDs: Type '/select-spec ' and press TAB to see specification IDs

[dim]Note: Completion works best when you type a few characters first[/dim]
"""
        self.console.print(Panel(help_text.strip(), border_style="blue"))
    
    def _show_completion_debug(self):
        """Show completion debug information."""
        debug_info = f"""
[bold yellow]Completion Debug Info[/bold yellow]

• Readline available: {self.session.readline_available}
• Current mode: {self.session.current_mode}
• Available commands: {len(self.session.completion.get_available_commands())}
• Available specs: {len(self.session.completion.get_available_spec_ids())}
"""
        self.console.print(Panel(debug_info.strip(), border_style="yellow"))
    
    async def _handle_project_command(self, args: List[str]):
        """Handle project directory change."""
        if not args:
            self.console.print("[red]Usage: /project <path>[/red]")
            return
        
        path = Path(args[0]).resolve()
        if not path.exists():
            self.console.print(f"[red]Path does not exist: {path}[/red]")
            return
        
        if not path.is_dir():
            self.console.print(f"[red]Path is not a directory: {path}[/red]")
            return
        
        self.session.project_root = path
        self.console.print(f"[green]Changed project directory to: {path}[/green]")
    
    async def _handle_model_command(self, args: List[str]):
        """Handle model change."""
        if not args:
            available_models = self.session.completion.models.get(self.session.provider, [])
            self.console.print(f"[yellow]Current model: {self.session.model}[/yellow]")
            self.console.print(f"[dim]Available models for {self.session.provider}: {', '.join(available_models)}[/dim]")
            return
        
        model = args[0]
        available_models = self.session.completion.models.get(self.session.provider, [])
        
        if model not in available_models:
            self.console.print(f"[red]Invalid model: {model}[/red]")
            self.console.print(f"[dim]Available models: {', '.join(available_models)}[/dim]")
            return
        
        self.session.model = model
        self.console.print(f"[green]Changed model to: {model}[/green]")
    
    async def _handle_provider_command(self, args: List[str]):
        """Handle provider change."""
        if not args:
            self.console.print(f"[yellow]Current provider: {self.session.provider}[/yellow]")
            self.console.print(f"[dim]Available providers: {', '.join(self.session.completion.providers)}[/dim]")
            return
        
        provider = args[0]
        if provider not in self.session.completion.providers:
            self.console.print(f"[red]Invalid provider: {provider}[/red]")
            self.console.print(f"[dim]Available providers: {', '.join(self.session.completion.providers)}[/dim]")
            return
        
        self.session.provider = provider
        # Reset model to first available for new provider
        available_models = self.session.completion.models.get(provider, [])
        if available_models:
            self.session.model = available_models[0]
        
        self.console.print(f"[green]Changed provider to: {provider}[/green]")
        self.console.print(f"[green]Changed model to: {self.session.model}[/green]")
    
    async def _handle_mode_command(self, args: List[str]):
        """Handle mode change."""
        if not args:
            self.console.print(f"[yellow]Current mode: {self.session.current_mode}[/yellow]")
            self.console.print(f"[dim]Available modes: {', '.join(self.session.completion.modes)}[/dim]")
            return
        
        mode = args[0]
        if mode not in self.session.completion.modes:
            self.console.print(f"[red]Invalid mode: {mode}[/red]")
            self.console.print(f"[dim]Available modes: {', '.join(self.session.completion.modes)}[/dim]")
            return
        
        self.session.current_mode = mode
        self.console.print(f"[green]Changed mode to: {mode}[/green]")
        
        # Show mode-specific welcome message
        welcome = self.get_mode_welcome_message()
        if welcome:
            self.console.print(Panel(
                welcome,
                title=f"🎯 {mode.title()} Mode",
                border_style="green"
            ))
    
    async def _handle_analyze_command(self):
        """Handle project analysis."""
        self.console.print("[dim]🔍 Analyzing project... (not yet implemented)[/dim]")
    
    async def _handle_scan_command(self):
        """Handle project scanning."""
        self.console.print("[dim]📊 Scanning project... (not yet implemented)[/dim]")
    
    async def _handle_stats_command(self):
        """Handle statistics display."""
        self.console.print("[dim]📈 Showing statistics... (not yet implemented)[/dim]")
    
    async def _handle_create_spec_command(self, args: List[str]):
        """Handle specification creation."""
        if len(args) < 2:
            self.console.print("[red]Usage: /create-spec <name> <description>[/red]")
            return
        
        name = args[0]
        description = " ".join(args[1:])
        self.console.print(f"[dim]📝 Creating specification '{name}': {description}... (not yet implemented)[/dim]")
    
    async def _handle_list_specs_command(self):
        """Handle listing specifications."""
        context = self.session.get_context_info()
        specs = context.get("available_specs", [])
        
        if not specs:
            self.console.print("[yellow]No specifications found[/yellow]")
            return
        
        spec_table = Table(title="📝 Available Specifications")
        spec_table.add_column("ID", style="cyan")
        spec_table.add_column("Name", style="green")
        spec_table.add_column("Ready for Contracts", style="yellow")
        
        for spec in specs:
            ready = "✓" if spec.get("ready_for_contracts", False) else "✗"
            spec_table.add_row(
                spec["id"],
                spec["name"],
                ready
            )
        
        self.console.print(spec_table)
    
    async def _handle_select_spec_command(self, args: List[str]):
        """Handle specification selection."""
        if not args:
            self.console.print("[red]Usage: /select-spec <spec_id>[/red]")
            available_specs = self.session.completion.get_available_spec_ids()
            if available_specs:
                self.console.print(f"[dim]Available specs: {', '.join(available_specs)}[/dim]")
            return
        
        spec_id = args[0]
        available_specs = self.session.completion.get_available_spec_ids()
        
        if spec_id not in available_specs:
            self.console.print(f"[red]Specification not found: {spec_id}[/red]")
            if available_specs:
                self.console.print(f"[dim]Available specs: {', '.join(available_specs)}[/dim]")
            return
        
        self.session.current_spec_id = spec_id
        self.console.print(f"[green]Selected specification: {spec_id}[/green]")
        
        # Switch to specification mode if not already there
        if self.session.current_mode != "specification":
            self.session.current_mode = "specification"
            self.console.print("[green]Switched to specification mode[/green]")
    
    async def _handle_show_spec_command(self):
        """Handle showing current specification."""
        if not self.session.current_spec_id:
            self.console.print("[red]No specification selected. Use /select-spec <spec_id>[/red]")
            return
        
        self.console.print(f"[dim]📋 Showing specification {self.session.current_spec_id}... (not yet implemented)[/dim]")
    
    async def _handle_show_hierarchy_command(self):
        """Handle showing specification hierarchy."""
        if not self.session.current_spec_id:
            self.console.print("[red]No specification selected. Use /select-spec <spec_id>[/red]")
            return
        
        self.console.print(f"[dim]🌳 Showing hierarchy for {self.session.current_spec_id}... (not yet implemented)[/dim]")

"""
Tab completion support for interactive axiomander session.
"""

from typing import List, Optional, Set
from pathlib import Path


class InteractiveCompletion:
    """Handles tab completion for the interactive session."""
    
    def __init__(self, session):
        self.session = session
        
        # Available commands by mode
        self.base_commands = [
            "status", "project", "model", "provider", "mode", "help", 
            "completion-help", "debug-completion", "quit"
        ]
        
        self.exploration_commands = [
            "analyze", "scan", "stats", "create-spec", "list-specs"
        ]
        
        self.specification_commands = [
            "show-spec", "show-hierarchy", "refine", "edit-spec", 
            "generate-contracts", "analyze-decomposition", "decompose",
            "decompose-interactive", "create-component", "refine-component",
            "sync-status", "select-spec"
        ]
        
        self.refinement_commands = [
            "decompose", "generate"
        ]
        
        self.implementation_commands = [
            "refactor", "implement"
        ]
        
        # Available providers and models
        self.providers = ["anthropic", "openai"]
        self.models = {
            "anthropic": ["sonnet", "haiku", "opus"],
            "openai": ["gpt-4", "gpt-3.5-turbo"]
        }
        
        self.modes = ["exploration", "specification", "refinement", "implementation"]
    
    def get_available_commands(self) -> List[str]:
        """Get available commands for current mode."""
        commands = self.base_commands.copy()
        
        if self.session.current_mode == "exploration":
            commands.extend(self.exploration_commands)
        elif self.session.current_mode == "specification":
            commands.extend(self.specification_commands)
        elif self.session.current_mode == "refinement":
            commands.extend(self.refinement_commands)
        elif self.session.current_mode == "implementation":
            commands.extend(self.implementation_commands)
        
        return commands
    
    def get_available_spec_ids(self) -> List[str]:
        """Get available specification IDs."""
        specs_dir = Path(".axiomander/specs")
        if not specs_dir.exists():
            return []
        
        spec_ids = []
        for spec_path in specs_dir.iterdir():
            if spec_path.is_dir() and spec_path.name.startswith("spec_"):
                spec_ids.append(spec_path.name)
        
        return spec_ids
    
    def get_path_completions(self, partial_path: str) -> List[str]:
        """Get path completions for the given partial path."""
        try:
            if not partial_path:
                # Complete from current directory
                base_path = Path(".")
                prefix = ""
            else:
                path = Path(partial_path)
                if partial_path.endswith("/"):
                    # Complete from the directory
                    base_path = path
                    prefix = partial_path
                else:
                    # Complete from parent directory
                    base_path = path.parent
                    prefix = str(path.parent) + "/" if path.parent != Path(".") else ""
            
            if not base_path.exists():
                return []
            
            completions = []
            for item in base_path.iterdir():
                if item.is_dir():
                    completions.append(prefix + item.name + "/")
                else:
                    completions.append(prefix + item.name)
            
            return sorted(completions)
        except:
            return []
    
    def completer(self, text: str, state: int) -> Optional[str]:
        """Readline completer function."""
        try:
            if state == 0:
                # First call - generate completions
                self.matches = self._get_completions(text)
            
            if state < len(self.matches):
                return self.matches[state]
            else:
                return None
        except:
            return None
    
    def _get_completions(self, text: str) -> List[str]:
        """Generate completions for the given text."""
        # Get the current line buffer
        try:
            import readline
            line_buffer = readline.get_line_buffer()
        except:
            line_buffer = text
        
        # Remove leading slash if present (command prefix)
        if line_buffer.startswith('/'):
            line_buffer = line_buffer[1:]
        
        parts = line_buffer.split()
        
        if not parts:
            # Complete commands
            commands = self.get_available_commands()
            return [cmd for cmd in commands if cmd.startswith(text)]
        
        command = parts[0].lower()
        
        # Command-specific completions
        if command == "model":
            if len(parts) == 1:
                # Complete model names for current provider
                models = self.models.get(self.session.provider, [])
                return [model for model in models if model.startswith(text)]
        
        elif command == "provider":
            if len(parts) == 1:
                return [provider for provider in self.providers if provider.startswith(text)]
        
        elif command == "mode":
            if len(parts) == 1:
                return [mode for mode in self.modes if mode.startswith(text)]
        
        elif command == "project":
            if len(parts) >= 1:
                # Complete paths
                return self.get_path_completions(text)
        
        elif command in ["select-spec", "implement"]:
            if len(parts) == 1:
                # Complete specification IDs
                spec_ids = self.get_available_spec_ids()
                return [spec_id for spec_id in spec_ids if spec_id.startswith(text)]
        
        elif command == "refine-component":
            if len(parts) == 1:
                # Complete component IDs (would need to implement this)
                return []
        
        # Default: complete commands if at the beginning
        if len(parts) == 1:
            commands = self.get_available_commands()
            return [cmd for cmd in commands if cmd.startswith(text)]
        
        return []

"""
Command and conversation handlers for interactive axiomander session.
"""

import json
import asyncio
from pathlib import Path
from typing import List, Dict, Any, Optional
from rich.console import Console
from rich.panel import Panel
from rich.table import Table
from rich.prompt import Confirm

from ..config import get_server_config
from ..http_client import make_request, stream_sse_request
from ..formatting import _display_statistics

console = Console()


class InteractiveHandlers:
    """Handles commands and conversations for the interactive session."""
    
    def __init__(self, session):
        self.session = session
        self.console = Console()
    
    async def execute_command(self, command: str, args: List[str]):
        """Execute a command with arguments."""
        try:
            if command == "help":
                self.session.display_commands()
            
            elif command == "status":
                self.session.display_status()
            
            elif command == "completion-help":
                self._show_completion_help()
            
            elif command == "debug-completion":
                self._show_completion_debug()
            
            elif command == "quit":
                self.session.session_active = False
            
            elif command == "project":
                await self._handle_project_command(args)
            
            elif command == "model":
                await self._handle_model_command(args)
            
            elif command == "provider":
                await self._handle_provider_command(args)
            
            elif command == "mode":
                await self._handle_mode_command(args)
            
            elif command == "analyze":
                await self._handle_analyze_command()
            
            elif command == "scan":
                await self._handle_scan_command()
            
            elif command == "stats":
                await self._handle_stats_command()
            
            elif command == "create-spec":
                await self._handle_create_spec_command(args)
            
            elif command == "list-specs":
                await self._handle_list_specs_command()
            
            elif command == "select-spec":
                await self._handle_select_spec_command(args)
            
            elif command == "show-spec":
                await self._handle_show_spec_command()
            
            elif command == "show-hierarchy":
                await self._handle_show_hierarchy_command()
            
            else:
                self.console.print(f"[red]Unknown command: {command}[/red]")
                self.console.print("[dim]Type '/help' for available commands[/dim]")
        
        except Exception as e:
            self.console.print(f"[red]Error executing command: {e}[/red]")
    
    async def handle_conversation(self, user_input: str):
        """Handle natural conversation with mode-specific AI agent."""
        self.console.print(f"[dim]🤖 AI conversation not yet implemented[/dim]")
        self.console.print(f"[dim]You said: {user_input}[/dim]")
        self.console.print(f"[dim]Current mode: {self.session.current_mode}[/dim]")
    
    def get_mode_welcome_message(self) -> str:
        """Get welcome message for current mode."""
        messages = {
            "exploration": "Explore your codebase and discover contract opportunities. I can help analyze your project structure and suggest where contracts would be most beneficial.",
            "specification": "Work with specifications and components. I can help refine specifications, decompose them into components, and generate contracts.",
            "refinement": "Refine and improve existing contracts. I can help decompose high-level aims and generate targeted contracts.",
            "implementation": "Generate implementations from specifications. I can help refactor code and implement contract-based designs."
        }
        return messages.get(self.session.current_mode, "")
    
    def get_mode_example_inputs(self) -> List[str]:
        """Get example inputs for current mode."""
        examples = {
            "exploration": [
                "Analyze my project for contract opportunities",
                "What functions would benefit from contracts?",
                "Show me the current contract statistics"
            ],
            "specification": [
                "Help me refine this specification",
                "Break this down into smaller components",
                "Generate contracts for the current spec"
            ],
            "refinement": [
                "Improve the contracts for error handling",
                "Make the preconditions more specific",
                "Add invariants for data consistency"
            ],
            "implementation": [
                "Generate implementation for this specification",
                "Refactor this code to use contracts",
                "Show me implementation opportunities"
            ]
        }
        return examples.get(self.session.current_mode, [])
    
    def _show_completion_help(self):
        """Show tab completion help."""
        help_text = """
[bold cyan]Tab Completion Help[/bold cyan]

• Commands: Type '/' and press TAB to see available commands
• Models: Type '/model ' and press TAB to see available models
• Providers: Type '/provider ' and press TAB to see available providers
• Modes: Type '/mode ' and press TAB to see available modes
• Paths: Type '/project ' and press TAB to browse directories
• Spec IDs: Type '/select-spec ' and press TAB to see specification IDs

[dim]Note: Completion works best when you type a few characters first[/dim]
"""
        self.console.print(Panel(help_text.strip(), border_style="blue"))
    
    def _show_completion_debug(self):
        """Show completion debug information."""
        debug_info = f"""
[bold yellow]Completion Debug Info[/bold yellow]

• Readline available: {self.session.readline_available}
• Current mode: {self.session.current_mode}
• Available commands: {len(self.session.completion.get_available_commands())}
• Available specs: {len(self.session.completion.get_available_spec_ids())}
"""
        self.console.print(Panel(debug_info.strip(), border_style="yellow"))
    
    async def _handle_project_command(self, args: List[str]):
        """Handle project directory change."""
        if not args:
            self.console.print("[red]Usage: /project <path>[/red]")
            return
        
        path = Path(args[0]).resolve()
        if not path.exists():
            self.console.print(f"[red]Path does not exist: {path}[/red]")
            return
        
        if not path.is_dir():
            self.console.print(f"[red]Path is not a directory: {path}[/red]")
            return
        
        self.session.project_root = path
        self.console.print(f"[green]Changed project directory to: {path}[/green]")
    
    async def _handle_model_command(self, args: List[str]):
        """Handle model change."""
        if not args:
            available_models = self.session.completion.models.get(self.session.provider, [])
            self.console.print(f"[yellow]Current model: {self.session.model}[/yellow]")
            self.console.print(f"[dim]Available models for {self.session.provider}: {', '.join(available_models)}[/dim]")
            return
        
        model = args[0]
        available_models = self.session.completion.models.get(self.session.provider, [])
        
        if model not in available_models:
            self.console.print(f"[red]Invalid model: {model}[/red]")
            self.console.print(f"[dim]Available models: {', '.join(available_models)}[/dim]")
            return
        
        self.session.model = model
        self.console.print(f"[green]Changed model to: {model}[/green]")
    
    async def _handle_provider_command(self, args: List[str]):
        """Handle provider change."""
        if not args:
            self.console.print(f"[yellow]Current provider: {self.session.provider}[/yellow]")
            self.console.print(f"[dim]Available providers: {', '.join(self.session.completion.providers)}[/dim]")
            return
        
        provider = args[0]
        if provider not in self.session.completion.providers:
            self.console.print(f"[red]Invalid provider: {provider}[/red]")
            self.console.print(f"[dim]Available providers: {', '.join(self.session.completion.providers)}[/dim]")
            return
        
        self.session.provider = provider
        # Reset model to first available for new provider
        available_models = self.session.completion.models.get(provider, [])
        if available_models:
            self.session.model = available_models[0]
        
        self.console.print(f"[green]Changed provider to: {provider}[/green]")
        self.console.print(f"[green]Changed model to: {self.session.model}[/green]")
    
    async def _handle_mode_command(self, args: List[str]):
        """Handle mode change."""
        if not args:
            self.console.print(f"[yellow]Current mode: {self.session.current_mode}[/yellow]")
            self.console.print(f"[dim]Available modes: {', '.join(self.session.completion.modes)}[/dim]")
            return
        
        mode = args[0]
        if mode not in self.session.completion.modes:
            self.console.print(f"[red]Invalid mode: {mode}[/red]")
            self.console.print(f"[dim]Available modes: {', '.join(self.session.completion.modes)}[/dim]")
            return
        
        self.session.current_mode = mode
        self.console.print(f"[green]Changed mode to: {mode}[/green]")
        
        # Show mode-specific welcome message
        welcome = self.get_mode_welcome_message()
        if welcome:
            self.console.print(Panel(
                welcome,
                title=f"🎯 {mode.title()} Mode",
                border_style="green"
            ))
    
    async def _handle_analyze_command(self):
        """Handle project analysis."""
        self.console.print("[dim]🔍 Analyzing project... (not yet implemented)[/dim]")
    
    async def _handle_scan_command(self):
        """Handle project scanning."""
        self.console.print("[dim]📊 Scanning project... (not yet implemented)[/dim]")
    
    async def _handle_stats_command(self):
        """Handle statistics display."""
        self.console.print("[dim]📈 Showing statistics... (not yet implemented)[/dim]")
    
    async def _handle_create_spec_command(self, args: List[str]):
        """Handle specification creation."""
        if len(args) < 2:
            self.console.print("[red]Usage: /create-spec <name> <description>[/red]")
            return
        
        name = args[0]
        description = " ".join(args[1:])
        self.console.print(f"[dim]📝 Creating specification '{name}': {description}... (not yet implemented)[/dim]")
    
    async def _handle_list_specs_command(self):
        """Handle listing specifications."""
        context = self.session.get_context_info()
        specs = context.get("available_specs", [])
        
        if not specs:
            self.console.print("[yellow]No specifications found[/yellow]")
            return
        
        spec_table = Table(title="📝 Available Specifications")
        spec_table.add_column("ID", style="cyan")
        spec_table.add_column("Name", style="green")
        spec_table.add_column("Ready for Contracts", style="yellow")
        
        for spec in specs:
            ready = "✓" if spec.get("ready_for_contracts", False) else "✗"
            spec_table.add_row(
                spec["id"],
                spec["name"],
                ready
            )
        
        self.console.print(spec_table)
    
    async def _handle_select_spec_command(self, args: List[str]):
        """Handle specification selection."""
        if not args:
            self.console.print("[red]Usage: /select-spec <spec_id>[/red]")
            available_specs = self.session.completion.get_available_spec_ids()
            if available_specs:
                self.console.print(f"[dim]Available specs: {', '.join(available_specs)}[/dim]")
            return
        
        spec_id = args[0]
        available_specs = self.session.completion.get_available_spec_ids()
        
        if spec_id not in available_specs:
            self.console.print(f"[red]Specification not found: {spec_id}[/red]")
            if available_specs:
                self.console.print(f"[dim]Available specs: {', '.join(available_specs)}[/dim]")
            return
        
        self.session.current_spec_id = spec_id
        self.console.print(f"[green]Selected specification: {spec_id}[/green]")
        
        # Switch to specification mode if not already there
        if self.session.current_mode != "specification":
            self.session.current_mode = "specification"
            self.console.print("[green]Switched to specification mode[/green]")
    
    async def _handle_show_spec_command(self):
        """Handle showing current specification."""
        if not self.session.current_spec_id:
            self.console.print("[red]No specification selected. Use /select-spec <spec_id>[/red]")
            return
        
        self.console.print(f"[dim]📋 Showing specification {self.session.current_spec_id}... (not yet implemented)[/dim]")
    
    async def _handle_show_hierarchy_command(self):
        """Handle showing specification hierarchy."""
        if not self.session.current_spec_id:
            self.console.print("[red]No specification selected. Use /select-spec <spec_id>[/red]")
            return
        
        self.console.print(f"[dim]🌳 Showing hierarchy for {self.session.current_spec_id}... (not yet implemented)[/dim]")

    async def _handle_show_spec_command(self):
        """Handle showing current specification."""
        if not self.session.current_spec_id:
            self.console.print("[red]No specification selected. Use /select-spec <spec_id>[/red]")
            return
        
        # Load specification metadata
        spec_path = Path(".axiomander/specs") / self.session.current_spec_id
        metadata_file = spec_path / "metadata.json"
        
        if not metadata_file.exists():
            self.console.print(f"[red]Specification metadata not found: {metadata_file}[/red]")
            return
        
        try:
            with open(metadata_file, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
                metadata = json.load(f)
            
            # Display specification details
            spec_info = f"""
[bold blue]Name:[/bold blue] {metadata.get('name', 'Unknown')}
[bold green]Type:[/bold green] {metadata.get('specification_type', 'Unknown')}
[bold yellow]Ready for Contracts:[/bold yellow] {'✓' if metadata.get('ready_for_contracts', False) else '✗'}
[bold cyan]Created:[/bold cyan] {metadata.get('created_at', 'Unknown')}
[bold magenta]Last Modified:[/bold magenta] {metadata.get('last_modified', 'Unknown')}
"""
            
            self.console.print(Panel(
                spec_info.strip(),
                title=f"📋 Specification: {self.session.current_spec_id}",
                border_style="blue"
            ))
            
            # Show description if available
            description = metadata.get('description', '').strip()
            if description:
                self.console.print(Panel(
                    description,
                    title="📝 Description",
                    border_style="green"
                ))
            
            # Show components if any
            components = metadata.get('components', [])
            if components:
                comp_table = Table(title="🧩 Components")
                comp_table.add_column("ID", style="cyan")
                comp_table.add_column("Name", style="green")
                comp_table.add_column("Status", style="yellow")
                
                for comp in components:
                    comp_table.add_row(
                        comp.get('id', 'Unknown'),
                        comp.get('name', 'Unknown'),
                        comp.get('status', 'Unknown')
                    )
                
                self.console.print(comp_table)
            
            # Show content if available

                try:

                    if content:
                        # Truncate very long content

                        self.console.print(Panel(

                            title="📄 Content",
                            border_style="cyan"
                        ))
                except Exception as e:
                    self.console.print(f"[yellow]Could not read content file: {e}[/yellow]")
            
        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
            self.console.print(f"[red]Error parsing specification metadata: {e}[/red]")
        except Exception as e:
            self.console.print(f"[red]Error loading specification: {e}[/red]")

    async def _handle_show_hierarchy_command(self):
        """Handle showing specification hierarchy."""
        if not self.session.current_spec_id:
            self.console.print("[red]No specification selected. Use /select-spec <spec_id>[/red]")
            return
        
        # Load specification metadata
        spec_path = Path(".axiomander/specs") / self.session.current_spec_id
        metadata_file = spec_path / "metadata.json"
        
        if not metadata_file.exists():
            self.console.print(f"[red]Specification metadata not found: {metadata_file}[/red]")
            return
        
        try:
            with open(metadata_file, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
                metadata = json.load(f)
            
            # Display hierarchy tree
            self.console.print(f"[bold blue]🌳 Hierarchy for {metadata.get('name', self.session.current_spec_id)}[/bold blue]\n")
            
            # Root specification
            self.console.print(f"📋 {metadata.get('name', 'Root Specification')}")
            
            # Show components in tree format
            components = metadata.get('components', [])
            if components:
                for i, comp in enumerate(components):
                    is_last = i == len(components) - 1
                    prefix = "└── " if is_last else "├── "
                    
                    comp_name = comp.get('name', comp.get('id', 'Unknown'))
                    comp_status = comp.get('status', 'unknown')
                    
                    # Color code by status
                    if comp_status == 'ready':
                        status_color = "green"
                    elif comp_status == 'draft':
                        status_color = "yellow"
                    else:
                        status_color = "red"
                    
                    self.console.print(f"    {prefix}🧩 [{status_color}]{comp_name}[/{status_color}] ({comp_status})")
                    
                    # Show component details if available
                    comp_path = spec_path / "components" / comp.get('id', '')
                    if comp_path.exists():
                        comp_metadata_file = comp_path / "metadata.json"
                        if comp_metadata_file.exists():
                            try:
                                with open(comp_metadata_file, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
                                    comp_metadata = json.load(f)
                                
                                description = comp_metadata.get('description', '').strip()
                                if description and len(description) < 100:
                                    indent = "        " if is_last else "    │   "
                                    self.console.print(f"{indent}[dim]{description}[/dim]")
                            except:
                                pass
            else:
                self.console.print("    [dim]No components defined[/dim]")
            
            # Show dependencies if any
            dependencies = metadata.get('dependencies', [])
            if dependencies:
                self.console.print(f"\n[bold yellow]📦 Dependencies:[/bold yellow]")
                for dep in dependencies:
                    self.console.print(f"  • {dep}")
            
            # Show synchronization status
            sync_file = spec_path / "sync.json"
            if sync_file.exists():
                try:
                    with open(sync_file, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
                        sync_data = json.load(f)
                    
                    last_sync = sync_data.get('last_sync_timestamp')
                    if last_sync:
                        self.console.print(f"\n[bold cyan]🔄 Last Sync:[/bold cyan] {last_sync}")
                except:
                    pass
            
        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
            self.console.print(f"[red]Error parsing specification metadata: {e}[/red]")
        except Exception as e:
            self.console.print(f"[red]Error loading specification hierarchy: {e}[/red]")

            conversation_file = spec_path / "conversation.md"
            if conversation_file.exists():
                try:
                    with open(conversation_file, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
                        conversation_content = f.read().strip()
                    
                    if conversation_content:
                        # Show last few exchanges or truncate if very long
                        lines = conversation_content.split('\n')
                        if len(lines) > 50:
                            # Show last 50 lines
                            conversation_content = '\n'.join(lines[-50:])
                            conversation_content = "[dim]... (showing last 50 lines)[/dim]\n\n" + conversation_content
                        
                        self.console.print(Panel(
                            conversation_content,
                            title="💬 Conversation History",
                            border_style="green"
                        ))
                except Exception as e:
                    self.console.print(f"[yellow]Could not read conversation file: {e}[/yellow]")
            
            # Show legacy content.md if it exists and no specification.md
            elif (spec_path / "content.md").exists():
                try:
                    with open(spec_path / "content.md", 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
                        content = f.read().strip()
                    
                    if content:

To see the original `client.py` file from git, you can use:

```bash
git show HEAD~3:src/axiomander/client.py
```

This will show the version from 3 commits ago. If you want to see it from a specific commit, you can use:

```bash
git show <commit-hash>:src/axiomander/client.py
```

Or if you want to see the file from before the interactive session changes, you could try:

```bash
git show a2eff54~1:src/axiomander/client.py
```

This shows the version right before commit a2eff54 (the one where you added the interactive modules).

    async def _handle_analyze_command(self):
        """Handle project analysis."""
        # Validate API key for the selected provider
        _validate_api_key_for_provider(self.session.provider)
        
        self.console.print(f"[blue]Analyzing project:[/blue] {self.session.project_root}")
        
        data = {
            "project_root": str(self.session.project_root),
            "mode": "specification",
            "focus_areas": [],
            "model": self.session.model,
            "provider": self.session.provider
        }
        
        try:
            result = make_request("POST", "/analyze-project", data)
            
            if not result.get("success"):
                self.console.print(f"[red]Analysis failed: {result}[/red]")
                return
            
            # Show immediate results
            self.console.print(f"[green]✓ Analysis started[/green] (Task ID: {result.get('task_id', 'Unknown')})")
            
            # Display statistics
            stats = result.get("statistics", {})
            if stats:
                _display_statistics(stats)
            
            # Display recommendations
            recommendations = result.get("recommendations", [])
            if recommendations:
                self.console.print(Panel(
                    "\n".join(f"• {rec}" for rec in recommendations),
                    title="🎯 Recommendations",
                    border_style="green"
                ))
        
        except Exception as e:
            self.console.print(f"[red]Analysis error: {e}[/red]")

    async def _handle_create_spec_command(self, args: List[str]):
        """Handle specification creation."""

            self.console.print("[red]Usage: /create-spec <name> <description>[/red]")

=======
    async def _handle_create_spec_command(self, args: List[str]):
        """Handle specification creation."""
        if len(args) < 2:
            self.console.print("[red]Usage: /create-spec <name> <description>[/red]")
            return
        
        name = args[0]
        description = " ".join(args[1:])
        
        # Validate API key for the selected provider
        _validate_api_key_for_provider(self.session.provider)
        
        self.console.print(f"[blue]Creating specification:[/blue] {name}")
        self.console.print(f"[cyan]Description:[/cyan] {description}")
        
        params = {
            "project_root": str(self.session.project_root),
            "specification_name": name,
            "initial_description": description,
            "model": self.session.model,
            "provider": self.session.provider
        }
        
        try:
            result = await stream_sse_request("/stream/create-specification", params)
            
            if result.get("success"):
                spec_id = result.get("specification_id")
                self.console.print(f"\n[green]✓ Specification created successfully![/green]")
                self.console.print(f"[cyan]Specification ID:[/cyan] {spec_id}")
                
                # Auto-select the new specification
                self.session.current_spec_id = spec_id
                if self.session.current_mode != "specification":
                    self.session.current_mode = "specification"
                    self.console.print("[green]Switched to specification mode[/green]")
                
                next_steps = result.get("next_steps", [])
                if next_steps:
                    self.console.print(Panel(
                        "\n".join(f"{i+1}. {step}" for i, step in enumerate(next_steps)),
                        title="📋 Next Steps",
                        border_style="green"
                    ))
                
                self.console.print(f"\n[cyan]Use natural conversation or '/refine \"your response\"' to continue[/cyan]")
            else:
                self.console.print(f"[red]Specification creation failed[/red]")
        
        except Exception as e:
            self.console.print(f"[red]Error creating specification: {e}[/red]")

        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
