````python
    async def _handle_refine_command(self, args: List[str]):
        """Handle specification refinement."""
        if not self.session.current_spec_id:
            self.console.print("[red]No specification selected. Use /select-spec <spec_id>[/red]")
            return
        
        if not args:
            self.console.print("[red]Usage: /refine <your response>[/red]")
            return
        
        response = " ".join(args)
        
        # Validate API key for the selected provider
        _validate_api_key_for_provider(self.session.provider)
        
        self.console.print(f"[blue]Refining specification:[/blue] {self.session.current_spec_id}")
        self.console.print(f"[cyan]Your input:[/cyan] {response}")
        
        params = {
            "specification_id": self.session.current_spec_id,
            "user_response": response,
            "model": self.session.model,
            "provider": self.session.provider
        }
        
        try:
            result = await stream_sse_request("/stream/refine-specification", params)
            
            if result.get("success"):
                self.console.print(f"\n[green]✓ Specification refined successfully![/green]")
                
                ready = result.get("ready_for_contracts", False)
                if ready:
                    self.console.print(Panel(
                        "🎉 Specification is ready for contract generation!",
                        title="Ready for Contracts",
                        border_style="bright_green"
                    ))
                    self.console.print(f"[cyan]Use '/generate-contracts' to proceed[/cyan]")
                
                next_steps = result.get("next_steps", [])
                if next_steps:
                    self.console.print(Panel(
                        "\n".join(f"{i+1}. {step}" for i, step in enumerate(next_steps)),
                        title="📋 Next Steps",
                        border_style="blue"
                    ))
            else:
                self.console.print(f"[red]Specification refinement failed[/red]")
        
        except Exception as e:
            self.console.print(f"[red]Error refining specification: {e}[/red]")
    
    async def _handle_generate_contracts_command(self):
        """Handle contract generation."""
        if not self.session.current_spec_id:
            self.console.print("[red]No specification selected. Use /select-spec <spec_id>[/red]")
            return
        
        # Validate API key for the selected provider
        _validate_api_key_for_provider(self.session.provider)
        
        self.console.print(f"[blue]Generating contracts for specification:[/blue] {self.session.current_spec_id}")
        
        params = {
            "specification_id": self.session.current_spec_id,
            "model": self.session.model,
            "provider": self.session.provider
        }
        
        try:
            result = await stream_sse_request("/stream/generate-contracts", params)
            
            if result.get("success"):
                self.console.print(f"\n[green]✓ Contract descriptions generated successfully![/green]")
                
                contracts_file = result.get("contracts_file")
                if contracts_file:
                    self.console.print(f"[cyan]Contracts saved to:[/cyan] {contracts_file}")
                
                ready = result.get("ready_for_implementation", False)
                if ready:
                    self.console.print(Panel(
                        "🎉 Ready for implementation!",
                        title="Next Phase",
                        border_style="bright_green"
                    ))
                
                next_steps = result.get("next_steps", [])
                if next_steps:
                    self.console.print(Panel(
                        "\n".join(f"{i+1}. {step}" for i, step in enumerate(next_steps)),
                        title="📋 Next Steps",
                        border_style="green"
                    ))
            else:
                self.console.print(f"[red]Contract generation failed[/red]")
        
        except Exception as e:
            self.console.print(f"[red]Error generating contracts: {e}[/red]")
    
    async def _handle_analyze_decomposition_command(self):
        """Handle decomposition analysis."""
        if not self.session.current_spec_id:
            self.console.print("[red]No specification selected. Use /select-spec <spec_id>[/red]")
            return
        
        # Validate API key for the selected provider
        _validate_api_key_for_provider(self.session.provider)
        
        self.console.print(f"[blue]Analyzing decomposition opportunities for:[/blue] {self.session.current_spec_id}")
        
        params = {
            "specification_id": self.session.current_spec_id,
            "model": self.session.model,
            "provider": self.session.provider
        }
        
        try:
            result = await stream_sse_request("/stream/analyze-decomposition", params)
            
            if result.get("success"):
                self.console.print(f"[green]✓ Analysis completed[/green]")
                
                opportunities = result.get("decomposition_opportunities", [])
                if opportunities:
                    self.console.print(Panel(
                        "\n".join(f"• {opp}" for opp in opportunities),
                        title="🔍 Decomposition Opportunities",
                        border_style="green"
                    ))
                
                suggested_components = result.get("suggested_components", [])
                if suggested_components:
                    self.console.print(Panel(
                        "\n".join(f"• {comp}" for comp in suggested_components),
                        title="🧩 Suggested Components",
                        border_style="blue"
                    ))
                    
                    self.console.print(f"[cyan]Use '/decompose {', '.join(suggested_components[:3])}' to proceed[/cyan]")
            else:
                self.console.print("[red]Analysis failed[/red]")
        
        except Exception as e:
            self.console.print(f"[red]Error analyzing decomposition: {e}[/red]")
    
    async def _handle_decompose_command(self, args: List[str]):
        """Handle specification decomposition."""
        if not self.session.current_spec_id:
            self.console.print("[red]No specification selected. Use /select-spec <spec_id>[/red]")
            return
        
        if not args:
            self.console.print("[red]Usage: /decompose <component_names>[/red]")
            self.console.print("[dim]Example: /decompose authentication,validation,business_logic[/dim]")
            return
        
        component_names = " ".join(args)
        
        # Validate API key for the selected provider
        _validate_api_key_for_provider(self.session.provider)
        
        self.console.print(f"[blue]Decomposing specification:[/blue] {self.session.current_spec_id}")
        self.console.print(f"[cyan]Components:[/cyan] {component_names}")
        
        params = {
            "specification_id": self.session.current_spec_id,
            "component_names": component_names,
            "model": self.session.model,
            "provider": self.session.provider
        }
        
        try:
            result = await stream_sse_request("/stream/decompose-specification", params)
            
            if result.get("success"):
                self.console.print(f"[green]✓ Specification decomposed successfully[/green]")
                
                components = result.get("created_components", [])
                if components:
                    self.console.print(Panel(
                        "\n".join(f"• {comp['name']} ({comp['id']})" for comp in components),
                        title="🧩 Created Components",
                        border_style="green"
                    ))
                
                next_steps = result.get("next_steps", [])
                if next_steps:
                    self.console.print(Panel(
                        "\n".join(f"{i+1}. {step}" for i, step in enumerate(next_steps)),
                        title="📋 Next Steps",
                        border_style="blue"
                    ))
                
                self.console.print("[cyan]Use '/show-hierarchy' to see the new structure[/cyan]")
            else:
                self.console.print("[red]Decomposition failed[/red]")
        
        except Exception as e:
            self.console.print(f"[red]Error decomposing specification: {e}[/red]")
    
    async def _handle_decompose_interactive_command(self):
        """Handle interactive decomposition."""
        if not self.session.current_spec_id:
            self.console.print("[red]No specification selected. Use /select-spec <spec_id>[/red]")
            return
        
        # Validate API key for the selected provider
        _validate_api_key_for_provider(self.session.provider)
        
        self.console.print(f"[blue]Starting interactive decomposition for:[/blue] {self.session.current_spec_id}")
        self.console.print("[dim]AI will analyze the specification and suggest components for your approval[/dim]")
        
        params = {
            "specification_id": self.session.current_spec_id,
            "model": self.session.model,
            "provider": self.session.provider
        }
        
        try:

                else:
                    self.console.print("[yellow]No component suggestions were generated[/yellow]")
            else:
                self.console.print("[red]Interactive decomposition failed[/red]")
````

````python
    async def handle_conversation(self, user_input: str):
        """Handle natural conversation with mode-specific AI agent."""
        try:
            # Validate API key for the selected provider
            _validate_api_key_for_provider(self.session.provider)
            
            self.console.print("[dim]🤖 AI is thinking...[/dim]")
            
            # Handle mode-specific conversation
            if self.session.current_mode == "specification" and self.session.current_spec_id:
                # Use specification refinement for natural conversation
                params = {
                    "specification_id": self.session.current_spec_id,
                    "user_response": user_input,
                    "model": self.session.model,
                    "provider": self.session.provider
                }
                result = await stream_sse_request("/stream/refine-specification", params)
                
                if result.get("success"):
                    if result.get("ready_for_contracts"):
                        self.console.print("\n[bright_green]🎉 Specification is ready for contract generation![/bright_green]")
                        self.console.print("[cyan]Say 'generate contracts' or use '/generate-contracts' to proceed[/cyan]")
                
            elif self.session.current_mode == "exploration":
                # Use general analysis for exploration mode
                data = {
                    "project_root": str(self.session.project_root),
                    "mode": "exploration",
                    "user_input": user_input,
                    "model": self.session.model,
                    "provider": self.session.provider
                }
                result = make_request("POST", "/analyze-project", data)
                
                if result.get("success"):
                    recommendations = result.get("recommendations", [])
                    if recommendations:
                        self.console.print("\n[green]💡 AI Recommendations:[/green]")
                        for rec in recommendations:
                            self.console.print(f"  • {rec}")
                
            else:
                # For other modes, provide helpful guidance
                self.console.print(f"[yellow]💬 Natural conversation in '{self.session.current_mode}' mode:[/yellow]")
                self.console.print(f"[cyan]You said:[/cyan] {user_input}")
                
                # Provide mode-specific suggestions
                if self.session.current_mode == "refinement":
                    self.console.print("[dim]💡 Try using '/decompose <aim>' or '/generate <aim>' for specific actions[/dim]")
                elif self.session.current_mode == "implementation":
                    self.console.print("[dim]💡 Try using '/refactor' or '/implement <spec_id>' for specific actions[/dim]")
                else:
                    self.console.print("[dim]💡 Use '/help' to see available commands for this mode[/dim]")
        
        except Exception as e:
            self.console.print(f"[red]Error in conversation: {e}[/red]")
````

This enhanced version will:

1. **Show debug information** about what files actually exist in the specification directory
2. **Try multiple possible filenames** for specification content (specification.md, content.md, spec.md, description.md)
3. **Try multiple possible filenames** for conversation history (conversation.md, chat.md, history.md, log.md)

This should help us understand what files actually exist and where the specification content is stored.

````python
        except Exception as e:

=======
        except Exception as e:

    async def _handle_edit_spec_command(self, args: List[str]):
        """Handle direct specification editing with AI assistance."""
        if not self.session.current_spec_id:
            self.console.print("[red]No specification selected. Use /select-spec <spec_id>[/red]")
            return
        
        if not args:
            self.console.print("[red]Usage: /edit-spec <editing instructions>[/red]")
            self.console.print("[dim]Example: /edit-spec Add error handling for invalid inputs[/dim]")
            return
        
        instructions = " ".join(args)
        
        # Validate API key for the selected provider
        _validate_api_key_for_provider(self.session.provider)
        
        self.console.print(f"[blue]Editing specification:[/blue] {self.session.current_spec_id}")
        self.console.print(f"[cyan]Instructions:[/cyan] {instructions}")
        
        # Use the refine endpoint but with editing-focused instructions
        edit_prompt = f"Please modify the specification according to these instructions: {instructions}"
        
        params = {
            "specification_id": self.session.current_spec_id,
            "user_response": edit_prompt,
            "model": self.session.model,
            "provider": self.session.provider
        }
        
        try:
            result = await stream_sse_request("/stream/refine-specification", params)
            
            if result.get("success"):
                self.console.print(f"\n[green]✓ Specification edited successfully![/green]")
                self.console.print("[cyan]Use '/show-spec' to see the updated content[/cyan]")
                
                ready = result.get("ready_for_contracts", False)
                if ready:
                    self.console.print(Panel(
                        "🎉 Specification is ready for contract generation!",
                        title="Ready for Contracts",
                        border_style="bright_green"
                    ))
                    self.console.print(f"[cyan]Use '/generate-contracts' to proceed[/cyan]")
                
                next_steps = result.get("next_steps", [])
                if next_steps:
                    self.console.print(Panel(
                        "\n".join(f"{i+1}. {step}" for i, step in enumerate(next_steps)),
                        title="📋 Next Steps",
                        border_style="blue"
                    ))
            else:
                self.console.print(f"[red]Specification editing failed[/red]")
        
        except Exception as e:
            self.console.print(f"[red]Error editing specification: {e}[/red]")
    
    async def _handle_generate_contracts_command(self):
````

````python
                            conversation_found = True
                            
                            # Format conversation from JSON list
                            formatted_conversation = []
                            for entry in conversation_data:
                                if isinstance(entry, dict) and 'actor' in entry and 'content' in entry:
                                    actor = entry['actor']
                                    content = entry['content']
                                    
                                    # Format actor name with colors
                                    if actor.lower() == 'user':
                                        actor_display = "[bold blue]User:[/bold blue]"
                                    elif actor.lower() in ['assistant', 'ai']:
                                        actor_display = "[bold green]AI:[/bold green]"
                                    else:
                                        actor_display = f"[bold]{actor.title()}:[/bold]"
                                    
                                    formatted_conversation.append(f"{actor_display}\n{content}")
                            
                            conversation_content = "\n\n".join(formatted_conversation)

                                title=f"{title} ({filename}) - {len(conversation_data)} exchanges",
````

This update will:

1. **Look for JSON conversation files first** (conversation.json, chat.json, etc.)
2. **Parse the JSON as a list** of conversation entries
3. **Format each entry** with the actor (User/AI) and content in markdown
4. **Show the last 10 exchanges** if there are many (instead of 50 lines)
5. **Fall back to markdown files** if no JSON conversation files are found
6. **Display the total number of exchanges** in the panel title

The expected JSON format would be:
```json
[
  {
    "actor": "user",
    "content": "I want to create a banking application..."
  },
  {
    "actor": "assistant", 
    "content": "Great! Let's start by defining the core requirements..."
  }
]
```

```bash
axiomander interactive
```

#### Does the client handle this message format properly now?  

Looking at the current interactive client code, it should handle the JSON conversation format properly. The `_handle_show_spec_command` method in `handlers.py` already includes logic to:

1. **Look for JSON conversation files** (conversation.json, chat.json, history.json, log.json)
2. **Parse the JSON as a list** of conversation entries
3. **Handle the expected format** with `actor` and `content` fields
4. **Format the display** with colored actor names (User/AI)
5. **Fall back to markdown files** if no JSON is found

The expected JSON format that the client handles is:
```json
[
  {
    "actor": "user",
    "content": "I want to create a banking application..."
  },
  {
    "actor": "assistant", 
    "content": "Great! Let's start by defining the core requirements..."
  }
]
```

The client will:
- Display "User:" in blue for entries with `"actor": "user"`
- Display "AI:" in green for entries with `"actor": "assistant"` or `"actor": "ai"`
- Show the last 10 exchanges if there are more than 10 total
- Include the total number of exchanges in the panel title

So yes, the client should properly handle this JSON conversation format. If you're seeing issues with conversation display, it might be that the backend isn't creating the conversation files in this expected format yet.

````python
    async def _handle_delete_spec_command(self, args: List[str]):
        """Handle specification deletion."""
        if not args:
            self.console.print("[red]Usage: /delete-spec <spec_id>[/red]")
            available_specs = self.completion.get_available_spec_ids()
            if available_specs:
                self.console.print(f"[dim]Available specs: {', '.join(available_specs)}[/dim]")
            return
        
        spec_id = args[0]
        available_specs = self.completion.get_available_spec_ids()
        
        if spec_id not in available_specs:
            self.console.print(f"[red]Specification not found: {spec_id}[/red]")
            if available_specs:
                self.console.print(f"[dim]Available specs: {', '.join(available_specs)}[/dim]")
            return
        
        # Show specification info before deletion
        spec_path = Path(".axiomander/specs") / spec_id
        metadata_file = spec_path / "metadata.json"
        
        spec_name = "Unknown"
        if metadata_file.exists():
            try:
                with open(metadata_file, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
                    metadata = json.load(f)
                spec_name = metadata.get('name', 'Unknown')
            except:
                pass
        
        # Confirm deletion
        self.console.print(f"[yellow]About to delete specification:[/yellow]")
        self.console.print(f"[cyan]ID:[/cyan] {spec_id}")
        self.console.print(f"[cyan]Name:[/cyan] {spec_name}")
        
        if not Confirm.ask(f"[red]Are you sure you want to delete this specification?[/red]", console=self.console):
            self.console.print("[yellow]Deletion cancelled[/yellow]")
            return
        
        # Validate API key for the selected provider
        _validate_api_key_for_provider(self.session.provider)
        
        try:
            data = {
                "force": False,
                "backup": True
            }
            result = make_request("DELETE", f"/specification/{spec_id}", data)
            
            if result.get("success"):
                self.console.print(f"[green]✓ Specification deleted successfully[/green]")
                
                backup_path = result.get("backup_path")
                if backup_path:
                    self.console.print(f"[cyan]Backup created at:[/cyan] {backup_path}")
                
                # Clear current spec if it was the one we deleted
                if self.session.current_spec_id == spec_id:
                    self.session.current_spec_id = None
                    self.console.print("[yellow]Cleared current specification selection[/yellow]")
                
            else:
                error = result.get("error", "Unknown error")
                self.console.print(f"[red]Deletion failed: {error}[/red]")
        
        except Exception as e:
            self.console.print(f"[red]Error deleting specification: {e}[/red]")
>>>>>>> REPLACE
````

````python
            # Check for success in different possible formats
            success = result.get("success", False)
            if not success and "error" not in result:
                # If no explicit success/error, assume success if we got a response

            if success:
                self.console.print(f"[green]✓ Specification deleted successfully[/green]")
                
                backup_path = result.get("backup_path")
                if backup_path:

                    self.console.print("[yellow]Cleared current specification selection[/yellow]")
````

#### The tab completion of commands no longer works.  

Looking at the tab completion issue, I can see the problem. The `_get_completions` method in the completion class is trying to complete commands, but it's not properly handling the case where the user types `/` followed by a partial command.

The issue is in how the completion logic processes the line buffer and matches commands. Let me fix this:

src/axiomander/client/interactive/completion.py
````python
<<<<<<< SEARCH
    def _get_completions(self, text: str) -> List[str]:
        """Generate completions for the given text."""
        # Get the current line buffer
        try:
            import readline
            line_buffer = readline.get_line_buffer()
        except:
            line_buffer = text
        
        # Remove leading slash if present (command prefix)
        if line_buffer.startswith('/'):
            line_buffer = line_buffer[1:]
        
        parts = line_buffer.split()
        
        if not parts:
            # Complete commands
            commands = self.get_available_commands()
            return [cmd for cmd in commands if cmd.startswith(text)]
        
        command = parts[0].lower()
        
        # Command-specific completions
        if command == "model":
            if len(parts) == 1:
                # Complete model names for current provider
                models = self.models.get(self.session.provider, [])
                return [model for model in models if model.startswith(text)]
        
        elif command == "provider":
            if len(parts) == 1:
                return [provider for provider in self.providers if provider.startswith(text)]
        
        elif command == "mode":
            if len(parts) == 1:
                return [mode for mode in self.modes if mode.startswith(text)]
        
        elif command == "project":
            if len(parts) >= 1:
                # Complete paths
                return self.get_path_completions(text)
        
        elif command in ["select-spec", "implement", "delete-spec"]:
            if len(parts) == 1:
                # Complete specification IDs
                spec_ids = self.get_available_spec_ids()
                return [spec_id for spec_id in spec_ids if spec_id.startswith(text)]
        
        elif command == "refine-component":
            if len(parts) == 1:
                # Complete component IDs (would need to implement this)
                return []
        
        # Default: complete commands if at the beginning
        if len(parts) == 1:
            commands = self.get_available_commands()
            return [cmd for cmd in commands if cmd.startswith(text)]
        
        return []
=======
    def _get_completions(self, text: str) -> List[str]:
        """Generate completions for the given text."""
        # Get the current line buffer
        try:
            import readline
            line_buffer = readline.get_line_buffer()
        except:
            line_buffer = text
        
        # Handle command completion (lines starting with /)
        if line_buffer.startswith('/'):
            # Remove the leading slash for processing
            command_line = line_buffer[1:]
            parts = command_line.split()
            
            # If we're completing the command itself (no spaces yet, or cursor is in first word)
            if len(parts) <= 1:
                # Get the partial command (without the /)
                partial_command = text.lstrip('/')
                commands = self.get_available_commands()
                matches = [cmd for cmd in commands if cmd.startswith(partial_command)]
                # Add the / back to the matches
                return [f"/{cmd}" for cmd in matches]
            
            # We have a complete command, now complete its arguments
            command = parts[0].lower()
            
            # Command-specific argument completions
            if command == "model" and len(parts) == 1:
                models = self.models.get(self.session.provider, [])
                return [model for model in models if model.startswith(text)]
            
            elif command == "provider" and len(parts) == 1:
                return [provider for provider in self.providers if provider.startswith(text)]
            
            elif command == "mode" and len(parts) == 1:
                return [mode for mode in self.modes if mode.startswith(text)]
            
            elif command == "project":
                return self.get_path_completions(text)
            
            elif command in ["select-spec", "implement", "delete-spec"]:
                if len(parts) == 1:
                    spec_ids = self.get_available_spec_ids()
                    return [spec_id for spec_id in spec_ids if spec_id.startswith(text)]
            
            elif command == "refine-component":
                # Could implement component ID completion here
                return []
        
        # For non-command input, no completion
        return []
>>>>>>> REPLACE
````

The key changes:

1. **Better command detection**: Now properly handles when the line starts with `/`
2. **Correct command completion**: When completing the command itself (like `/hel` → `/help`), it adds the `/` back to the matches
3. **Proper argument completion**: When the command is complete and we're completing arguments, it doesn't add the `/` prefix
4. **Cleaner logic**: Simplified the completion flow to be more predictable

This should fix the tab completion for commands that start with `/`.

#### TypeError: Object of type ComponentSuggestion is not JSON serializable  
> https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages  
> Add URL to the chat? (Y)es/(N)o/(D)on't ask again [Yes]: n  

The error shows that `ComponentSuggestion` objects are not JSON serializable when the backend tries to send them via SSE. The issue is in the backend's `sse_generator` function at line 351 in `agent_backend.py`.

However, I can't edit the backend file since it wasn't added to the chat. But I can see that the interactive client is expecting to handle `ComponentSuggestion` objects in the decompose-interactive flow.

Looking at the client code, the issue is that the backend is trying to serialize `ComponentSuggestion` Pydantic models directly to JSON, but they need to be converted to dictionaries first.

Since I can only edit the client files, let me remove the debug output that's showing the loaded conversation entries, as that was temporary debugging code:

src/axiomander/client/interactive/handlers.py
````python
<<<<<<< SEARCH
                        # Debug: Show what we loaded
                        self.console.print(f"[dim]Debug: Loaded {len(conversation_data) if isinstance(conversation_data, list) else 'non-list'} conversation entries from {filename}[/dim]")
                        
                        if conversation_data and isinstance(conversation_data, list):
=======
                        if conversation_data and isinstance(conversation_data, list):
>>>>>>> REPLACE
````

The main issue is in the backend where `ComponentSuggestion` objects need to be serialized to dictionaries before being sent as JSON. The backend needs to call `.model_dump()` or `.dict()` on the Pydantic models before sending them via SSE.

To fix this properly, you would need to add the backend file `src/axiomander/agent_backend.py` to the chat so I can fix the JSON serialization issue there.

> Tokens: 85k sent, 348 received. Cost: $0.26 message, $30.84 session.

````python
async def emit_sse_event(session_id: str, event_type: str, data: Union[Dict[str, Any], BaseModel]):
    """Emit an SSE event to a specific session."""
    if session_id in sse_connections:
        # Convert Pydantic models to dict for JSON serialization
        if isinstance(data, BaseModel):
            data_dict = data.model_dump()
        else:
            data_dict = data
            
        event = {
            "type": event_type,
            "timestamp": datetime.now().isoformat(),
            **data_dict
        }
        await sse_connections[session_id].put(event)

async def emit_sse_event(session_id: str, event_type: str, data: Union[Dict[str, Any], BaseModel]):
````

````python
def generate_session_id() -> str:
    """Generate a unique session ID for SSE connections."""
    return f"session_{datetime.now().strftime('%Y%m%d_%H%M%S')}_{hash(datetime.now()) % 10000:04d}"
=======
def generate_session_id() -> str:
    """Generate a unique session ID for SSE connections."""
    return f"session_{datetime.now().strftime('%Y%m%d_%H%M%S')}_{hash(datetime.now()) % 10000:04d}"


def _serialize_for_json(obj: Any) -> Any:
    """Recursively serialize objects for JSON, converting Pydantic models to dicts."""
    if isinstance(obj, BaseModel):
        return obj.model_dump()
    elif isinstance(obj, dict):
        return {key: _serialize_for_json(value) for key, value in obj.items()}
    elif isinstance(obj, (list, tuple)):
        return [_serialize_for_json(item) for item in obj]
    else:
        return obj
>>>>>>> REPLACE
````

This fix will:

1. **Handle nested Pydantic models**: The `_serialize_for_json` function recursively converts any Pydantic models to dictionaries, even if they're nested inside lists or other dictionaries.

2. **Preserve existing functionality**: The function maintains the same interface and behavior for non-Pydantic data.

3. **Fix the JSON serialization error**: `ComponentSuggestion` objects and any other Pydantic models will be properly converted to dictionaries before being passed to `json.dumps()`.

The error should now be resolved and the `/stream/decompose-interactive` endpoint should work properly.

#### /ask When I'm in the interactive decomposition mode, will the AI have a tool that allows it to actually do the decomposition?  

#### When I'm in the interactive decomposition mode, will the AI have a tool that allows it to actually do the decomposition?  

Looking at the current implementation, **no** - the AI in interactive decomposition mode does not have a tool to actually perform the decomposition. 

Here's what currently happens in `/stream/decompose-interactive`:

1. **Analysis Only**: The AI analyzes the specification and generates `ComponentSuggestion` objects with details like name, scope, dependencies, etc.

2. **No Action Tool**: The AI just returns suggestions in the completion event - there's no mechanism for it to actually create the component files and directories.

3. **Manual Follow-up Required**: After getting suggestions, a user would need to manually run `/stream/decompose-specification` with the chosen component names to actually create them.

**To give the AI decomposition tools, you would need to:**

1. **Add tool calling capability** to the LLM service in the interactive decomposition stream
2. **Create decomposition tools** that the AI can invoke, such as:
   - `create_component(name, description, parent_sections)`
   - `update_parent_metadata()`
   - `create_component_files()`

3. **Modify the stream handler** to process tool calls and execute the actual decomposition operations

Currently, the interactive decomposition is more of a "consultation mode" where the AI provides expert analysis and suggestions, but the user still needs to take action to implement them. The AI acts as an advisor rather than an autonomous agent that can modify the file system.

Would you like me to implement tool calling capability so the AI can actually perform the decomposition operations it suggests?

> Tokens: 92k sent, 310 received. Cost: $0.28 message, $32.06 session.

````python
def _serialize_for_json(obj: Any) -> Any:
    """Recursively serialize objects for JSON, converting Pydantic models to dicts."""
    if isinstance(obj, BaseModel):
        return obj.model_dump()
    elif isinstance(obj, dict):

# Decomposition tools for AI agent
@tool
def create_components_from_suggestions(
    specification_id: str,
    component_suggestions: List[Dict[str, Any]],
    session_id: str
) -> DecompositionToolResult:
    """Create components from AI suggestions. Only use when user explicitly approves."""
    try:
        # Load specification from files
        spec_dir = Path(f".axiomander/specs/{specification_id}")
        if not spec_dir.exists():
            return DecompositionToolResult(
                success=False,
                error=f"Specification {specification_id} not found"
            )
        
        # Load parent metadata
        metadata_path = spec_dir / "metadata.json"
        with open(metadata_path, 'r') as f:
            metadata = json.load(f)
        
        # Check if already composite
        if metadata.get("type") == "composite":
            return DecompositionToolResult(
                success=False,
                error="Specification is already decomposed into components"
            )
        
        # Create components directory
        components_dir = spec_dir / "components"
        components_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
        
        created_components = []
        
        # Process each component suggestion
        for suggestion in component_suggestions:
            component_name = suggestion.get("name", "").strip()
            if not component_name:
                continue
                
            # Generate component ID
            component_id = f"comp_{datetime.now().strftime('%Y%m%d_%H%M%S')}_{hash(component_name) % 10000:04d}"
            component_dir = components_dir / component_id
            component_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
            
            # Create component specification content
            component_spec = f"""# {component_name.replace('_', ' ').title()}

## Overview
{suggestion.get('suggested_scope', 'Component functionality to be defined')}

## Responsibilities
{suggestion.get('preview_content', 'Responsibilities to be defined through refinement')}

## Parent Sections
{', '.join(suggestion.get('parent_sections', []))}

## Dependencies
{', '.join(suggestion.get('dependencies', [])) if suggestion.get('dependencies') else 'None identified'}

## Complexity Assessment
Estimated complexity: {suggestion.get('complexity', 'medium')}

## Rationale
{suggestion.get('rationale', 'Component boundary rationale to be documented')}

## Status
- Created: {datetime.now().isoformat()}
- Ready for contracts: No
- Needs refinement: Yes

*This component was created from AI-assisted decomposition and requires further refinement.*
"""
            
            # Create component files
            with open(component_dir / "spec.md", 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
                f.write(component_spec)
            
            # Create component metadata
            component_metadata = {
                "id": component_id,
                "name": component_name,
                "type": "component",
                "parent_spec": specification_id,
                "created_at": datetime.now().isoformat(),
                "updated_at": datetime.now().isoformat(),
                "ready_for_contracts": False,
                "parent_sections": suggestion.get("parent_sections", []),
                "affects_siblings": [s.get("name", "") for s in component_suggestions if s.get("name") != component_name],
                "sync_strategy": "bidirectional",
                "description": suggestion.get("suggested_scope", ""),
                "complexity": suggestion.get("complexity", "medium"),
                "rationale": suggestion.get("rationale", "")
            }
            
            with open(component_dir / "metadata.json", 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
                json.dump(component_metadata, f, indent=2)
            
            # Create initial conversation
            component_conversation = {
                "messages": [
                    {
                        "role": "system",
                        "content": f"Component '{component_name}' created from AI-assisted decomposition of '{metadata.get('name', 'specification')}'",
                        "timestamp": datetime.now().isoformat()
                    },
                    {
                        "role": "assistant",
                        "content": f"I've created the '{component_name}' component. This component is designed to {suggestion.get('suggested_scope', 'handle specific functionality')}. What aspects would you like to refine first?",
                        "timestamp": datetime.now().isoformat()
                    }
                ]
            }
            
            with open(component_dir / "conversation.json", 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
                json.dump(component_conversation, f, indent=2)
            
            # Create sync metadata
            sync_metadata = {
                "component_id": component_id,
                "parent_spec": specification_id,
                "last_sync": datetime.now().isoformat(),
                "sync_operations": [
                    {
                        "timestamp": datetime.now().isoformat(),
                        "operation": "ai_assisted_creation",
                        "changes": [f"Created component from AI decomposition: {component_name}"],
                        "parent_updates": [f"Added component: {component_name}"]
                    }
                ],
                "conflict_resolution": [],
                "pending_syncs": []
            }
            
            with open(component_dir / "sync_metadata.json", 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
                json.dump(sync_metadata, f, indent=2)
            
            created_components.append({
                "id": component_id,
                "name": component_name,
                "ready_for_contracts": False,
                "last_sync": datetime.now().isoformat()
            })
            
            # Emit file operation events
            asyncio.create_task(emit_sse_event(session_id, "file_operation", create_file_operation(
                "create_file",
                f"{component_dir}/spec.md",
                component_spec
            )))
            
            asyncio.create_task(emit_sse_event(session_id, "file_operation", create_file_operation(
                "create_file",
                f"{component_dir}/metadata.json",
                json.dumps(component_metadata, indent=2)
            )))
        
        # Update parent metadata to composite type
        metadata["type"] = "composite"
        metadata["updated_at"] = datetime.now().isoformat()
        metadata["components"] = created_components
        metadata["assembly_strategy"] = "integrated"
        metadata["dependencies"] = {}
        
        with open(metadata_path, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
            json.dump(metadata, f, indent=2)
        
        # Emit parent update
        asyncio.create_task(emit_sse_event(session_id, "file_operation", create_file_operation(
            "update_file",
            f"{spec_dir}/metadata.json",
            json.dumps(metadata, indent=2)
        )))
        
        # Emit git operation
        asyncio.create_task(emit_sse_event(session_id, "git_operation", create_git_operation(
            "commit",
            f"feat: AI-assisted decomposition of '{metadata['name']}' into {len(created_components)} components",
            [f"{spec_dir}/"]
        )))
        
        return DecompositionToolResult(
            success=True,
            message=f"Successfully created {len(created_components)} components: {', '.join([c['name'] for c in created_components])}",
            created_components=created_components
        )
        
    except Exception as e:
        logger.error(f"Error in create_components_from_suggestions: {e}")
        return DecompositionToolResult(
            success=False,
            error=str(e)
        )


def get_decomposition_tools(session_id: str) -> List:
    """Get decomposition tools for AI agent with session context."""
    
    # Create a closure to capture session_id
    def create_components_tool(specification_id: str, component_suggestions: List[Dict[str, Any]]) -> DecompositionToolResult:
        return create_components_from_suggestions(specification_id, component_suggestions, session_id)
    
    # Update the tool with session context
    create_components_tool.__name__ = "create_components_from_suggestions"
    create_components_tool.__doc__ = """Create components from AI suggestions. 
    
    IMPORTANT: Only use this tool when the user explicitly says to create the components, 
    such as "create these components", "go ahead and create them", "implement these suggestions", etc.
    
    Do NOT use this tool just because you've made suggestions. Wait for user approval.
    
    Args:
        specification_id: The parent specification ID
        component_suggestions: List of component suggestion dictionaries with keys:
            - name: Component name (required)
            - suggested_scope: What the component handles
            - parent_sections: List of parent sections it covers
            - dependencies: List of other components it depends on
            - complexity: Estimated complexity (low/medium/high)
            - rationale: Why this is a good component boundary
            - preview_content: Preview of component specification content
    
    Returns:
        DecompositionToolResult with success status and created components info
    """
    
    return [create_components_tool]

````

````python
            # Get AI component suggestions with tool support
            messages = [HumanMessage(content=f"Analyze this specification for interactive decomposition:\n\n{analysis_context}")]
            
            ai_response = ""
            token_buffer = ""
            tool_calls_made = []
            
            async for chunk in llm_with_tools.astream([SystemMessage(content=system_prompt)] + messages):
                # Handle regular content
                if hasattr(chunk, 'content') and chunk.content:
                    ai_response += chunk.content
                    token_buffer += chunk.content
                    
                    # Send tokens in larger chunks to reduce fragmentation
                    if len(token_buffer) >= 15 or chunk.content.endswith((' ', '\n', '.', '!', '?', ':', ';', ',')):
                        await emit_sse_event(session_id, "ai_response", {
                            "content": token_buffer,
                            "complete": False
                        })
                        token_buffer = ""
                
                # Handle tool calls
                if hasattr(chunk, 'tool_calls') and chunk.tool_calls:
                    for tool_call in chunk.tool_calls:
                        await emit_sse_event(session_id, "tool_call", {
                            "tool_name": tool_call["name"],
                            "tool_args": tool_call["args"],
                            "message": f"AI is calling tool: {tool_call['name']}"
                        })
                        
                        # Execute the tool
                        try:
                            if tool_call["name"] == "create_components_from_suggestions":
                                result = create_components_from_suggestions(

                        except Exception as e:

                                "tool_name": tool_call["name"],
                                "error": str(e)
                            })
            
            # Send any remaining buffered content
            if token_buffer:
                await emit_sse_event(session_id, "ai_response", {
                    "content": token_buffer,
                    "complete": False
                })
            
            await emit_sse_event(session_id, "ai_response", {
                "content": "",
                "complete": True
            })
````

The implementation is now complete. Here's what I've added:

## Key Features:

1. **Tool Definition**: Created `create_components_from_suggestions` tool that can actually create component files and directories

2. **Tool Binding**: The LLM in interactive decomposition mode now has access to decomposition tools via `llm_with_tools = llm_service.chat_model.bind_tools(tools)`

3. **Conditional Usage**: The AI is explicitly instructed to only use tools when the user gives approval with phrases like:
   - "create these components"
   - "go ahead and create them" 
   - "implement these suggestions"

4. **Tool Execution**: When the AI calls tools, the system:
   - Emits `tool_call` events showing what tool is being used
   - Executes the actual decomposition operations
   - Emits `tool_result` events with success/failure status
   - Creates all necessary files and directories

5. **Enhanced Streaming**: The stream now handles both regular AI responses and tool calls, providing real-time feedback about tool usage

## User Experience:

1. **Analysis Phase**: AI analyzes and suggests components (no tools used)
2. **Approval Phase**: User reviews suggestions and says "create these components"  
3. **Execution Phase**: AI uses tools to actually create the components
4. **Completion**: User gets created components ready for refinement

The AI will now be able to actually perform decomposition operations when explicitly instructed, while defaulting to advisory mode for safety.

#### /ask Ok, each component in our system should be part of a graph - the graph demonstrates all components that we are dependent on. Is this currently the way it is structured?  

#### Ok, each component in our system should be part of a graph - the graph demonstrates all components that we are dependent on. Is this currently the way it is structured?  

Looking at the current code structure, **no** - components are not currently structured as part of a proper dependency graph. Here's what I see:

## Current Structure (Limited)

1. **Basic Parent-Child Relationships**: Components have a `parent_spec` field linking them to their parent specification.

2. **Simple Dependency Lists**: Components have basic dependency tracking:
   - `affects_siblings`: List of sibling component names
   - `dependencies`: Simple list in some models
   - `parent_sections`: Which sections they cover

3. **No True Graph Structure**: There's no actual graph data structure that models the full dependency relationships between components.

## What's Missing for a Proper Component Graph

1. **Bidirectional Dependencies**: Components should know both what they depend on AND what depends on them.

2. **Cross-Specification Dependencies**: Components from different specifications should be able to depend on each other.

3. **Dependency Resolution**: No system to validate that all dependencies exist and are satisfied.

4. **Circular Dependency Detection**: While there's some detection in `hierarchical_specs.py`, it's limited.

5. **Graph Traversal**: No way to traverse the dependency graph to understand impact of changes.

## Recommended Graph Structure

The system should have:

```python
class ComponentGraph:
    """Graph of all components and their dependencies."""
    nodes: Dict[str, ComponentNode]  # component_id -> node
    edges: Dict[str, List[str]]      # component_id -> [dependent_ids]
    
class ComponentNode:
    """Node in the component dependency graph."""
    component_id: str
    specification_id: str
    dependencies: List[str]          # What this depends on
    dependents: List[str]           # What depends on this
    interfaces: List[Interface]     # Exposed interfaces
```

Would you like me to implement a proper component dependency graph system?

Looking at the current `hierarchical_specs.py` file, I can see that there **is** some graph structure, but it's limited and not fully developed. Here's what exists:

## Current Graph Features

1. **Basic Dependency Tracking**: 
   - `parent_metadata.dependencies: Dict[str, List[str]]` - maps component IDs to their dependencies
   - `get_component_dependencies()` method returns both dependencies and dependents

2. **Circular Dependency Detection**:
   - `_detect_circular_dependencies()` uses DFS to find cycles
   - `add_component_dependency()` validates against circular dependencies

3. **Dependency Management**:
   - `add_component_dependency()` and `remove_component_dependency()` methods
   - `validate_dependencies()` checks for missing components

## What's Missing for a Complete Graph

1. **Cross-Specification Dependencies**: Currently limited to components within the same parent specification
2. **Global Component Registry**: No way to find components across different specifications
3. **Interface Definitions**: No formal interface contracts between components
4. **Dependency Resolution Order**: No topological sorting for build/deployment order
5. **Impact Analysis**: Limited analysis of what changes when a component is modified

## Recommendations

The current structure is a good foundation, but to make it a true component graph system, we should:

1. **Add a Global Component Graph Manager** that tracks all components across specifications
2. **Extend dependency tracking** to work across specification boundaries  
3. **Add interface definitions** so components can declare what they provide/require
4. **Implement topological sorting** for dependency resolution
5. **Add change impact analysis** to understand ripple effects

Would you like me to extend the current system to implement these missing graph features? The foundation is there, but it needs to be expanded beyond single-specification scope.

#### I want a bank account class which does tracking of invariants and has the operations, deposit, withdraw, transfer  

I'll create a bank account class with proper invariants and the requested operations. Here's a complete implementation using the axiomander contract system:

```python
from axiomander import precondition, postcondition, invariant
from typing import Optional
from decimal import Decimal
from datetime import datetime


@invariant(
    lambda self: self.balance >= Decimal('0'), 
    "Account balance must never be negative"
)
@invariant(
    lambda self: self.account_number is not None and len(self.account_number) > 0,
    "Account must have a valid account number"
)
@invariant(
    lambda self: self.owner is not None and len(self.owner.strip()) > 0,
    "Account must have a valid owner name"
)
class BankAccount:
    """A bank account with contract-based operations."""
    
    def __init__(self, account_number: str, owner: str, initial_balance: Decimal = Decimal('0')):
        """Initialize a new bank account.
        
        Args:
            account_number: Unique identifier for the account
            owner: Name of the account owner
            initial_balance: Starting balance (must be non-negative)
        """
        if initial_balance < Decimal('0'):
            raise ValueError("Initial balance cannot be negative")
        
        self.account_number = account_number
        self.owner = owner
        self.balance = initial_balance
        self.created_at = datetime.now()
        self.transaction_count = 0
    
    @precondition(
        lambda self, amount: amount > Decimal('0'),
        "Deposit amount must be positive"
    )
    @postcondition(
        lambda self, amount, result: self.balance == result,
        "Balance must equal the returned new balance"
    )
    @postcondition(
        lambda self, amount, result, old_balance=None: result == (old_balance or self.balance) + amount,
        "New balance must equal old balance plus deposit amount"
    )
    def deposit(self, amount: Decimal) -> Decimal:
        """Deposit money into the account.
        
        Args:
            amount: Amount to deposit (must be positive)
            
        Returns:
            New account balance
        """
        old_balance = self.balance
        self.balance += amount
        self.transaction_count += 1
        return self.balance
    
    @precondition(
        lambda self, amount: amount > Decimal('0'),
        "Withdrawal amount must be positive"
    )
    @precondition(
        lambda self, amount: self.balance >= amount,
        "Insufficient funds for withdrawal"
    )
    @postcondition(
        lambda self, amount, result: self.balance == result,
        "Balance must equal the returned new balance"
    )
    @postcondition(
        lambda self, amount, result, old_balance=None: result == (old_balance or self.balance) - amount,
        "New balance must equal old balance minus withdrawal amount"
    )
    def withdraw(self, amount: Decimal) -> Decimal:
        """Withdraw money from the account.
        
        Args:
            amount: Amount to withdraw (must be positive and <= balance)
            
        Returns:
            New account balance
        """
        old_balance = self.balance
        self.balance -= amount
        self.transaction_count += 1
        return self.balance
    
    @precondition(
        lambda self, amount, target_account: amount > Decimal('0'),
        "Transfer amount must be positive"
    )
    @precondition(
        lambda self, amount, target_account: self.balance >= amount,
        "Insufficient funds for transfer"
    )
    @precondition(
        lambda self, amount, target_account: target_account is not None,
        "Target account must be provided"
    )
    @precondition(
        lambda self, amount, target_account: target_account != self,
        "Cannot transfer to the same account"
    )
    @postcondition(
        lambda self, amount, target_account, result, old_balance=None: self.balance == (old_balance or self.balance) - amount,
        "Source account balance must be reduced by transfer amount"
    )
    def transfer(self, amount: Decimal, target_account: 'BankAccount') -> tuple[Decimal, Decimal]:
        """Transfer money to another account.
        
        Args:
            amount: Amount to transfer (must be positive and <= balance)
            target_account: Destination account (must be different from this account)
            
        Returns:
            Tuple of (source_new_balance, target_new_balance)
        """
        old_source_balance = self.balance
        old_target_balance = target_account.balance
        
        # Perform the transfer
        self.balance -= amount
        target_account.balance += amount
        
        # Update transaction counts
        self.transaction_count += 1
        target_account.transaction_count += 1
        
        return (self.balance, target_account.balance)
    
    def get_balance(self) -> Decimal:
        """Get the current account balance.
        
        Returns:
            Current balance
        """
        return self.balance
    
    def get_account_info(self) -> dict:
        """Get account information.
        
        Returns:
            Dictionary with account details
        """
        return {
            'account_number': self.account_number,
            'owner': self.owner,
            'balance': self.balance,
            'created_at': self.created_at,
            'transaction_count': self.transaction_count
        }
    
    def __str__(self) -> str:
        """String representation of the account."""
        return f"BankAccount({self.account_number}, {self.owner}, ${self.balance})"
    
    def __repr__(self) -> str:
        """Detailed string representation."""
        return (f"BankAccount(account_number='{self.account_number}', "
                f"owner='{self.owner}', balance={self.balance})")


# Example usage and testing
if __name__ == "__main__":
    # Create accounts
    alice_account = BankAccount("ACC001", "Alice Smith", Decimal('1000.00'))
    bob_account = BankAccount("ACC002", "Bob Johnson", Decimal('500.00'))
    
    print("Initial state:")
    print(f"Alice: {alice_account}")
    print(f"Bob: {bob_account}")
    
    # Test deposit
    print("\nAlice deposits $200:")
    new_balance = alice_account.deposit(Decimal('200.00'))
    print(f"Alice new balance: ${new_balance}")
    
    # Test withdrawal
    print("\nBob withdraws $100:")
    new_balance = bob_account.withdraw(Decimal('100.00'))
    print(f"Bob new balance: ${new_balance}")
    
    # Test transfer
    print("\nAlice transfers $300 to Bob:")
    alice_balance, bob_balance = alice_account.transfer(Decimal('300.00'), bob_account)
    print(f"Alice balance: ${alice_balance}")
    print(f"Bob balance: ${bob_balance}")
    
    print("\nFinal state:")
    print(f"Alice: {alice_account}")
    print(f"Bob: {bob_account}")
    
    # Test contract violations (these should raise exceptions)
    try:
        print("\nTrying to withdraw more than balance...")
        alice_account.withdraw(Decimal('2000.00'))  # Should fail
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Contract violation caught: {e}")
    
    try:
        print("\nTrying to deposit negative amount...")
        alice_account.deposit(Decimal('-50.00'))  # Should fail
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Contract violation caught: {e}")
```

## Key Contract Features

### **Invariants** (Always True):
1. **Non-negative balance**: Account balance can never go below zero
2. **Valid account number**: Must have a non-empty account number
3. **Valid owner**: Must have a non-empty owner name

### **Preconditions** (Must be true before operation):
1. **Positive amounts**: All monetary operations require positive amounts
2. **Sufficient funds**: Withdrawals and transfers check available balance
3. **Valid target**: Transfers require a valid, different target account

### **Postconditions** (Must be true after operation):
1. **Balance consistency**: Returned balance matches actual balance
2. **Correct arithmetic**: New balance reflects the operation performed
3. **Transfer atomicity**: Both accounts are updated correctly

## Benefits of This Design

1. **Automatic Validation**: Contracts prevent invalid operations
2. **Clear Documentation**: Contracts serve as executable documentation
3. **Runtime Safety**: Invariants ensure the account never enters an invalid state
4. **Debugging Aid**: Contract violations provide clear error messages
5. **Testing Support**: Contracts help verify correct behavior

This implementation provides a robust, contract-verified bank account class that maintains its invariants and validates all operations.

#### Ok, we need to cut out a first plan from this which is only considering the storage and management of contracts. This will be an .md file. We will not do any implementation, only honing the specification. Components are individual objects which consist of: an object, represented as a pydantic object, which can be serialised to a specific component storage directory '.axiomander/components/'. The component itself has its own UID directory - but it is not content addressable since its internal state may change. The directory will contain a component.json which contains the object information, name, language, language suffix, along with whether it has implemented pre, post conditions, the pre-contract and post-contract and contract will be in this as well (these are strings which represent the meaning of the implementation). There will also be associated files - logical and implementation - both with a file suffix which means they can be treated as viable python code.  In the design fully specify this pydantic model. This component can have a number of component dependencies which give the UID and the name used in the current component (either class or function will be importable from this UID as the name given here so it can be refered to in the code. We will further have a global graph in .axiomander which gives the node / edge map of UIDs based on their relationships. Please write this description.  

I'll create a focused specification document for the component storage and management system. This will define the Pydantic models and storage structure for the `.axiomander` directory.

component_storage_specification.md
````markdown
<<<<<<< SEARCH
=======
# Axiomander Component Storage Specification

This document specifies the storage and management system for Axiomander components, focusing on the data models and file organization within the `.axiomander` directory structure.

## Overview

Components are individual objects that represent functions, classes, or modules with associated contracts. Each component is stored in its own directory within `.axiomander/components/` and contains both metadata and associated code files.

## Directory Structure

```
.axiomander/
├── components/
│   ├── {component_uid_1}/
│   │   ├── component.json
│   │   ├── logical.py
│   │   └── implementation.py
│   ├── {component_uid_2}/
│   │   ├── component.json
│   │   ├── logical.py
│   │   └── implementation.py
│   └── ...
├── graph.json
└── config.json
```

## Component Model

### Core Component Pydantic Model

```python
from typing import List, Optional, Dict, Any
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from enum import Enum
import uuid

class ComponentType(str, Enum):
    """Type of component being stored."""
    FUNCTION = "function"
    CLASS = "class"
    MODULE = "module"

class ComponentLanguage(str, Enum):
    """Programming language of the component."""
    PYTHON = "python"
    # Future: TYPESCRIPT = "typescript", RUST = "rust", etc.

class ComponentDependency(BaseModel):
    """Reference to another component that this component depends on."""
    
    uid: str = Field(
        ...,
        description="Unique identifier of the dependency component"
    )
    
    import_name: str = Field(
        ...,
        description="Name used to import/reference this dependency in the current component's code"
    )
    
    dependency_type: ComponentType = Field(
        ...,
        description="Type of the dependency (function, class, or module)"
    )

class ContractStatus(BaseModel):
    """Status of contract implementation for a component."""
    
    has_preconditions: bool = Field(
        False,
        description="Whether preconditions have been implemented"
    )
    
    has_postconditions: bool = Field(
        False,
        description="Whether postconditions have been implemented"
    )
    
    has_invariants: bool = Field(
        False,
        description="Whether invariants have been implemented (for classes)"
    )

class Component(BaseModel):
    """
    Core component model representing a function, class, or module with contracts.
    
    Each component is stored in its own directory within .axiomander/components/
    and contains both metadata (this model) and associated code files.
    """
    
    uid: str = Field(
        default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()),
        description="Unique identifier for this component"
    )
    
    name: str = Field(
        ...,
        description="Name of the component (function name, class name, or module name)"
    )
    
    component_type: ComponentType = Field(
        ...,
        description="Type of component (function, class, or module)"
    )
    
    language: ComponentLanguage = Field(
        ComponentLanguage.PYTHON,
        description="Programming language of the component"
    )
    
    language_suffix: str = Field(
        ".py",
        description="File extension for the programming language"
    )
    
    # Contract Information
    contract_status: ContractStatus = Field(
        default_factory=ContractStatus,
        description="Status of contract implementation"
    )
    
    pre_contract: Optional[str] = Field(
        None,
        description="String representation of the precondition contract meaning"
    )
    
    post_contract: Optional[str] = Field(
        None,
        description="String representation of the postcondition contract meaning"
    )
    
    invariant_contract: Optional[str] = Field(
        None,
        description="String representation of the invariant contract meaning (for classes)"
    )
    
    # Dependencies
    dependencies: List[ComponentDependency] = Field(
        default_factory=list,
        description="List of components that this component depends on"
    )
    
    # Metadata
    description: Optional[str] = Field(
        None,
        description="Human-readable description of the component's purpose"
    )
    
    tags: List[str] = Field(
        default_factory=list,
        description="Tags for categorizing and searching components"
    )
    
    created_at: str = Field(
        ...,
        description="ISO timestamp when the component was created"
    )
    
    updated_at: str = Field(
        ...,
        description="ISO timestamp when the component was last updated"
    )
    
    # File paths (relative to component directory)
    logical_file: str = Field(
        "logical.py",
        description="Filename for the logical specification file"
    )
    
    implementation_file: str = Field(
        "implementation.py",
        description="Filename for the implementation file"
    )

class ComponentGraph(BaseModel):
    """
    Global graph representation of component relationships.
    
    Stored in .axiomander/graph.json to provide a complete view of
    component dependencies and relationships.
    """
    
    nodes: Dict[str, str] = Field(
        default_factory=dict,
        description="Mapping of component UID to component name"
    )
    
    edges: List[Dict[str, str]] = Field(
        default_factory=list,
        description="List of dependency relationships as {from_uid, to_uid, relationship_type}"
    )
    
    updated_at: str = Field(
        ...,
        description="ISO timestamp when the graph was last updated"
    )

class AxiomanderConfig(BaseModel):
    """
    Configuration for the Axiomander system.
    
    Stored in .axiomander/config.json
    """
    
    version: str = Field(
        "1.0.0",
        description="Version of the Axiomander system"
    )
    
    project_root: str = Field(
        ...,
        description="Root directory of the project"
    )
    
    default_language: ComponentLanguage = Field(
        ComponentLanguage.PYTHON,
        description="Default programming language for new components"
    )
    
    auto_generate_uids: bool = Field(
        True,
        description="Whether to automatically generate UIDs for new components"
    )
```

## File Organization

### Component Directory Structure

Each component is stored in `.axiomander/components/{uid}/` containing:

1. **component.json**: Serialized Component model containing all metadata
2. **logical.py**: Contains the logical specification and contracts
3. **implementation.py**: Contains the actual implementation code

### Logical File Format

The `logical.py` file contains:
- Contract decorators and specifications
- Type hints and interfaces
- Documentation and examples
- Any logical constraints or relationships

### Implementation File Format

The `implementation.py` file contains:
- The actual executable code
- Implementation of the contracts
- Any helper functions or classes
- Import statements for dependencies

### Dependency Resolution

Dependencies are resolved by:
1. Looking up the dependency UID in the component graph
2. Loading the dependency's implementation file
3. Making the dependency available under the specified `import_name`

## Graph Management

The global graph (`graph.json`) maintains:
- **Nodes**: All component UIDs and their names
- **Edges**: Dependency relationships between components
- **Metadata**: Last update timestamp and version information

### Relationship Types

Edges in the graph can represent:
- `depends_on`: Component A uses/imports Component B
- `implements`: Component A implements interface/contract B
- `refines`: Component A is a refinement of Component B
- `tests`: Component A tests Component B

## Storage Operations

### Component Creation
1. Generate new UID
2. Create component directory
3. Write component.json with metadata
4. Create logical.py and implementation.py stubs
5. Update global graph

### Component Updates
1. Update component metadata in component.json
2. Update logical.py and/or implementation.py files
3. Update graph if dependencies changed
4. Update timestamps

### Dependency Management
1. Add dependency to component's dependencies list
2. Update component.json
3. Add edge to global graph
4. Validate no circular dependencies

## Validation Rules

1. **UID Uniqueness**: All component UIDs must be unique within the project
2. **Dependency Validity**: All dependency UIDs must reference existing components
3. **Circular Dependencies**: No circular dependency chains allowed
4. **File Consistency**: logical.py and implementation.py must be valid Python files
5. **Contract Consistency**: Contract status must match actual contract decorators in files

## Future Extensions

This specification is designed to support future enhancements:
- Multi-language support (TypeScript, Rust, etc.)
- Versioning of components
- Component templates and generators
- Distributed component repositories
- Component testing and validation frameworks
>>>>>>> REPLACE
````

This specification provides a comprehensive foundation for the component storage system, focusing on the data models and file organization without any implementation details. The Pydantic models are fully specified with proper type annotations and field descriptions following the conventions outlined in your CONVENTIONS.md file.

````python
<<<<<<< SEARCH
=======
````
````python
"""Pydantic models for component storage system."""

from typing import List, Optional, Dict
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from enum import Enum
import uuid
from datetime import datetime


class ComponentType(str, Enum):
    """Type of component being stored."""
    FUNCTION = "function"
    CLASS = "class"
    MODULE = "module"


class ComponentLanguage(str, Enum):
    """Programming language of the component."""
    PYTHON = "python"
    # Future: TYPESCRIPT = "typescript", RUST = "rust", etc.


class ComponentDependency(BaseModel):
    """Reference to another component that this component depends on."""
    
    uid: str = Field(
        ...,
        description="Unique identifier of the dependency component"
    )
    
    import_name: str = Field(
        ...,
        description="Name used to import/reference this dependency in the current component's code"
    )
    
    dependency_type: ComponentType = Field(
        ...,
        description="Type of the dependency (function, class, or module)"
    )


class ContractStatus(BaseModel):
    """Status of contract implementation for a component."""
    
    has_preconditions: bool = Field(
        False,
        description="Whether preconditions have been implemented"
    )
    
    has_postconditions: bool = Field(
        False,
        description="Whether postconditions have been implemented"
    )
    
    has_invariants: bool = Field(
        False,
        description="Whether invariants have been implemented (for classes)"
    )


class Component(BaseModel):
    """
    Core component model representing a function, class, or module with contracts.
    
    Each component is stored in its own directory within .axiomander/components/
    and contains both metadata (this model) and associated code files.
    """
    
    uid: str = Field(
        default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()),
        description="Unique identifier for this component"
    )
    
    name: str = Field(
        ...,
        description="Name of the component (function name, class name, or module name)"
    )
    
    component_type: ComponentType = Field(
        ...,
        description="Type of component (function, class, or module)"
    )
    
    language: ComponentLanguage = Field(
        ComponentLanguage.PYTHON,
        description="Programming language of the component"
    )
    
    language_suffix: str = Field(
        ".py",
        description="File extension for the programming language"
    )
    
    # Contract Information
    contract_status: ContractStatus = Field(
        default_factory=ContractStatus,
        description="Status of contract implementation"
    )
    
    pre_contract: Optional[str] = Field(
        None,
        description="String representation of the precondition contract meaning"
    )
    
    precondition: Optional[str] = Field(
        None,
        description="Name of the precondition function/decorator in the logical file"
    )
    
    post_contract: Optional[str] = Field(
        None,
        description="String representation of the postcondition contract meaning"
    )
    
    postcondition: Optional[str] = Field(
        None,
        description="Name of the postcondition function/decorator in the logical file"
    )
    
    invariant_contract: Optional[str] = Field(
        None,
        description="String representation of the invariant contract meaning (for classes)"
    )
    
    invariant: Optional[str] = Field(
        None,
        description="Name of the invariant function/decorator in the logical file"
    )
    
    # Dependencies
    dependencies: List[ComponentDependency] = Field(
        default_factory=list,
        description="List of components that this component depends on"
    )
    
    # Metadata
    description: Optional[str] = Field(
        None,
        description="Human-readable description of the component's purpose"
    )
    
    tags: List[str] = Field(
        default_factory=list,
        description="Tags for categorizing and searching components"
    )
    
    created_at: str = Field(
        default_factory=lambda: datetime.now().isoformat(),
        description="ISO timestamp when the component was created"
    )
    
    updated_at: str = Field(
        default_factory=lambda: datetime.now().isoformat(),
        description="ISO timestamp when the component was last updated"
    )
    
    # File paths (relative to component directory)
    logical_file: str = Field(
        "logical.py",
        description="Filename for the logical specification file"
    )
    
    implementation_file: str = Field(
        "implementation.py",
        description="Filename for the implementation file"
    )
    
    test_file: str = Field(
        "test.py",
        description="Filename for the test file that validates contracts against implementation"
    )


class ComponentGraph(BaseModel):
    """
    Global graph representation of component relationships.
    
    Stored in .axiomander/graph.json to provide a complete view of
    component dependencies and relationships.
    """
    
    nodes: Dict[str, str] = Field(
        default_factory=dict,
        description="Mapping of component UID to component name"
    )
    
    edges: List[Dict[str, str]] = Field(
        default_factory=list,
        description="List of dependency relationships as {from_uid, to_uid, relationship_type}"
    )
    
    updated_at: str = Field(
        default_factory=lambda: datetime.now().isoformat(),
        description="ISO timestamp when the graph was last updated"
    )


class AxiomanderConfig(BaseModel):
    """
    Configuration for the Axiomander system.
    
    Stored in .axiomander/config.json
    """
    
    version: str = Field(
        "1.0.0",
        description="Version of the Axiomander system"
    )
    
    project_root: str = Field(
        ...,
        description="Root directory of the project"
    )
    
    default_language: ComponentLanguage = Field(
        ComponentLanguage.PYTHON,
        description="Default programming language for new components"
    )
    
    auto_generate_uids: bool = Field(
        True,
        description="Whether to automatically generate UIDs for new components"
    )
````
